bio_short_en: Abu Huraira (RA) was one of the most prolific narrators of hadith in Islamic history, transmitting over 5,000 narrations. A companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), he embraced Islam and devoted himself entirely to the Prophet's company, memorizing his sayings and deeds with extraordinary dedication. He accompanied the Prophet during the later years of his life and prioritized learning over worldly pursuits. His vast memory and tireless narration made him an indispensable source for the Sunnah, and scholars of hadith across generations relied heavily on his transmissions.
bio_en: Abu Huraira (RA), whose full name was Abd al-Rahman ibn Sakhr al-Dawsi, was born in Yemen and embraced Islam in 7 AH, arriving in Medina shortly before the Battle of Khaybar. His kunya Abu Huraira — Father of the Kitten — was given by the Prophet ﷺ himself after he was seen carrying a small cat. He is the single most prolific narrator of hadith in Islamic history, with over 5,374 narrations attributed to him across the major collections. He achieved this through total dedication: he chose to remain with the Prophet ﷺ rather than pursue trade or agriculture, accepting hunger as the price of knowledge. The Prophet ﷺ once prayed for him: 'O Allah, cause Your servant to be loved by the believers and cause the believers to be loved by him.' He served as governor of Bahrain under Caliph Umar (RA) and later settled permanently in Medina. After the Prophet's death, he taught in the mosque, transmitting hadith to thousands of students across many generations of Tabi'in, including Hammam ibn Munabbih whose Sahifah is one of the earliest written hadith collections. He passed away around 57–59 AH in Medina. Scholars of all generations regard his narrations as foundational to the entire corpus of hadith literature.
bio_short_id: Abu Hurairah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang paling banyak meriwayatkan hadith, dengan lebih dari 5.000 hadith. Ia masuk Islam pada tahun 7 H dan selalu menyertai Nabi, mengutamakan ilmu di atas kepentingan duniawi.

bio_short_en: Aisha (RA), daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq and wife of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), was one of the most knowledgeable companions and a major source of hadith. She narrated thousands of traditions, particularly on matters of the Prophet's private life, worship, and character. Scholars and companions regularly consulted her on religious rulings, and she was known for her sharp intellect and willingness to correct misunderstandings. Her narrations are foundational to Islamic jurisprudence, especially in areas of family law, purification, and personal conduct.
bio_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (RA) was the daughter of the first Caliph Abu Bakr al-Siddiq and the beloved wife of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. She is counted among the Mothers of the Believers and was distinguished by the Prophet ﷺ as the most beloved to him of all people. She was remarkable for her intellect, her prodigious memory, and her mastery of Arabic poetry and genealogy — skills she brought to bear in preserving and explaining prophetic traditions. She narrated approximately 2,210 hadiths, covering every aspect of the Prophet's private life, worship, character, and legal rulings. Senior companions, including Umar ibn al-Khattab and Abu Musa al-Ashari, regularly sought her guidance on religious questions, and she was not shy about correcting errors even among the greatest companions. She was particularly authoritative on matters of purification, prayer, household conduct, and the Prophet's personal worship at night. After the Prophet's death she lived in Medina for approximately forty-four years, teaching and narrating until her death around 57–58 AH. She is one of the four figures — along with Abu Huraira, Ibn Umar, and Ibn Abbas — who form the backbone of the entire Sunnah in the major collections.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih, serta sering dimintai fatwa oleh para Sahabat.

bio_short_en: Anas ibn Malik (RA) served the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) for ten years from the age of ten until the Prophet's death. A companion from Medina, he was one of the youngest major hadith narrators and lived to an advanced age, allowing him to transmit thousands of narrations to later generations. He is among the seven companions known as the Mukthirun — those who narrated the most hadith. His close personal service to the Prophet gave him unique insight into the Prophet's daily habits, worship, and manners.
bio_en: Anas ibn Malik al-Ansari (RA) was born in Medina around 10 years before the Hijra. When the Prophet ﷺ arrived in Medina, Anas's mother Umm Sulaym brought her ten-year-old son to serve him, and the Prophet ﷺ accepted. For the next ten years — until the Prophet's death — Anas served him closely, observing his daily habits, worship, character, and conduct with unparalleled intimacy. The Prophet ﷺ made a special supplication for him: 'O Allah, give him wealth and children and bless him therein.' This du'a was visibly answered: Anas lived to approximately 93 AH, outliving nearly all the companions, had numerous children, and narrated over 2,286 hadiths. He is one of the Mukthirun — the seven companions who narrated the most hadith. After the Prophet's death he participated in the conquest campaigns and then settled in Basra, where he became its last surviving major companion and taught thousands of Tabi'i scholars, including al-Hasan al-Basri and Muhammad ibn Sirin. His long life and ten years of personal service gave him a uniquely detailed knowledge of the Prophet's Sunnah in every dimension of daily life.
bio_short_id: Anas bin Malik adalah Sahabat Nabi yang melayani Rasulullah ﷺ selama 10 tahun. Ia meriwayatkan lebih dari 2.000 hadith dan hidup hingga usia lanjut, menjadi sumber utama bagi ulama tabiin di Basra.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Umar (RA), son of the second caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab, was among the most prolific and scrupulous hadith narrators among the companions. He embraced Islam as a child and participated in major military campaigns. Known for his extreme caution in narrating hadith and his strict adherence to the Prophet's Sunnah in every detail of life, he was widely respected for his piety and precision. His narrations number in the thousands and are found throughout the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Umar (RA), son of the second Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab and brother-in-law of the Prophet ﷺ, was born in Mecca around 610 CE. He embraced Islam as a young boy alongside his father, and though he was turned away from participating in the Battle of Badr for being too young, he was permitted to fight at Uhud at approximately fifteen years of age. He is counted among the most prolific companion narrators with over 2,630 hadiths, making him one of the Mukthirun. He was celebrated above all for his extraordinary meticulousness: he would follow the Prophet's Sunnah in the smallest details of life, including the exact spots where the Prophet ﷺ would dismount during travel, pray, or rest. Imam al-Shafi'i said: 'No one among the companions preserved the Sunnah more completely than Ibn Umar.' He deliberately avoided political involvement during the period of civil strife and was widely respected across all factions for his piety and impartiality. He lived in Medina until shortly before his death in 73 or 74 AH — reportedly killed by a poisoned arrow during the siege of Mecca — making him among the last surviving senior companions. His narrations are transmitted extensively through his son Salim and his freed slave Nafi', both of whom are themselves leading authorities in hadith.
bio_short_id: Abdullah bin Umar adalah putra Khalifah Umar bin Khattab dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak dari kalangan Sahabat. Ia dikenal atas ketakwaannya yang luar biasa dan kepatuhan ketat terhadap Sunnah Nabi dalam setiap detail kehidupan.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), the cousin of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, was born three years before the Hijra and died in 68 AH. Nicknamed Hibr al-Ummah (the Scholar of the Ummah) and Tarjuman al-Quran (the Interpreter of the Quran), he narrated over 1,600 hadiths. The Prophet ﷺ made special supplication for him: 'O Allah, teach him wisdom and the interpretation of the Quran.' He became the foremost authority on Quranic exegesis among the Companions, and thousands of students traveled to learn from him. He is considered the founder of the tafsir tradition in Islam.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), known as Hibr al-Ummah (the Scholar of the Ummah) and Turjuman al-Quran (the Interpreter of the Quran), was the cousin of the Prophet ﷺ, born three years before the Hijra. He grew up in the Prophet's household and was deeply shaped by his proximity to revelation. The Prophet ﷺ made a famous supplication for him: 'O Allah, give him deep understanding of the religion and teach him the interpretation of the Quran.' This prayer was conspicuously answered: Ibn Abbas became the undisputed foremost authority on Quranic exegesis among all the companions and subsequent generations. After the Prophet's death, though still a young man, he devoted himself to learning from the senior companions, reportedly waiting at the doors of companions like Zayd ibn Thabit to acquire single hadiths. He narrated over 1,660 hadiths and gathered thousands of students who sat in his circles at Mecca and Ta'if. He served as governor of Basra for a period during Ali's caliphate. His tafsir narrations — transmitted especially through his student Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and Ikrima — are the foundation of the classical tafsir tradition. He lost his eyesight in his later years and passed away in Ta'if around 68 AH.
bio_short_id: Abdullah bin Abbas adalah sepupu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan dijuluki "Tarjuman al-Quran" (Penafsir Al-Quran). Nabi mendoakannya dengan: "Ya Allah, ajarkan dia hikmah dan tafsir Al-Quran." Ia menjadi otoritas utama dalam ilmu tafsir.

bio_short_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah (RA) was an Ansari companion from Medina who narrated over 1,500 hadiths covering worship, transactions, the Prophet's biography, and many other topics. He participated in numerous military expeditions alongside the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and was known for his close companionship and reliability as a narrator. He lived into the era of the Tabi'in and continued teaching and transmitting prophetic knowledge until late in his life, making him one of the most cited companion narrators in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (RA) was born in Medina and belonged to the Khazraj tribe. His father Abdullah ibn Amr was among the Ansar who took the pledge of Aqabah before the Hijra, and Jabir himself grew up in the immediate orbit of the Prophet ﷺ. He participated in nineteen military expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ and was a constant companion in many of the most significant moments of the prophetic mission. He narrated over 1,540 hadiths, placing him among the most prolific companion narrators. His narrations cover all areas of Islamic practice and are especially notable for highly detailed accounts such as the full description of the Farewell Pilgrimage, which is one of the most complete accounts of the Hajj ritual in the Sunnah. After the Prophet's death, Jabir lived long enough to become a major teaching authority in Medina for the Tabi'in generation. Scholars traveled from distant regions to hear from him. He reportedly lost his eyesight in old age. He passed away in Medina around 78 AH, having spent the final decades of his life transmitting prophetic knowledge to the next generation. His narrations are foundational across all major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin Abdullah adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.500 hadith. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ dan menjadi sumber penting bagi ulama tabiin di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Masud (RA) was among the earliest Muslims and one of the most learned companions in Quranic recitation and Islamic jurisprudence. He was so close to the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) that he served as his personal attendant in many matters. The Prophet described him as one whose guidance should be followed. He was a primary teacher in Kufa, and his students became leading scholars of the next generation. His narrations are especially valued for their precision and deep legal insight.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA) was among the very first people to embrace Islam in Mecca, accepting the faith while still a young shepherd boy in service to Uqba ibn Abi Mu'ayt. He is said to have been the sixth person to enter Islam. His closeness to the Prophet ﷺ was exceptional: he was given permission to enter the Prophet's home without announcing himself, and the companions described him as the person most similar to the Prophet in his walk, his conduct, and his manners. He was one of the primary scribes and readers of the Quran and the Prophet ﷺ specifically recommended that people learn the Quran from him. He narrated 848 hadiths and was appointed by Caliph Umar (RA) as a teacher, treasurer, and judge in Kufa — a position of enormous influence over one of the main intellectual centers of early Islam. His students in Kufa included Alqama ibn Qais, al-Aswad ibn Yazid, and Masruq — all of whom became leading Tabi'i scholars. He was deeply learned in Islamic law and Quranic recitation, and is considered one of the four great authorities on the Quran among the companions. He passed away in Medina around 32–33 AH.
bio_short_id: Abdullah bin Masud adalah salah satu Sahabat yang pertama masuk Islam dan sangat dekat dengan Nabi ﷺ. Ia dikenal sebagai ahli Al-Quran dan meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, fikih, dan akhlak.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who was uniquely permitted by the Prophet to write down hadiths, making him one of the earliest systematic recorders of prophetic traditions. He was known for his intense devotion to worship and learning, and he narrated hundreds of hadiths on a wide range of religious topics. He also had knowledge of earlier scriptures, which gave him a broad perspective on religious matters. His narrations are found in all major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (RA) was the son of the famous companion and general Amr ibn al-As, but he embraced Islam before his father. He was one of the few companions explicitly permitted by the Prophet ﷺ to write down hadiths in a personal collection, which he titled al-Sahifah al-Sadiqah — the Truthful Scroll. This makes him one of the earliest systematic recorders of prophetic traditions in Islamic history. He narrated approximately 700 hadiths, though his full collection was larger. He was known for his extreme devotion to worship — fasting continuously and standing in prayer all night — until the Prophet ﷺ counseled him to moderate his practice and balance worship with the rights of his body, family, and guests. He was also knowledgeable in the scriptures of earlier prophets, which gave him a broad comparative religious perspective. He participated in many military campaigns. His narrations are found in all six major hadith collections and span a wide range of legal, ethical, and eschatological topics. He passed away around 65–68 AH.
bio_short_id: Abdullah ibn Amr adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 158 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was the cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and the fourth Caliph of Islam. He was among the first to embrace Islam and was raised in the Prophet's household, giving him unparalleled access to prophetic knowledge and daily practice. Renowned for his scholarship, piety, and bravery, he narrated numerous hadiths and is considered one of the most authoritative sources on prophetic traditions, particularly in matters of jurisprudence, prayer, and governance.
bio_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was the cousin of the Prophet ﷺ and was raised in the Prophet's household from a young age, when his father Abu Talib was burdened by poverty and the Prophet ﷺ took him in. He was among the very first to accept Islam — by some accounts the first male to do so. He married the Prophet's daughter Fatimah (RA) and was the father of al-Hasan and al-Husayn. He was renowned for his extraordinary courage, demonstrated in every major battle of early Islam, and the Prophet ﷺ gave him the honorific Asad Allah al-Ghalib — the Victorious Lion of Allah. He served as the fourth Caliph of Islam from 35 to 40 AH, during a turbulent period of civil strife. His scholarship was vast: he was deeply versed in Quranic interpretation, Islamic jurisprudence, Arabic rhetoric, and prophetic traditions. He narrated approximately 536 hadiths, and his legal opinions are quoted extensively across all schools of Islamic law. He was martyred in the mosque of Kufa in 40 AH by Abd al-Rahman ibn Muljam, a Kharijite. His narrations and legal legacy have deeply shaped both Sunni and Shia scholarly traditions.
bio_short_id: Ali bin Abi Thalib adalah sepupu dan menantu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, serta Khalifah keempat dalam Islam. Ia adalah salah satu ulama terbesar di kalangan Sahabat dan meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang ibadah dan hukum Islam.

bio_short_en: Abu Musa al-Ashari (RA) was a companion from Yemen who emigrated to Medina and became a devoted follower of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). He served as a governor in both Basra and Kufa and was known for his exceptionally beautiful recitation of the Quran, a quality the Prophet himself praised and admired. A prolific narrator, he transmitted hundreds of hadiths and was greatly respected by the companions and Tabi'in for his religious knowledge, upright character, and dedicated service to Islam.
bio_en: Abu Musa al-Ashari (RA), whose full name was Abdullah ibn Qays al-Ashari, was from the tribe of Ash'ar in Yemen. He emigrated to Abyssinia in the early years of Islam and later came to Medina, arriving with Jafar ibn Abi Talib at the time of the Battle of Khaybar. He became a devoted companion and was described by the Prophet ﷺ as possessing a voice like a flute from the family of Prophet Dawud — a recognition of his exceptionally beautiful Quran recitation. He served as governor of Basra and then Kufa under Caliph Umar (RA), and later played a significant role in the events of the civil war as an arbitrator. He narrated approximately 360 hadiths on a wide range of topics including prayer, purification, and social conduct. He was known for his gentleness, his administrative ability, and his deep knowledge of Islamic law. His son Abu Burda narrated extensively from him and became a major transmitter of his father's knowledge to the Tabi'in. He passed away around 42–52 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Musa al-Asy'ari adalah Sahabat Nabi dari Yaman yang terkenal dengan suara indahnya dalam membaca Al-Quran. Ia menjabat sebagai gubernur Basra dan Kufah, dan meriwayatkan banyak hadith.

bio_short_en: Abu Said al-Khudri (RA), whose full name was Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari, was born in Madinah and lived until 74 AH. He participated in twelve military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ and became one of the most prolific transmitters of hadith, narrating over 1,170 traditions. He was particularly known for his detailed transmission of the Prophet's guidance on acts of worship, spiritual purification, and social conduct. He remained in Madinah throughout his life, teaching hadith and serving as a reference for the Companions and Successors on religious matters.
bio_en: Abu Said al-Khudri (RA), whose full name was Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari al-Khazraji, was born in Medina and participated in twelve military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ, beginning from the Battle of al-Khandaq (the Trench). He was too young to participate at Uhud, and his father was martyred there. He narrated over 1,170 hadiths, making him one of the most prolific companion narrators and one of the Mukthirun. He was particularly active in transmitting hadith after the Prophet's death and was known for his strong stance against recording hadiths without due care, insisting on precision. His narrations are especially notable for detailed guidance on acts of worship, the nature of the afterlife, the signs of the Last Day, and social ethics. He remained in Medina throughout his life, refusing to leave, and the city's scholarly community relied on him as a living reference for the Sunnah well into the Tabi'i era. He passed away in Medina around 74 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir. Ia dikenal atas keberaniannya dalam menyampaikan kebenaran.

bio_short_en: Nafi was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and freed slave of Abdullah ibn Umar (RA). He is one of the most important links in the chain of hadith transmission, particularly in the famous isnad known as the 'Golden Chain' — Malik from Nafi from Ibn Umar. He served Ibn Umar closely for many years and transmitted a vast body of prophetic narrations on his authority. Imam Malik considered him among the most trustworthy of narrators, and his reports are found throughout the major hadith collections and are held in the highest regard.
bio_en: Nafi was a mawla (freed slave) of Abdullah ibn Umar (RA) who served him for many years and became one of the most important figures in the chain of hadith transmission. His exact origins are uncertain — some accounts suggest he was from Nishapur in Khorasan and was brought to Medina as a captive. He spent his life in close proximity to Ibn Umar, learning from him directly and recording his practices in meticulous detail. He became the primary transmitter of Ibn Umar's narrations to the next generation, and his chain — Malik from Nafi from Ibn Umar — was famously called by Imam al-Bukhari the 'Golden Chain' (silsilat al-dhahab). He also transmitted from other companions and Tabi'in. Imam Malik ibn Anas was his most celebrated student and drew heavily on his narrations in al-Muwatta. He is regarded by scholars of hadith criticism as fully reliable and trustworthy (thiqah). He passed away in Medina around 117–120 AH.
bio_short_id: Nafi' adalah maula (budak yang dimerdekakan) Abdullah bin Umar dan salah satu perawi hadith terpercaya dari generasi tabiin. Riwayatnya melalui sanad Malik — Nafi' — Ibnu Umar disebut sebagai "silsilah emas" dalam ilmu hadith.

bio_short_en: Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who lived to an exceptional age, reportedly becoming one of the last surviving companions in Medina. His long life allowed him to narrate prophetic traditions to generations of Tabi'in who had not witnessed the Prophet directly, making him a vital link in the chain of transmission. He transmitted narrations covering various aspects of the Prophet's life, worship, and character, and is regarded as a trustworthy and valued narrator.
bio_en: Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (RA) was from the Ansar of Medina, from the tribe of Banu Sa'ida of the Khazraj. He was born shortly before the Hijra and accompanied the Prophet ﷺ from his earliest years of awareness. His long life — he reportedly lived to over ninety years and died around 88–91 AH — made him one of the last surviving companions in Medina. This extraordinary longevity gave him an irreplaceable role: Tabi'i scholars who had never met the Prophet ﷺ could still receive direct narrations from a man who had seen and served him. He narrated approximately 188 hadiths on the Prophet's worship, character, miracles, and daily conduct. He was known for his reliability and his deep attachment to Medina, which he reportedly never left. His student Hazim ibn Abi Hazim was among those who transmitted his narrations to later generations. He is cited throughout all the major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Sahl bin Sa'd al-Sa'idi adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang kehidupan di Madinah. Ia hidup hingga usia sangat tua dan menjadi salah satu Sahabat terakhir yang meninggal di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was a Companion from the Ansar of Madinah who participated in eighteen military expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ. Though he was initially too young to participate in the Battle of Badr — the Prophet ﷺ sent him back due to his young age — he later took part in all subsequent major campaigns. He narrated approximately 305 hadiths covering various aspects of worship, conduct, and the Prophet's character. He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death and was highly regarded as a transmitter in Iraq.
bio_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was from the Ansar of Medina, of the tribe of Aws. He was one of the companions who was initially prevented from participating in the Battle of Badr because of his young age — the Prophet ﷺ sent him back — and this detail is preserved in hadith. He went on to participate in eighteen military expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ, including all of the major campaigns. He narrated approximately 305 hadiths covering prayer, the description of the Prophet ﷺ, funeral rites, and events from the early Islamic conquests. After the Prophet's death he settled in Kufa, where he became an important and reliable transmitter of prophetic knowledge in Iraq. His narrations are found in all major hadith collections and are particularly valued for their firsthand accounts of the Prophet's conduct during battles and expeditions. He passed away in Kufa around 72 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Bara' bin Azib adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang peperangan dan ibadah. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ dan dikenal atas kesalehannya.

bio_short_en: AbuSa'id al-Khudri (RA) was an Ansari companion and prolific narrator of hadith who transmitted hundreds of prophetic traditions across a wide range of religious topics. He was devoted to preserving and spreading the Sunnah after the Prophet Muhammad's (peace be upon him) death and is particularly noted for narrations related to worship, eschatology, and the signs of the Last Day. His dedication to accuracy and his close companionship with the Prophet made him a trusted authority among subsequent scholars of hadith.
bio_en: AbuSa'id al-Khudri (RA) is an alternate entry for Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari, known by his kunya Abu Sa'id al-Khudri. He was an Ansari companion from Medina who participated in twelve military campaigns with the Prophet ﷺ. He narrated a very large number of hadiths — well over one thousand — and was one of the most dedicated of the companions in transmitting the Sunnah after the Prophet's death. He is especially known for his narrations on eschatology, the signs of the Last Day, acts of worship, and the importance of commanding good and forbidding evil. His biography in the collections under his full name is the primary reference. He passed away in Medina around 74 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), cousin of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), was the foremost Quranic exegete of his generation and is known by the title 'Scholar of the Ummah.' The Prophet prayed specifically for him to be granted wisdom and deep understanding of the religion. Ibn Abbas narrated hundreds of hadiths and his explanations of the Quran are foundational to classical tafsir literature. He gathered knowledge from senior companions after the Prophet's death, becoming an encyclopedic authority recognized across all schools of Islamic scholarship.
bio_en: Ibn Abbas (RA) — this entry refers to Abdullah ibn Abbas in the Bulugh al-Maram collection. Abdullah ibn Abbas was the cousin of the Prophet ﷺ, born approximately three years before the Hijra, and is the foremost authority on Quranic exegesis in Islamic history. The Prophet ﷺ prayed specifically for him to receive wisdom and deep understanding of the Quran. His full biography is documented in entry 5 above. The Prophet's prayer was unmistakably answered: Ibn Abbas became the undisputed authority on Quranic interpretation among all the companions, and his narrations and explanations remained the primary source of classical tafsir for over a millennium. His narrations in Bulugh al-Maram cover a wide range of legal rulings drawn from his vast knowledge of the Sunnah. He passed away around 68 AH in Ta'if.
bio_short_id: Ibn 'Abbas (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 45 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) from the Ansar of Medina. He narrated a large number of hadiths and was known for his commitment to transmitting the Prophet's words accurately and completely. His narrations span a wide range of religious topics including prayer, purification, charity, and the afterlife, and are considered authoritative in the hadith sciences. He continued teaching and narrating after the Prophet's death, serving as an important link to the prophetic tradition for subsequent generations.
bio_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA) — this entry represents the same companion, Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan, appearing under a slightly variant transliteration in the data. His full biography is covered under entry 11. He was one of the most prolific Ansari companion narrators, with over 1,170 hadiths, and was a resident of Medina until his death around 74 AH. He narrated extensively on eschatology, worship, and prophetic ethics. His name appears under several transliterations across different hadith collections: Abu Sa'id, AbuSa'id, Abu Sa'eed, Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri — all referring to the same companion.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir.

bio_short_en: Urwa ibn al-Zubayr was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and the son of al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam, a companion of the Prophet (peace be upon him). As the nephew of Aisha (RA), he was one of the most important transmitters of her extensive narrations and a primary source for knowledge of the Prophet's household. Urwa was among the Seven Jurists of Medina, a recognized group of Tabi'in scholars whose scholarly opinions shaped early Islamic jurisprudence. His narrations appear frequently in all major hadith collections and he is regarded as highly reliable.
bio_en: Urwa ibn al-Zubayr was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina, born around 23 AH. He was the son of the companion al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA) — one of the ten companions given glad tidings of Paradise — and the nephew of Aisha (RA) through his mother Asma bint Abi Bakr. This family position made him one of the most important transmitters of Aisha's extensive narrations, which he learned directly from her. He is counted among the Seven Jurists of Medina (al-fuqaha al-sab'a), a recognized group of Tabi'i scholars whose scholarly consensus shaped the foundations of the Medinan legal tradition and influenced Imam Malik profoundly. He was known for his reliability, his vast knowledge of seerah and hadith, and his refusal to engage in political controversies. His students included al-Zuhri and Hisham ibn Urwa, his own son. He reportedly suffered the amputation of a leg and the death of a son on the same day, and bore both calamities with exemplary patience. He passed away around 93–94 AH.
bio_short_id: Urwa bin az-Zubair adalah tabiin terkemuka dan keponakan Aisyah RA. Ia adalah salah satu dari tujuh fuqaha Madinah dan perawi utama hadith dari Aisyah.

bio_short_en: Aishah (RA), the beloved wife of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, was one of the greatest scholarly authorities in the early Muslim community. She narrated thousands of hadiths, particularly on matters of the Prophet's personal conduct, worship, and household life. Companions and Tabi'in regularly sought her rulings on religious questions, and she was known for her willingness to correct errors in narration. Her deep understanding of Islamic law and her proximity to the Prophet made her an unparalleled source of authentic prophetic tradition.
bio_en: Aisha (RA) — this entry refers to the same Mother of the Believers whose full biography appears under entry 2, here appearing under a variant transliteration in the Bulugh al-Maram collection. Aisha bint Abi Bakr was the wife of the Prophet ﷺ, the daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, and one of the greatest scholarly authorities in early Islam. She narrated thousands of hadiths covering the Prophet's worship, household conduct, legal rulings, and personal character. Her narrations in Bulugh al-Maram reflect her authority across all areas of Islamic jurisprudence. She passed away around 57–58 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA) was one of the earliest converts to Islam, embracing the faith while still a young man in Makkah. He was so close to the Prophet ﷺ that he was given permission to enter the Prophet's home without seeking permission and described as one who resembled the Prophet ﷺ most closely in his walk and conduct. He narrated 848 hadiths and was appointed by Umar (RA) as a teacher and judge in Kufa. He is particularly renowned for his Quranic recitation and is considered one of the four great authorities on the Quran among the Companions.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA) — this entry under the spelling 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud' refers to the same companion whose full biography appears under entry 7. He was among the first six to embrace Islam, served the Prophet ﷺ with exceptional intimacy, and was one of the foremost authorities on the Quran and hadith. He narrated 848 hadiths and was appointed by Umar (RA) as judge and teacher in Kufa. The narrations attributed to this entry in the collections include his famous reports on the importance of correct prayer and the warnings against innovations in worship. He passed away in Medina around 32–33 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 39 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) was the cousin of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and one of the most learned scholars among the companions, known by the title 'Scholar of the Ummah.' He devoted his life to Quranic scholarship and hadith narration. His narrations and tafsir explanations are central references in Islamic scholarship across all traditions and legal schools. He gathered knowledge diligently from senior companions after the Prophet's death and became an encyclopedic authority whose contributions remain indispensable to Islamic learning.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) — this entry under the spelling 'Abdullah bin Abbas' is the same companion as entries 5 and 16, appearing under a different transliteration in the data. Abdullah ibn Abbas was the cousin of the Prophet ﷺ and the foremost exegete of the Quran among the companions. He narrated over 1,660 hadiths and is regarded as the founding authority of the tafsir tradition. The narrations in this subset of the collection further attest to the breadth of his scholarship. His full biography is documented under entry 5. He passed away in Ta'if around 68 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 39 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (RA) was one of the earliest Muslims and among the most ascetic and outspoken of the Prophet Muhammad's (peace be upon him) companions. He was known for his strong stance against worldly attachment, accumulation of wealth, and injustice. He narrated numerous hadiths and is particularly remembered for narrations emphasizing simplicity, sincerity, and social justice in Islam. His bold character and proximity to the Prophet made him a distinctive and respected figure, and his narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (RA), whose full name was Jundub ibn Junadah, was from the tribe of Ghifar in the Hijaz. He is reported by some scholars to have been the fourth or fifth person to embrace Islam — coming to Mecca before the Hijra and accepting the Prophet's message very early. He was profoundly ascetic, rejecting the accumulation of wealth and refusing all worldly comfort beyond necessity. He was known for his forthright character: he would speak truth without fear regardless of who was listening, and the Prophet ﷺ described him as 'the most truthful-tongued of my community.' He narrated approximately 281 hadiths. During Uthman's caliphate his views on wealth brought him into conflict with certain governors, and he was eventually sent to live in al-Rabadha in the Arabian interior, where he died alone around 31–32 AH. He is revered across Islamic tradition as a model of sincerity, social conscience, and renunciation of the world.
bio_short_id: Abu Dzar al-Ghifari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal atas kezuhudan dan keterusterangannya dalam menyampaikan kebenaran. Ia termasuk di antara orang-orang pertama yang masuk Islam.

bio_short_en: Hudhaifa ibn al-Yaman (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) uniquely entrusted with knowledge of the signs of trials, tribulations, and future events (fitan). The Prophet shared with him the names of the hypocrites and secrets about events to come, making him a singular source for this type of narration. His hadiths are particularly important in chapters on eschatology and civil strife. He later served as a trusted governor of Mada'in under Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA).
bio_en: Hudhaifa ibn al-Yaman (RA) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who was uniquely entrusted with knowledge that no other companion possessed: the Prophet ﷺ disclosed to him the names of the hypocrites in Medina and confided in him knowledge about the major trials and tribulations (fitan) that would befall the Muslim community. Because of this, Hudhaifa became known as Sahib al-Sirr — Keeper of the Secret. Senior companions including Umar ibn al-Khattab would quietly observe whether Hudhaifa prayed the funeral prayer over a deceased person, using this as an indication of whether the deceased was truly a hypocrite. He narrated approximately 225 hadiths, and his narrations in chapters on fitan and the signs of the Last Day are among the most important in the entire hadith literature. He served as governor of Mada'in under Caliph Umar (RA). He passed away shortly after Uthman's assassination, around 35–36 AH, reportedly having prayed not to outlive the fitna of civil strife — a prayer that was answered.
bio_short_id: Hudzaifah bin al-Yaman adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dipercaya menyimpan rahasia tentang orang-orang munafiq. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang fitnah dan tanda-tanda hari kiamat.

bio_short_en: Umm Salama (RA), whose full name was Hind bint Abi Umayya, was a wife of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She was among the early Muslims who emigrated to Abyssinia and later to Medina, enduring great hardship for her faith. Known for her wisdom and sound judgment, she narrated numerous hadiths and was frequently consulted on religious matters by companions and Tabi'in alike. Her narrations appear throughout the major hadith collections and are considered authoritative.
bio_en: Umm Salama (RA), whose full name was Hind bint Abi Umayya al-Makhzumiyya, was one of the earliest Muslims and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She and her first husband Abu Salama were among those who emigrated to Abyssinia in the first hijra, then later to Medina. After Abu Salama's death from a wound sustained at Uhud, she became a wife of the Prophet ﷺ. She was known for her wisdom, her sound judgment, and her courage in offering counsel: she famously advised the Prophet ﷺ at al-Hudaybiyya when the companions were reluctant to follow his instruction, and her advice proved decisive. She narrated approximately 378 hadiths covering the Prophet's personal conduct, worship, household life, and legal rulings. Companions and Tabi'in regularly sought her rulings and clarifications on religious matters. She outlived all the other wives of the Prophet ﷺ, dying around 62–63 AH — the last of the Mothers of the Believers to pass away.
bio_short_id: Ummu Salamah adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu Ummahatul Mukminin. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan dikenal atas kebijaksanaan serta kecerdasannya.

bio_short_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah (RA) was an Ansari companion from Medina who became one of the most prolific narrators among the companions. He participated in numerous battles alongside the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and spent many years transmitting prophetic knowledge to the Tabi'in. Known for his reliability, sincerity, and dedication to the Sunnah, his narrations cover a wide range of topics including worship, commerce, and the Prophet's Farewell Pilgrimage. He is among the most frequently cited companions in all major hadith literature.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah (RA) — this entry in the Bulugh al-Maram collection refers to the same companion whose full biography appears under entry 6. Jabir was from the Ansar of Medina, participated in nineteen expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ, and narrated over 1,540 hadiths. He was among the last of the senior companions to survive in Medina and continued teaching the Tabi'in until late in his life. His narrations in Bulugh al-Maram are particularly significant for legal rulings on transactions, marriage, and acts of worship. He passed away around 78 AH.
bio_short_id: Jabir (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 33 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr was a distinguished Tabi'i scholar and student of Ibn Abbas (RA). He was renowned for his deep knowledge of Quranic exegesis and Islamic law, and he transmitted many narrations from the companions with great care and accuracy. Known for his courage and piety, he ultimately died as a martyr at the hands of the tyrant al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf, refusing to renounce his principles. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections and he is regarded as one of the most reliable transmitters of his generation.
bio_en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr was a prominent Tabi'i scholar of Kufa, born around 45 AH. He was of Abyssinian origin and was the mawla (freed client) of the tribe of Banu Asad. He was among the most devoted students of Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) and acquired from him a profound mastery of Quranic tafsir and Islamic law. He also narrated from Ibn Umar, Aisha, and other senior companions. Scholars of his generation regarded him as one of the most knowledgeable and reliable narrators alive, with Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal considering him among the most learned of the Tabi'in. He was also a teacher of al-Zuhri and other major scholars. His end was tragic but glorious: the tyrant al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf captured and executed him around 94–95 AH after he refused to submit to his authority or renounce his principles. His martyrdom is considered one of the great tragedies of the Tabi'i generation. His narrations appear in all the major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Sa'id bin Jubair adalah tabiin terkemuka dan murid Ibn Abbas. Ia dikenal sebagai ahli tafsir dan fikih, dan syahid dibunuh oleh al-Hajjaj bin Yusuf karena tidak mau mendukung pemberontakan Ibn al-Asy'ats.

bio_short_en: Abu Bakra (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who embraced Islam during the siege of Ta'if and was freed from slavery upon his acceptance of the faith. He settled in Basra after the Prophet's death and became an important source of prophetic traditions for the Muslim community there. He narrated numerous hadiths on various aspects of Islamic practice and belief, and his narrations are cited frequently in the major collections. He was known for his reliability and sincere commitment to the Sunnah.
bio_en: Abu Bakra (RA), whose full name was Nufay ibn al-Harith al-Thaqafi, was a slave who had been held in the fortress of Ta'if. During the siege of Ta'if in 8 AH he escaped by lowering himself from the walls on a pulley (bakra), and this act gave him his kunya. He came to the Prophet ﷺ and embraced Islam, and the Prophet ﷺ freed him. He settled in Basra after the Prophet's death and became one of the most important companions in that region, serving as a teacher and religious authority for the Muslim community there. He narrated approximately 132 hadiths and is especially well known for the narration in which the Prophet ﷺ warned against taking up arms in civil strife. This narration had a direct impact on Abu Bakra's own life: he refused to fight on either side in the Battle of the Camel and remained neutral. He passed away in Basra around 51–52 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Bakrah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith, termasuk hadith masyhur tentang dilarangnya membunuh pemimpin dan fitnah di akhir zaman. Ia tinggal di Basra.

bio_short_en: Umm Salamah (RA) was one of the Mothers of the Believers and a wife of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) of great wisdom and noble character. She was among the earliest Muslims and one of those who emigrated to Abyssinia before the Hijra. She narrated many hadiths and was known for her careful preservation of prophetic teachings, particularly on matters relating to women's religious practice, family life, and the Prophet's private conduct. Her narrations are valued across all major hadith collections.
bio_en: Umm Salamah (RA) — this entry under 'Umm Salamah, Ummul Mu'minin' refers to the same Mother of the Believers whose full biography appears under entry 24, here appearing under a slightly different transliteration in the Abu Dawud collection. Umm Salamah was one of the earliest Muslims, emigrated twice for the sake of Islam, and became a wife of the Prophet ﷺ after the death of her first husband. She was renowned for her wisdom, her large number of hadith narrations (approximately 378), and her role as a counselor and scholar in the early community. She outlived all the other wives of the Prophet ﷺ and passed away around 62–63 AH.
bio_short_id: Ummu Salamah adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu Ummahatul Mukminin. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan dikenal atas kebijaksanaan serta kecerdasannya.

bio_short_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) was the second Caliph of Islam and one of the most influential companions of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). His conversion to Islam was a turning point for the early Muslim community, and his caliphate is regarded as a model of just governance. Known for his unwavering commitment to the Sunnah, he narrated many hadiths and his juristic opinions are foundational references across all schools of Islamic law. His narrations are found in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) was the second Caliph of Islam and one of the greatest companions of the Prophet ﷺ. His conversion to Islam — around the fifth or sixth year of the prophethood — was a turning point for the early Muslim community, strengthening its resolve and enabling the Muslims to pray openly at the Kaaba for the first time. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Islam has been strengthened by the conversion of Umar.' He was known for his uncompromising adherence to justice, his deep reverence for the Sunnah, and his outstanding administrative ability. He expanded the Islamic state more than any other Caliph, overseeing the conquest of Persia, Iraq, Syria, and Egypt. He narrated approximately 537 hadiths. His juristic opinions — known as Umar's qiyas — are referenced in all schools of Islamic law, and many of his administrative precedents became standard Islamic governance. He was martyred in 23 AH while leading Fajr prayer, stabbed by a Persian slave named Abu Lu'lu'a.
bio_short_id: Umar bin al-Khattab adalah Khalifah kedua dalam Islam dan salah satu Sahabat paling berpengaruh. Ia meriwayatkan ratusan hadith dan memerintah selama 10 tahun dengan keadilan yang diakui.

bio_short_en: Masruq ibn al-Ajda was a Tabi'i scholar of Kufa who was a devoted student of major companions including Abdullah ibn Masud (RA) and Aisha (RA). He was highly regarded for his knowledge of Islamic law, his scrupulous approach to hadith narration, and his personal piety. He transmitted many narrations from the companions and is considered a reliable and authoritative link in the chains of transmission. Scholars praised him for his precision and integrity, and his narrations appear throughout the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Masruq ibn al-Ajda al-Hamdani al-Wada'i was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Kufa. He emigrated from Yemen to Kufa as a young man and became a devoted student of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA), from whom he acquired his primary knowledge of the Quran and Sunnah. He also narrated extensively from Aisha (RA), traveling to Medina specifically to learn from her and developing a close scholarly relationship with her. Other teachers included Umar ibn al-Khattab and Abu Bakr al-Siddiq. He was known for his exceptional piety, his night-long prayers, and his meticulous care in hadith narration. Imam al-Shafi'i and others praised him as one of the most reliable Tabi'i scholars. He was among the Seven Jurists of Kufa in some listings. His narrations appear throughout the major hadith collections and he is regarded as a fully trustworthy transmitter. He passed away around 62–63 AH.
bio_short_id: Masruq bin al-Ajda' adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah dan murid utama Abdullah bin Masud dan Aisyah. Ia dikenal atas ibadah dan ilmunya yang mendalam.

bio_short_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) was the second Caliph of Islam and a companion of the highest rank and distinction. He was among the closest associates of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), whose daughter Hafsa (RA) became a wife of the Prophet. His conversion strengthened the early Muslim community. He narrated hadiths that are foundational to Islamic scholarship and legal thought, and his understanding of the Sunnah deeply influenced the development of Islamic jurisprudence across all subsequent generations of scholars.
bio_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) — this entry represents the second Caliph appearing under a slightly variant transliteration. His full biography is documented under entry 29. He was the second Caliph, a companion of the highest distinction, and a narrator of approximately 537 hadiths. His narrations span all areas of Islamic jurisprudence, and his opinions shaped the legal schools profoundly. He was martyred in 23 AH.
bio_short_id: Umar bin al-Khattab adalah Khalifah kedua dalam Islam dan salah satu Sahabat paling berpengaruh. Ia meriwayatkan ratusan hadith.

bio_short_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (RA) was one of the ten companions given glad tidings of Paradise by the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and was among the earliest Muslims, having embraced Islam as a young man. He was a skilled military commander who led the Muslim forces to victory at the Battle of al-Qadisiyyah, opening Persia to Islam. He narrated numerous hadiths covering faith, prayer, and prophetic biography, and is cited in the major hadith collections as a trustworthy companion of the highest distinction.
bio_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (RA), whose full name was Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Uhayb al-Zuhri, was from the Quraysh of Mecca and was one of the earliest Muslims — by his own account, the third or seventh person to embrace Islam. He was about sixteen or seventeen years old when he accepted Islam. He is one of the ten companions explicitly promised Paradise by the Prophet ﷺ (al-ashara al-mubashshara). He was a skilled archer and the Prophet ﷺ said of him: 'Shoot, Sa'd — may my mother and father be ransom for you' — a distinction not given to any other companion. He was the commanding general of the Muslim forces at the pivotal Battle of al-Qadisiyyah in 14–15 AH, which led to the collapse of the Sasanian Persian empire and the opening of Iraq and Persia to Islam. He later served as the first governor of Kufa. He narrated approximately 270 hadiths and passed away around 55–58 AH, reportedly the last of the ten promised Paradise to die.
bio_short_id: Sa'd bin Abi Waqqash adalah salah satu dari sepuluh Sahabat yang dijamin masuk surga. Ia adalah pahlawan penaklukan Persia dan meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting.

bio_short_en: Abu Wail Shaqiq ibn Salama al-Asadi was a leading Successor (Tabi'i) from Kufa who lived to approximately 82 AH. Though he missed seeing the Prophet ﷺ, he narrated hadiths from major Companions including Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman, and Abu Musa al-Ash'ari. He was among the most reliable and trusted transmitters in Kufa and a major link in the isnad chains connecting the Companions to the next generation. Scholars of hadith relied on his transmission extensively in the Six Major Collections.
bio_en: Abu Wail Shaqiq ibn Salama al-Asadi was a leading Tabi'i scholar from Kufa who, despite not meeting the Prophet ﷺ directly, became one of the most significant transmitters between the companion generation and the Tabi'in. He narrated from an exceptionally wide circle of companions including Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Hudhaifa ibn al-Yaman, Abu Musa al-Ashari, and Ammar ibn Yasir. He was among the most reliable and trusted narrators in Kufa and was the teacher of later leading scholars including al-Amash. He was known for his long life — reportedly reaching approximately 82 AH or later — his deep asceticism, and his consistent reliability as a narrator. Hadith critics considered him fully trustworthy (thiqah). His narrations appear extensively in all six major hadith collections, and he serves as an essential bridge between the companions of the Prophet ﷺ and the generation that came after them.
bio_short_id: Abu Wail Syaqiq bin Salamah adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah dan murid Abdullah bin Masud dan Umar. Ia dikenal atas kezuhudan dan penguasaan ilmunya.

bio_short_en: Muhammad ibn Shihab al-Zuhri was one of the most important Tabi'i scholars and hadith transmitters in Islamic history. He was among the first to systematically collect and organize hadiths at the encouragement of Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, playing a foundational role in the formal preservation of the Sunnah. He narrated from numerous companions and Tabi'in and is considered a cornerstone figure in the science of hadith. His narrations appear extensively throughout all major hadith collections and are regarded as among the most reliable.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (full name: Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Ubaydullah ibn Shihab al-Zuhri) was one of the most pivotal scholars in the entire history of hadith transmission. He was a Tabi'i, born around 50–58 AH in Medina, and he narrated from a remarkable array of senior companions and Tabi'in including Anas ibn Malik, Sahl ibn Sa'd, and Urwa ibn al-Zubayr. He was among the first scholars to systematically collect and transmit hadiths as an organized discipline, doing so at the explicit instruction of Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz. His efforts are considered foundational in the formal preservation and codification of the Sunnah. Imam Malik, Ibn Uyayna, and al-Awzai were among his most prominent students. He was considered the most knowledgeable person of his time regarding the differences among the companions and the early Muslim community. He passed away in 124 AH. His chains of transmission appear in virtually every chapter of the major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Ibnu Syihab az-Zuhri adalah tabiin terkemuka dan salah satu perawi hadith paling penting dalam sejarah Islam. Ia berperan besar dalam kodifikasi hadith.

bio_short_en: Imran ibn Husain (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) known for his deep piety, asceticism, and spiritual experiences. He narrated numerous hadiths and was greatly respected by the companions and Tabi'in for his knowledge of prophetic traditions and his closeness to the Prophet. He settled in Basra and became an important religious teacher in that region. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections and he is regarded as a reliable and distinguished narrator.
bio_en: Imran ibn Husayn al-Khuza'i (RA), whose kunya was Abu Nujaid, was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the tribe of Khuza'a. He embraced Islam in 7 AH when the Prophet ﷺ sent him and some others to call his tribe to Islam. He was known for his extraordinary piety, his close relationship with the angels — who would give him salams until he stopped using cauterization as a medical treatment (apparently at the angels' disapproval of it) — and his patience in severe illness. He narrated approximately 180 hadiths. He settled in Basra and became one of the leading religious authorities there, known as the most learned person in Basra during his time. His student Mutarrif ibn Abdullah transmitted many of his narrations. He was afflicted with a painful illness for thirty years but never complained and maintained his gratitude to Allah until the end. He passed away in Basra around 52–53 AH.
bio_short_id: Imran bin Husain adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan dikenal atas kedudukannya yang tinggi. Para malaikat pun konon turut hadir dalam majelisnya.

bio_short_en: Abu Qatada al-Ansari (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) from Medina who participated in many military expeditions and was known for his bravery and devotion. He narrated numerous hadiths covering various aspects of Islamic practice, including prayer, ritual purity, and the conduct of warfare. He was particularly active in transmitting prophetic knowledge after the Prophet's death, and his narrations are found in the major hadith collections where he is regarded as a reliable and sincere narrator.
bio_en: Abu Qatada al-Ansari (RA), whose full name was al-Harith ibn Rib'i al-Ansari al-Khazraji, was from the Ansar of Medina. He was one of the most active participants in military expeditions, joining the Prophet ﷺ in battles and raids. He was known for his bravery in combat and was reportedly called the 'Horseman of Allah' (Faris Allah) for his cavalry skill. He participated in major battles including Uhud, Khandaq, Khaybar, and Hunayn. He narrated approximately 170 hadiths on prayer, ritual purity, military conduct, and prophetic supplication. He was particularly known for narrations describing the Prophet's prayer and its details, including the famous description of the Prophet carrying his granddaughter Umama during prayer. He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death, though some accounts say he remained in Medina. He passed away around 54 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Qatadah al-Anshari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal sebagai "Penunggang Kuda Rasulullah." Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, puasa, dan jihad.

bio_short_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) was the second Caliph of Islam, a companion of exceptional rank, and a close advisor to the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). His conversion to Islam significantly strengthened the early Muslim community, and his caliphate became synonymous with justice, piety, and the expansion of the Islamic state. He narrated hadiths that scholars across all generations have relied upon, and his understanding of the Sunnah and his juristic opinions are foundational to classical Islamic legal thought and governance.
bio_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) — this entry under 'Umar ibn al-Khattab' with a person_slug refers to the same second Caliph of Islam as entries 29 and 31, appearing in the Abu Dawud collection under this spelling. His full biography is documented under entry 29. He was among the greatest companions, a narrator of approximately 537 hadiths, and the Caliph who oversaw the largest expansion of the Islamic state. He was martyred in 23 AH while leading the Fajr prayer in the mosque of Medina.
bio_short_id: Umar bin al-Khattab adalah Khalifah kedua dalam Islam dan salah satu Sahabat paling berpengaruh. Ia meriwayatkan ratusan hadith dan memerintah selama 10 tahun dengan keadilan yang diakui.

bio_short_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was a Companion from the Ansar of Madinah who participated in eighteen military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ. Initially too young to join the Battle of Badr, he later participated in all major campaigns. He narrated approximately 305 hadiths and settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death, where he became a key transmitter of prophetic traditions in Iraq. His reports cover prayer, fasting, funeral rites, and other pillars of Islamic practice.
bio_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) — this entry appears as 'Al-Bara bin Azib' in the data, referring to the same Ansari companion whose full biography is under entry 14. He participated in eighteen military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ after being initially turned away from Badr for being too young. He narrated approximately 305 hadiths and settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death, becoming an important transmitter of the Sunnah in Iraq. His narrations cover prayer, fasting, funeral rites, and eyewitness accounts from the expeditions. He passed away around 72 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Bara' bin Azib adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang peperangan dan ibadah. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) known for his long life and his critical role in transmitting prophetic traditions to the Tabi'in. He was reportedly among the last surviving companions in Medina, which made him an essential direct link for scholars seeking authentic narrations from the prophetic era. His reports cover the Prophet's worship, character, and sayings, and are found throughout the major hadith collections where he is regarded as trustworthy.
bio_en: Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (RA) — this entry 'Sahl' refers to the same Ansari companion whose biography is under entry 13. He was one of the last surviving companions in Medina, living to approximately 88–91 AH, which made him an essential direct link for Tabi'i scholars seeking authentic narrations from the prophetic era. He narrated approximately 188 hadiths on the Prophet's conduct, character, and worship. His longevity and residence in Medina made him a permanent resource for the scholarly community long after most companions had passed away.
bio_short_id: Sahl bin Sa'd adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang kehidupan di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) from the Ansar of Medina and one of the most prolific narrators among the companions, transmitting over 1,500 hadiths. He accompanied the Prophet on many expeditions and lived long enough to transmit prophetic knowledge to major Tabi'i scholars. His narrations are found throughout the major hadith collections and cover a wide range of religious topics including worship, transactions, and detailed accounts from the Prophet's life.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah (RA) — this entry 'Jabir bin Abdullah' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion as entries 6 and 25, appearing primarily in Abu Dawud and Tirmidhi. His full biography is under entry 6. He was from the Ansar of Medina, participated in nineteen expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ, and narrated over 1,540 hadiths. He was one of the last senior companions to survive in Medina and continued teaching until late in his life. He passed away around 78 AH.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin Abdullah adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.500 hadith. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ dan menjadi sumber penting bagi ulama tabiin di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Urwa ibn al-Zubayr was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina, the son of the companion al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA), and a nephew of Aisha (RA). He served as one of the primary transmitters of Aisha's narrations and is counted among the Seven Jurists of Medina, a group whose scholarly opinions shaped the foundations of early Islamic jurisprudence. His narrations appear extensively in all major hadith collections and he is regarded as one of the most reliable and authoritative Tabi'i transmitters.
bio_en: Urwa ibn al-Zubayr — this entry 'Urwa bin Az-Zubair' is an alternate transliteration of the same Tabi'i scholar whose full biography appears under entry 18. He was the son of the companion al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA), the nephew of Aisha (RA), and one of the Seven Jurists of Medina. He was the primary transmitter of Aisha's narrations to the next generation and a cornerstone scholar of the Medinan tradition. His students included al-Zuhri and Hisham ibn Urwa. He passed away around 93–94 AH.
bio_short_id: Urwa bin az-Zubair adalah tabiin terkemuka dan keponakan Aisyah RA. Ia adalah salah satu dari tujuh fuqaha Madinah dan perawi utama hadith dari Aisyah.

bio_short_en: Anas ibn Malik (RA) was the personal servant of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ for ten years, beginning when he was ten years old when his mother brought him to serve the Prophet in Madinah. He is one of the most prolific narrators of hadith, transmitting over 2,200 traditions. The Prophet ﷺ made supplication for him: 'O Allah, give him wealth and children and bless him therein.' He lived to approximately 93 AH, becoming the last surviving major Companion in Basra, where he narrated to thousands of students. His long life and personal proximity to the Prophet made him an irreplaceable source of prophetic knowledge.
bio_en: Anas ibn Malik (RA) — this entry 'Anas (RA)' represents the same companion appearing in the Bulugh al-Maram collection under a shortened form of his name. His full biography is documented under entry 3. He served the Prophet ﷺ for ten years from age ten, narrated over 2,286 hadiths, and lived to approximately 93 AH — becoming the last surviving major companion in Basra. His narrations in Bulugh al-Maram cover a wide range of legal topics drawn from his decade of intimate service to the Prophet ﷺ.
bio_short_id: Anas (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 24 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who embraced Islam in Medina and participated in numerous military campaigns in the early years of Islam. He narrated a significant number of hadiths on various topics including prayer, the funeral rites, and events from the Prophet's life and character. He is considered a reliable narrator whose transmissions are well-attested in the major hadith collections, and he continued to transmit prophetic knowledge well into the era of the Tabi'in.
bio_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) — this entry 'Al-Bara'' is yet another transliteration variant of the same Ansari companion documented fully under entry 14. He participated in eighteen expeditions, narrated approximately 305 hadiths, and settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death. His narrations cover prayer, descriptions of the Prophet, and firsthand accounts of the battles. He passed away around 72 AH in Kufa.
bio_short_id: Al-Bara' bin Azib adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang peperangan dan ibadah.

bio_short_en: Adi ibn Hatim al-Ta'i (RA) was initially a Christian Arab from the tribe of Tayy before embracing Islam around 9 AH. He was the son of the legendary Arab chieftain Hatim al-Ta'i, famous for his generosity. After accepting Islam, Adi ibn Hatim became a devoted Companion and narrated approximately 66 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ. He participated in the early Islamic conquests and lived to approximately 68 AH, settling in Kufa. He was known for his loyalty to Ali (RA) during the period of civil strife and remained a respected elder figure in Iraq.
bio_en: Adi ibn Hatim al-Ta'i (RA) was from the tribe of Tayy in northern Arabia and was the son of Hatim al-Ta'i, the legendary figure of Arab generosity whose hospitality was proverbial throughout the ancient Arabian world. Adi himself was initially a Christian and came to Medina around 9 AH to meet the Prophet ﷺ, intending to examine his claims. His conversation with the Prophet ﷺ was so persuasive that he embraced Islam on the spot. The Prophet ﷺ was particularly pleased with his conversion, saying it signified that the whole tribe of Tayy would follow. Adi became a devoted companion and participated in the early Islamic conquests. He narrated approximately 66 hadiths covering worship, permissible and impermissible food, and Islamic ethics. He was loyal to Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) during the civil strife and participated on his side. He settled in Kufa and became a respected elder figure in Iraq. He passed away around 67–68 AH at an old age.
bio_short_id: Adi bin Hatim adalah putra Hatim ath-Tha'i yang masuk Islam. Ia meriwayatkan hadith-hadith tentang hukum berburu dan makan.

bio_short_en: Muadh ibn Jabal (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) singled out as the most knowledgeable of the companions in matters of the lawful and the unlawful. The Prophet sent him to Yemen as a teacher and judge, demonstrating great confidence in his scholarship and character. He narrated hadiths on a wide range of topics and is regarded as one of the most learned and reliable companions in both religious knowledge and practical judgment. His narrations are found in all major hadith collections.
bio_en: Muadh ibn Jabal al-Ansari (RA) was from the tribe of Khazraj in Medina and is counted among the most learned companions in matters of religious law. The Prophet ﷺ singled him out with the statement: 'The most knowledgeable of my community regarding the halal and haram is Muadh ibn Jabal.' He embraced Islam at a young age and was present at the second pledge of Aqabah. He accompanied the Prophet ﷺ in all his major campaigns and was one of the companions who compiled the Quran during the Prophet's lifetime. The Prophet ﷺ sent him to Yemen as a teacher, judge, and governor — one of the greatest expressions of prophetic confidence in a single companion. His famous dialogue with the Prophet on judicial reasoning (ijtihad) is a foundational text in the theory of Islamic jurisprudence. He narrated approximately 157 hadiths. He died in Syria during the plague of Amwas around 17–18 AH at approximately 33 years of age — one of the great early losses of the companion generation.
bio_short_id: Muadz bin Jabal adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal sebagai ahli halal dan haram. Nabi mengutusnya ke Yaman sebagai guru dan hakim, dan ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang ibadah dan hukum.

bio_short_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) was the cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), raised in his household from a young age and among the very first to accept Islam. He served as the fourth Caliph of Islam and was renowned for his courage, deep piety, and vast Islamic scholarship. He narrated numerous hadiths and his scholarly legacy has deeply influenced Islamic jurisprudence and spirituality across all subsequent generations. His narrations are considered highly authoritative in all major schools of Islamic thought.
bio_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) — this entry 'Ali bin Abi Talib' is the same companion as entry 9, appearing in Tirmidhi and Bukhari under a different transliteration. He was the cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet ﷺ, raised in the Prophet's household, and among the very first to embrace Islam. He was the fourth Caliph of Islam, renowned for his courage, scholarship, and deep piety. His full biography is documented under entry 9. He narrated approximately 536 hadiths and was martyred in Kufa in 40 AH.
bio_short_id: Ali bin Abi Thalib adalah sepupu dan menantu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, serta Khalifah keempat dalam Islam. Ia adalah salah satu ulama terbesar di kalangan Sahabat dan meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang ibadah dan hukum Islam.

bio_short_en: Usama ibn Zaid (RA) was the son of Zaid ibn Haritha (RA), the freed companion, and was deeply beloved by the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), who called him 'my beloved, son of my beloved.' He was appointed by the Prophet to lead a military expedition at a young age, demonstrating the Prophet's confidence in him. He narrated several important hadiths and is cited in the major hadith collections. His unique closeness to the Prophet's household gave his narrations a particular significance and authority.
bio_en: Usama ibn Zaid ibn Haritha (RA) was the son of Zaid ibn Haritha — the Prophet's freed slave and adopted son before the prohibition of adoption in the legal sense — and was known as Hibb al-Nabi ibn Hibbihi (the beloved of the Prophet, son of his beloved). He was born in Mecca and grew up in the Prophet's household alongside al-Hasan and al-Husayn. The Prophet ﷺ showed him exceptional love and affection from childhood, often embracing him together with al-Hasan on his knee. At age seventeen or eighteen, the Prophet ﷺ appointed Usama as the commander of an army that was being assembled to campaign in the Levant — an appointment that provoked some murmuring due to his young age, which the Prophet ﷺ firmly defended. This expedition was completed under Abu Bakr's caliphate after the Prophet's death. He narrated approximately 128 hadiths. After the Prophet's death he retired to al-Jurf near Medina and refused to participate in the civil wars. He passed away around 54–59 AH.
bio_short_id: Usamah bin Zaid adalah anak angkat Zaid bin Haritsah dan dicintai oleh Nabi ﷺ. Ia menjadi panglima termuda dalam sejarah Islam dan meriwayatkan banyak hadith.

bio_short_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who embraced Islam in his youth and participated in many military expeditions in the early history of Islam. He narrated numerous hadiths on a wide range of topics including prayer, the funeral rites, descriptions of the Prophet, and events from his life. He lived to an old age and continued transmitting prophetic knowledge to the Tabi'in. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections and he is regarded as a reliable and sincere narrator.
bio_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) — this entry 'Al-Bara ibn Azib' is yet another transliteration of the same Ansari companion documented under entry 14, here appearing in the Abu Dawud collection. He was from the Ansar, participated in eighteen expeditions, narrated approximately 305 hadiths, and settled in Kufa. All details match the primary biography under entry 14. He passed away around 72 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Bara' bin Azib adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang peperangan dan ibadah. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ dan dikenal atas kesalehannya.

bio_short_en: Buraydah ibn al-Hasib al-Aslami (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who led his entire tribe to embrace Islam when he himself converted. He participated in many military expeditions and later settled in Khurasan, where he became an important transmitter of prophetic knowledge in that region. He narrated numerous hadiths covering many aspects of Islamic worship and conduct. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections and he is considered a reliable and trustworthy narrator.
bio_en: Buraydah ibn al-Hasib al-Aslami (RA) was from the tribe of Aslam near Medina. When the Prophet ﷺ was traveling to Medina after the Hijra, Buraydah encountered him along the way. The encounter was so compelling that Buraydah and eighty members of his tribe embraced Islam on the spot. This mass conversion was a significant early success for Islam in the region around Medina. He participated in many military expeditions, including those to Khaybar and Mecca. After the Prophet's death he chose not to remain in Medina or the Hijaz but migrated eastward, eventually settling in Marw (Merv) in Khorasan — one of the farthest companions to settle from the Arabian heartland. He narrated approximately 165 hadiths on a range of topics including prayer, warfare, and the administration of justice. His narrations helped establish the Sunnah in the eastern provinces. He passed away in Khorasan around 62–63 AH.
bio_short_id: Buraidah bin al-Hushaib adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, puasa, dan jihad. Ia tinggal di Marw pada masa tuanya.

bio_short_en: Samurah ibn Jundub (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who narrated a number of prophetic traditions and later served as a governor of Basra. He is known in hadith literature for transmitting several narrations related to Quranic recitation, dream interpretation, and religious observances. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections. Despite some scholarly discussion about a few of his transmissions, he is generally regarded as an accepted narrator from among the companions.
bio_en: Samurah ibn Jundub al-Fazari (RA) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who embraced Islam as a young man. There is a well-known account that as a youth he was refused permission by the Prophet ﷺ to join the army because of his young age, but he was eventually permitted after demonstrating his strength. He narrated approximately 128 hadiths covering prayer, commercial dealings, and various religious observances. One of the most discussed of his narrations is his report on the famous 'dream vision' of the Prophet in which the Prophet saw various scenes of the afterlife — this narration is particularly detailed and regularly cited in chapters on eschatology. He later served as governor of Basra under Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan and then under Yazid ibn Muawiya, and his tenure was considered harsh by some historical sources. He passed away around 58–59 AH.
bio_short_id: Samurah bin Jundub adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith. Ia menjabat sebagai gubernur Basra dan dikenal atas sikapnya yang keras dalam menegakkan hukum.

bio_short_en: Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (RA), whose full name was Uqba ibn Amr al-Ansari al-Badri, was from the Ansar of Madinah and was present at the Second Pledge of Aqabah. He participated in the Battle of Badr — a distinction that earned him the epithet 'al-Badri.' He narrated approximately 103 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ covering prayer, charity, the Day of Judgment, and the Prophet's character. He later settled in Kufa where he served as an important early authority on hadith and Islamic rulings. He died during the caliphate of Muawiya.
bio_en: Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (RA), whose full name was Uqba ibn Amr ibn Tha'labah al-Badri al-Ansari, was from the Ansar of Medina and participated in the Second Pledge of Aqabah, pledging his allegiance to the Prophet ﷺ before the Hijra. He earned the epithet 'al-Badri' because he lived near the well of Badr — though some scholars differ on whether he participated in the battle itself. He narrated approximately 103 hadiths covering prayer, the nature of faith, the Day of Judgment, and the Prophet's character. Among his most famous narrations is the hadith that connects proper performance of prayer with charity, and another warning about innovation in worship.
