He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death and served as an important religious authority there. After Hudhaifa ibn al-Yaman's death he served briefly as governor of Kufa. He passed away during the caliphate of Muawiya, around 40–42 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Mas'ud al-Anshari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting tentang shalat dan fikih. Ia termasuk di antara orang-orang yang hadir di Bai'at Aqabah.

bio_short_en: This narrator refers to Urwa ibn al-Zubayr, the father of Hisham ibn Urwa, as identified in certain hadith chains where Hisham narrates on his father's authority. Urwa was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina, a nephew of Aisha (RA), and one of the Seven Jurists of Medina. He was an authoritative and highly reliable transmitter of prophetic narrations who played a central role in preserving the knowledge of the Prophet's household. His narrations transmitted through his son Hisham form an important and widely attested chain found throughout the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Urwa ibn al-Zubayr — this entry 'Hisham's father' refers to Urwa ibn al-Zubayr, identified in certain hadith chains as the father of Hisham ibn Urwa. The same Tabi'i scholar is documented fully under entry 18. He was one of the Seven Jurists of Medina, the nephew of Aisha (RA), and the primary transmitter of her narrations. This chain — Hisham from his father Urwa from Aisha — is among the most frequently cited and reliable chains in the major collections. He passed away around 93–94 AH.
bio_short_id: Hisham's father adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 21 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Amr ibn Shu'ayb narrated on the authority of his father Shu'ayb ibn Muhammad, who narrated from his grandfather Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (RA), a companion of the Prophet (peace be upon him). This chain — Amr from his father from his grandfather — is a recognized and frequently discussed isnad in hadith criticism, accepted by many scholars as reliable while others examined its continuity carefully. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections and contain important legal rulings and prophetic guidance.
bio_en: Amr ibn Shu'ayb narrated on the authority of his father Shu'ayb ibn Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As, who narrated from his grandfather Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (RA), the companion of the Prophet ﷺ. This chain — known in hadith terminology as 'Amr from his father from his grandfather' — is one of the most discussed chains in the science of hadith criticism. The majority of leading scholars including Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Yahya ibn Ma'in, and al-Tirmidhi accepted it as reliable. The narrations through this chain appear in Abu Dawud's Sunan and contain important legal rulings on inheritance, family law, trade, and proper conduct. The chain's reliability rests ultimately on the companion Abdullah ibn Amr, who was one of the first systematic recorders of hadith and one of the most prolific companion narrators.
bio_short_id: 'Amr b. Suh'aib adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 20 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Mujahid ibn Jabr was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the most important authorities on Quranic tafsir. He was a devoted student of Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) and reportedly recited the entire Quran to him multiple times, pausing at each verse to ask about its meaning and context. His tafsir narrations are among the most foundational references in classical Quranic scholarship. He also narrated hadiths from companions and is considered a reliable and authoritative transmitter in the chains of hadith.
bio_en: Mujahid ibn Jabr al-Makki was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the most important authorities on Quranic exegesis in Islamic history. Born around 21 AH, he was a mawla (freed client) of the Makhzum clan. His greatest teacher was Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), from whom he learned the entire tafsir of the Quran in the most thorough manner known to any student: he reportedly recited the entire Quran to Ibn Abbas three times, stopping at every single verse to ask about its meaning, context, occasion of revelation, and proper interpretation. This meticulous training made him the preeminent authority on tafsir after Ibn Abbas. His tafsir narrations are the primary source for all subsequent classical commentators, including al-Tabari. He also transmitted hadiths from Ibn Umar, Abu Huraira, and other companions. He was known for his personal piety and the breadth of his Islamic knowledge. He passed away in Mecca around 103–104 AH.
bio_short_id: Mujahid bin Jabr adalah tabiin terkemuka dan murid utama Abdullah bin Abbas. Ia adalah salah satu mufassir (ahli tafsir) paling terpercaya dari generasi tabiin.

bio_short_en: Qatada ibn Di'ama was a prominent Tabi'i scholar of Basra renowned for his extraordinary memory and expertise in Quranic exegesis and hadith. He narrated from many companions and is considered a major figure in the transmission of both tafsir and prophetic traditions. Despite being blind from birth, he excelled in scholarship and memorization beyond most of his peers. He is regarded as one of the most knowledgeable and reliable narrators of his generation and his narrations appear extensively in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Qatada ibn Di'ama al-Sadusi al-Basri was a prominent Tabi'i scholar of Basra who was born blind. Despite this, he possessed one of the most remarkable memories in the history of hadith scholarship: it is reported that he could memorize a text after hearing it just once, and he never forgot anything he had heard. He narrated from numerous companions including Anas ibn Malik, Sahl ibn Sa'd, and Abdullah ibn Sarjas, and was a major student of al-Hasan al-Basri and Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab. He was an authority in hadith, tafsir, and Arabic language. Some scholars noted that he held Qadarite views on free will, which led a small number of critics to be cautious about him, but the majority regarded him as fully reliable in hadith. He passed away around 117–118 AH. His narrations appear extensively in all six major collections.
bio_short_id: Qatadah bin Di'amah adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Basra yang dikenal atas hafalan dan penguasaannya dalam ilmu Al-Quran, tafsir, dan hadith.

bio_short_en: Jarir ibn Abdullah al-Bajali (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who embraced Islam and was known for his noble character and dignified presence. The Prophet reportedly remarked that Jarir resembled the prophets of the Bani Isra'il. He narrated several hadiths preserved in the major collections, covering topics relating to faith, the conduct of a Muslim, and various aspects of Islamic practice. He was respected by the companions and Tabi'in for his sincerity and his commitment to the Sunnah.
bio_en: Jarir ibn Abdullah al-Bajali (RA) was from the tribe of Bajila in Yemen and was known for his exceptionally handsome appearance — the companions described him as a man of striking dignity and beauty. The Prophet ﷺ reportedly said that his appearance reminded him of the prophets of the Children of Israel. He embraced Islam in 10 AH, shortly before the Prophet's death, and the Prophet ﷺ assigned him specific missions including the destruction of the idol Dhu al-Khalasa in Yemen — which Jarir accomplished successfully. He narrated approximately 116 hadiths. He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death. He was present at some of the events of the civil strife but tried to maintain neutrality. He passed away around 51–54 AH, with some accounts placing him in al-Sarat in Yemen at his death.
bio_short_id: Jarir bin Abdullah al-Bajali adalah Sahabat Nabi dari kabilah Bajilah. Ia masuk Islam dan dikirim Nabi ﷺ untuk menghancurkan berhala Dzul Khalasah. Ia meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting.

bio_short_en: Qais ibn Abi Hazim was a distinguished Tabi'i scholar of Kufa who narrated from a remarkable number of senior companions of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), including Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, and many others. He is considered one of the most prolific Tabi'i transmitters from the first generation of companions and is regarded as highly reliable by the scholars of hadith. His narrations appear throughout the major hadith collections and represent an important bridge between the companions and later scholarship.
bio_en: Qais ibn Abi Hazim al-Bajali al-Ahmasi was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Kufa who lived through the era of the Prophet ﷺ but was too young to have been a companion. He migrated to Medina but arrived after the Prophet's death. Despite this, he narrated from an extraordinary range of senior companions — reportedly from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, Umar ibn al-Khattab, Uthman ibn Affan, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Talha, al-Zubayr, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and many others — making him the Tabi'i who narrated from the greatest number of the ten promised Paradise. He is considered by hadith critics as fully reliable (thiqah) and one of the most important transmission links between the first generation and those that followed. His narrations appear extensively in all major collections. He passed away around 84–98 AH.
bio_short_id: Qais bin Abi Hazim adalah tabiin terkemuka yang bertemu banyak Sahabat Nabi. Ia meriwayatkan hadith dari Abu Bakr, Umar, Utsman, Ali, dan Sahabat terkemuka lainnya.

bio_short_en: Zaid ibn Thabit (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who served as one of his primary scribes, recording Quranic revelation and official correspondence. He was entrusted with the monumental task of compiling the Quran into a single volume during the caliphate of Abu Bakr (RA) and later oversaw the preparation of the standardized Uthmanic mushaf. He narrated numerous hadiths and was a leading scholarly authority in Medina on matters of Quranic science, recitation, and Islamic law.
bio_en: Zaid ibn Thabit al-Ansari (RA) was from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and was one of the principal scribes of the Quranic revelation. He learned to write at a young age and was brought to the Prophet ﷺ when he was about eleven years old. The Prophet ﷺ also instructed him to learn Hebrew and Syriac to assist in correspondence with Jewish and Christian communities. When the compilation of the Quran was undertaken during Abu Bakr's caliphate after the Battle of Yamama — at Umar's urging — it was Zaid who was entrusted with the monumental task of collecting all written fragments and verified oral recitations into a single codex. He later oversaw the preparation of the standardized Uthmanic mushaf during Uthman's caliphate. He narrated approximately 92 hadiths and was the foremost authority in Medina on Quranic recitation, inheritance law, and the rules of obligatory shares (fara'id). He passed away in Medina around 45–55 AH.
bio_short_id: Zaid bin Tsabit adalah sekretaris wahyu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan ahli ilmu waris. Ia memimpin tim penyusunan mushaf Al-Quran pada masa Khalifah Abu Bakr dan menyempurnakannya pada masa Khalifah Utsman.

bio_short_en: Abu Sa'eed al-Khudri (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) from the Ansar of Medina who narrated hundreds of hadiths. He is particularly known for narrations on eschatology, the signs of the Last Day, and matters of purification and prayer. His dedication to preserving and transmitting the Sunnah made him one of the most cited companions in the hadith literature, and his narrations are found extensively in all major collections where they are regarded as highly authentic.
bio_en: Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri (RA) — this entry in the Tirmidhi collection refers once again to Sa'd ibn Malik al-Khudri, the prolific Ansari companion documented under entries 11, 15, and 17. He is one of the Mukthirun, with over 1,170 narrations. He was particularly known for his narrations on eschatology and jurisprudence. His various transliterations (Abu Sa'id, Abu Sa'eed, AbuSa'id, Abu Sa'id al-Khudri) all refer to the same companion. He passed away around 74 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir.

bio_short_en: Abu Hurayrah (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and the single most prolific narrator of hadith, with over 5,000 narrations attributed to him. He devoted himself entirely to learning from the Prophet, sacrificing comfort and worldly pursuits to remain in his company. After the Prophet's death, he continued to teach and transmit this knowledge for decades, becoming an essential and irreplaceable source for Islamic tradition. His narrations are foundational across all major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abu Hurayrah (RA) — this entry is an alternate transliteration of the same most-prolific companion narrator documented fully under entry 1. He is Abu Huraira (RA), the Yemeni companion who devoted himself entirely to the Prophet's company, narrated over 5,374 hadiths, and passed away around 57–59 AH. This entry represents his narrations in the Abu Dawud Sunan under an alternate spelling of his kunya.
bio_short_id: Abu Hurairah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang paling banyak meriwayatkan hadith, dengan lebih dari 5.000 hadith. Ia masuk Islam pada tahun 7 H dan selalu menyertai Nabi, mengutamakan ilmu di atas kepentingan duniawi.

bio_short_en: Umm Salamah (RA) was a wife of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She was among the earliest Muslims and emigrated twice — first to Abyssinia and then to Medina — enduring hardship for her faith. Known for her wisdom and intelligence, she was frequently consulted on religious matters. She narrated numerous hadiths and continued to be a scholarly authority after the Prophet's death. Her narrations are cited in all major hadith collections and are particularly valued on matters of worship and household conduct.
bio_en: Umm Salamah (RA) — this entry represents the same Mother of the Believers documented under entries 24 and 28, appearing in Tirmidhi and Abu Dawud under the spelling 'Umm Salamah.' Hind bint Abi Umayya was among the earliest Muslims, emigrated twice for the sake of her faith, and became a wife of the Prophet ﷺ after her first husband's death. She was wise, courageous, and deeply learned, narrating approximately 378 hadiths. She outlived all the other Mothers of the Believers, passing away around 62–63 AH.
bio_short_id: Ummu Salamah adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu Ummahatul Mukminin. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith.

bio_short_en: Amr ibn Shu'ayb narrated from his father Shu'ayb ibn Muhammad, who narrated from his grandfather Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (RA), a companion of the Prophet (peace be upon him) and one of the first to systematically write down hadith. This three-generation chain is accepted by many hadith scholars as reliable, though it has been the subject of scholarly analysis regarding its continuity. Narrations through this chain appear in the major hadith collections and contain valuable religious rulings and prophetic guidance.
bio_en: Amr ibn Shu'ayb — this entry 'Amr bin Shu'aib' is the same narrator documented under entry 53, appearing in Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, and Nasai. He transmitted on the authority of his father, who narrated from his grandfather Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (RA). This chain — Amr, from his father, from his grandfather — is one of the most well-known and examined chains in hadith literature. The narrations through this chain are accepted as reliable by the majority of hadith scholars and contain important legal rulings across a wide range of topics.
bio_short_id: Amr bin Syu'aib meriwayatkan hadith dari ayahnya dari kakeknya (Abdullah bin Amr), yang merupakan sanad yang banyak digunakan dalam kitab-kitab hadith.

name_en: Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid
bio_short_en: This entry refers to narrations transmitted jointly or in parallel by two companions: Abu Huraira (RA), the most prolific hadith narrator in Islamic history, and Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (RA), an Ansari companion known for his piety, generosity, and devotion. Both were companions of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), and their joint narrations typically appear in the context of a shared experience or their both hearing the same prophetic statement. Their combined transmission adds strength to the authenticity of the narrations they share.
bio_en: Abu Huraira and Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (RA) — this entry refers to narrations jointly transmitted by two companions: Abu Huraira (RA), the most prolific narrator in hadith history, and Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (RA), a companion from the tribe of Juhayna who participated in the Battle of Hudaybiyyah and was known for his piety, generosity, and devotion to worship. Joint narrations from two companions typically appear in instances where both companions heard the same prophetic statement together, or where their narrations of the same event are presented in combination. These paired narrations carry enhanced authority by virtue of having two independent companion witnesses. Their joint narrations in Bukhari are cited in chapters on found property, purification, and legal rulings.
bio_short_id: Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 18 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Burda ibn Abi Musa al-Ashari was a Tabi'i scholar and the son of the companion Abu Musa al-Ashari (RA). He narrated hadiths from his father and from other companions, and is regarded as a reliable transmitter. He served as a judge in Kufa and was respected in the community for his knowledge, integrity, and sound legal judgment. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections and provide an important link in the chains of transmission from the companions to subsequent generations of scholars.
bio_en: Abu Burda ibn Abi Musa al-Ashari was a Tabi'i scholar and the son of the companion Abu Musa al-Ashari (RA). He grew up hearing hadith from his father and from other companions, and is regarded as a reliable and trustworthy transmitter. He served as a judge (qadi) in Kufa and was known for his knowledge of Islamic law and his integrity in judicial matters. Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz had a high regard for him. He transmitted his father's narrations as well as those of other companions and became an important link between Abu Musa's knowledge and the Tabi'i generation. He also narrated from Ibn Umar (RA). His narrations appear in all the major hadith collections and he is considered a reliable narrator in the science of hadith criticism. He passed away around 103–104 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Burda adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 18 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Maimuna bint al-Harith (RA) was a wife of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She was known for her piety, generosity, and deep devotion to worship. She narrated a number of hadiths relating to the Prophet's personal conduct, prayer, and purification, offering valuable insight into his private life and religious practice. She was respected among the companions and Tabi'in as a reliable narrator and an authoritative source of knowledge about the Prophet's household.
bio_en: Maimuna bint al-Harith al-Hilaliyya (RA) was the last woman to marry the Prophet ﷺ, with the marriage taking place in 7 AH during the Umrat al-Qada. She was a widow and is described in Islamic sources as a woman of great piety, generosity, and devotion. Her sister Umm al-Fadl was the wife of al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib, making Maimuna a sister-in-law of the Prophet's uncle. The Prophet ﷺ was particularly fond of her and she was known for her constant prayers and generosity to the poor. She narrated approximately 76 hadiths on the Prophet's prayer, purification, personal conduct, and household life. Her narrations provide unique windows into the Prophet's private domestic practices. She passed away in 51 AH in the same location — Sarif, near Mecca — where she had been married, reportedly having requested to die there.
bio_short_id: Maimunah binti al-Harits adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan termasuk Ummahatul Mukminin. Ia meriwayatkan beberapa hadith dari Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who was among the last surviving companions in Medina. His extraordinary longevity allowed him to transmit prophetic knowledge directly to Tabi'i scholars who had no personal experience of the Prophet's era, making him an invaluable bridge to the prophetic tradition. He narrated hadiths on topics ranging from worship to the Prophet's character and miracles, and his narrations are found in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (RA) — this entry 'Sahl bin Sa'd As-Sa'idi' is the same companion documented under entries 13 and 39, appearing in Bukhari and Tirmidhi under a more complete spelling of his name. He was an Ansari companion of the Prophet ﷺ who lived to an exceptional old age — approximately 88–91 AH — becoming one of the last surviving companions in Medina. His longevity made him an invaluable direct link to the prophetic era for Tabi'i scholars. He narrated approximately 188 hadiths on the Prophet's worship, character, and miraculous acts.
bio_short_id: Sahl bin Sa'd al-Sa'idi adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang kehidupan di Madinah. Ia hidup hingga usia sangat tua.

bio_short_en: This refers to Abdullah ibn Umar (RA), identified as the father of Salim in certain isnad references. Abdullah ibn Umar was one of the most prolific and scrupulous narrator-companions, known for his extreme care in adhering to the Prophet's Sunnah in every aspect of his life. His narrations transmitted through his son Salim represent one of the most reliable and well-documented chains in hadith literature, appearing throughout the major collections and carrying great weight among scholars of the hadith sciences.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Umar (RA) — this entry 'Salim's father' refers to Ibn Umar as identified through the chain via his son Salim. Abdullah ibn Umar's full biography appears under entry 4. He is one of the most prolific companion narrators with over 2,630 hadiths, was scrupulous in following the Sunnah in every detail, and passed away around 73–74 AH. Chains transmitted through his son Salim — 'Salim from his father Ibn Umar' — are among the most reliable and frequently cited in all major collections.
bio_short_id: Salim's father adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 17 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (RA) was one of the earliest Muslims and one of the ten companions given glad tidings of Paradise by the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). He was a military leader of great distinction who commanded the Muslim forces at the pivotal Battle of al-Qadisiyyah, leading to the conquest of Persia. He narrated numerous hadiths and is cited across the major collections. His early acceptance of Islam and his close relationship with the Prophet made him a highly authoritative and respected narrator.
bio_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (RA) — this entry 'Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas' is the same companion documented under entry 32, appearing in Bukhari, Tirmidhi, and Nasai. He was one of the ten companions promised Paradise, the commanding general at al-Qadisiyyah, and an early Muslim from Quraysh. He narrated approximately 270 hadiths and passed away around 55–58 AH. His narrations in this subset of the collections include matters of prayer, medicine, and his personal proximity to the Prophet ﷺ.
bio_short_id: Sa'd bin Abi Waqqash adalah salah satu dari sepuluh Sahabat yang dijamin masuk surga. Ia adalah pahlawan penaklukan Persia dan meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting.

bio_short_en: Abu Juhaifa Wahb ibn Abdullah (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who narrated several valuable hadiths. He is particularly well known for a narration describing the physical appearance of the Prophet in vivid detail, which is among the most widely cited descriptions in the hadith literature. He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death and became an important transmitter of prophetic knowledge to the scholars there. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections and are considered reliable.
bio_en: Abu Juhaifa Wahb ibn Abdullah al-Suwa'i (RA) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who was young enough that he was known as a junior companion. Despite his young age he is particularly celebrated for a narration preserved in Bukhari that gives a detailed description of the Prophet Muhammad's ﷺ physical appearance — his face likened to the sun or moon in beauty, his hair, his complexion, and his garments. This narration is one of the most cited descriptions of the Prophet's appearance in the hadith literature. He narrated approximately 46 hadiths. He also transmitted the famous hadith in which Ibn Masud is described as the person most similar to the Prophet in conduct. He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death and became a reliable transmitter for the Tabi'in there. He passed away around 74 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Juhaifa adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 17 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Salim ibn Abdullah ibn Umar was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and the son of the companion Abdullah ibn Umar (RA). He was recognized as one of the Seven Jurists of Medina, a select group whose scholarly depth and influence shaped the foundations of early Islamic jurisprudence. He transmitted many narrations from his father, one of the most scrupulous companion narrators, and is himself regarded as highly reliable. His narrations appear extensively in the major hadith collections and he is counted among the most authoritative Tabi'i scholars.
bio_en: Salim ibn Abdullah ibn Umar was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina and the son of the companion Abdullah ibn Umar (RA). He is counted among the Seven Jurists of Medina and was considered by his contemporaries as one of the most knowledgeable and reliable scholars of the Medinan tradition. He was the principal transmitter of his father's narrations and his chain — Salim from his father Ibn Umar — is one of the most consistently cited and trusted chains in all of hadith literature. Imam Malik relied heavily on his narrations in al-Muwatta. He was known for his personal piety, his refusal to accept gifts from rulers, and his strict adherence to his father's model of the Sunnah. He was particularly knowledgeable on matters of pilgrimage, prayer, and family law. He passed away in Medina around 106 AH.
bio_short_id: Salim adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 17 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Aswad ibn Yazid al-Nakha'i was a prominent Tabi'i scholar of Kufa and a devoted student of major companions including Aisha (RA) and Abdullah ibn Masud (RA). He was a nephew of the companion Alqama ibn Qais and was known for his strict asceticism and deep personal piety. He narrated numerous hadiths and is regarded as a highly reliable transmitter by the scholars of hadith. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections and he is considered an authoritative and trustworthy link in the chains of transmission.
bio_en: Al-Aswad ibn Yazid al-Nakha'i al-Kufi was a prominent Tabi'i scholar of Kufa and a leading student of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA). He was the nephew of the companion Alqama ibn Qais al-Nakha'i and grew up in the main intellectual circle of Kufa. He also traveled to Medina to narrate directly from Aisha (RA), who held him in high regard and called him 'my son.' He was known for his extraordinary asceticism: he fasted very frequently, stood in prayer most of the night, and performed Hajj and Umra eighty times. Despite this demanding spiritual practice he maintained impeccable reliability in hadith transmission. He is regarded as fully trustworthy (thiqah thiqah) by all major hadith critics. His narrations appear throughout the major collections. He passed away in Kufa around 74–75 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Aswad adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 17 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (RA) was among the earliest Muslims and one of the most principled and outspoken companions of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). He was known for his rejection of worldly wealth, his deep spirituality, and his fearless advocacy for justice regardless of personal consequence. He narrated hadiths on topics ranging from faith and prayer to social ethics, and is regarded as a sincere and reliable narrator. His narrations are found throughout the major hadith collections and he is revered as a model of conviction.
bio_en: Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (RA) — this entry 'AbudDharr' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion documented under entry 22, appearing in the Abu Dawud Sunan. His full name was Jundub ibn Junadah from the Ghifar tribe. He was among the earliest Muslims, was intensely ascetic, refused worldly accumulation, and was known as the most truthful-tongued of the companions. He narrated approximately 281 hadiths. He passed away alone in al-Rabadha around 31–32 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Dzar al-Ghifari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal atas kezuhudan dan keterusterangannya dalam menyampaikan kebenaran. Ia termasuk di antara orang-orang pertama yang masuk Islam.

bio_short_en: Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) known for his knowledge of Quranic recitation, Islamic law, and military arts. He served as the governor of Egypt during the caliphate of Muawiyah and was highly respected for his scholarship, piety, and administrative ability. He narrated numerous hadiths covering topics such as prayer, charity, and proper conduct. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections where he is regarded as a trustworthy and reliable narrator.
bio_en: Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (RA) was from the tribe of Juhayna and came to Medina during the final years of the Prophet's mission. He was known for his expertise in Quranic recitation, archery, and horsemanship — skills the Prophet ﷺ specifically encouraged. He served as the leader of the delegation from his tribe to the Prophet ﷺ and participated in several military expeditions. He narrated approximately 55 hadiths covering prayer, charity, the permissibility of archery, and Islamic conduct. He was appointed governor of Egypt by Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan and was recognized there as a religious authority and teacher. He was regarded as one of the most learned companions in Egypt during his governorship. He passed away in Egypt around 58 AH.
bio_short_id: Uqbah bin Amir adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, Al-Quran, dan ibadah lainnya. Ia menjadi gubernur Mesir dan dikenal atas kefasihannya.

bio_short_en: Ikrima was a Tabi'i scholar and the freed slave of Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), through whom he transmitted a large body of hadiths and tafsir narrations. He was considered one of the most knowledgeable scholars of Quranic exegesis of his generation and traveled widely to transmit and gather knowledge. His narrations appear in all the major hadith collections. Despite some scholarly discussion about his character in early biographical literature, the majority of hadith scholars accepted him as a reliable transmitter of prophetic traditions.
bio_en: Ikrima was a Tabi'i scholar, the freed slave (mawla) of Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), and one of the most important transmitters of Ibn Abbas's vast knowledge of Quranic exegesis and hadith. He was of Berber origin and came to Ibn Abbas as a captive or slave, eventually earning his freedom. He spent his life in devoted study under Ibn Abbas and became so thoroughly trained in tafsir that many scholars regarded his explanations of Quranic verses as the most reliable after those of Ibn Abbas himself. He traveled widely across the Islamic world — to North Africa, Khorasan, and elsewhere — transmitting knowledge and acquiring narrations from various companions and Tabi'in. His character was debated by some early biographers, but the vast majority of hadith scholars including al-Bukhari accepted him as reliable, and his narrations appear in all six major collections. He passed away around 104–107 AH.
bio_short_id: Ikrimah adalah maula (budak yang dimerdekakan) Abdullah bin Abbas dan perawi hadith terpercaya dari generasi tabiin. Ia dikenal atas penguasaan ilmu tafsir.

bio_short_en: Ubada ibn al-Samit (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and one of the respected leaders of the Ansar. He was among those who took the first and second pledges of Aqabah, demonstrating his early and committed support for Islam. He participated in major military campaigns and later served as a teacher and judge in the Levant. His narrations cover topics including the fundamentals of faith and Islamic practice, and they are found in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Ubada ibn al-Samit al-Ansari al-Khazraji (RA) was from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and was among those who took the Second Pledge of Aqabah, committing themselves to the Prophet ﷺ before the Hijra. He was one of twelve leaders (nuqaba) appointed among the Ansar at that pledge. He participated in the Battle of Badr and all subsequent major campaigns. He was appointed by Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) as a teacher and judge in the Levant (al-Sham), where he became one of the most important religious authorities. He narrated approximately 181 hadiths. One of his most famous narrations is the foundational hadith on the bay'a (pledge of allegiance) at Aqabah, in which the Prophet ﷺ stipulated conditions for being Muslim. He also narrated the important hadith on the necessary conditions for valid prayer. He passed away in Syria around 34 AH.
bio_short_id: Ubadah bin ash-Shamit adalah Sahabat Anshar yang termasuk di antara pemimpin Bai'at Aqabah. Ia meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting dan menjadi hakim di Palestina.

bio_short_en: Jubayr ibn Mut'im (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who embraced Islam after initially opposing it. He was known for his expertise in Arab genealogy and his knowledge of religious matters. He narrated a number of hadiths relating to the Quran, prayer, and various aspects of Islamic practice and belief. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections and he is considered a reliable narrator whose transmissions provide valuable insight into the prophetic era.
bio_en: Jubayr ibn Mut'im ibn Adi al-Nawfali al-Qurashi (RA) was from the Quraysh of Mecca and initially was among those who opposed Islam. He came to Medina after the Battle of Badr to ransom prisoners before his formal conversion to Islam. Some accounts indicate it was during this very visit that he heard the Prophet ﷺ reciting Surah al-Tur, and his heart was moved by the Quran. He embraced Islam and became a companion of distinction. He was particularly renowned for his knowledge of Arab genealogy — a science critical for legal, social, and diplomatic purposes in early Islamic society — and was regarded as one of the greatest experts in this field among all the companions. He narrated approximately 60 hadiths. He was also considered an authority in the distribution of the Prophet's family share (khumus) in spoils of war. He passed away around 57–59 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Jubair bin Muth'im adalah Sahabat Nabi dari kabilah Quraisy yang meriwayatkan hadith tentang shalat, nasab, dan berbagai topik lainnya.

bio_short_en: Thawban (RA) was a freed slave of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who became so deeply devoted to him that he could not bear to be apart from him. After the Prophet's death, he settled in Syria and continued to transmit prophetic knowledge to subsequent generations. He narrated numerous hadiths and is particularly known for narrations about the latter times, the virtues of certain religious practices, and the importance of steadfastness in worship. His narrations are found throughout the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Thawban ibn Bujdud (RA) was a freed slave who became deeply devoted to the Prophet ﷺ and chose to remain in his service rather than returning to independence. He was so attached to the Prophet ﷺ that scholars noted he could not bear to be separated from him — and when the Prophet ﷺ saw the sorrow in his face, he asked about it, and Thawban explained that he feared being separated from the Prophet in the afterlife by virtue of the Prophet's higher station. The Prophet ﷺ responded with a narration about those who will be with the people they love. He narrated approximately 128 hadiths, including important traditions on eschatology, the superiority of certain acts of worship, and warnings about the latter days. After the Prophet's death he settled in Syria, where he lived and taught until his death around 54 AH.
bio_short_id: Tsauban adalah maula (budak yang dimerdekakan) Nabi Muhammad ﷺ yang setia menemani beliau. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang ibadah dan akhlak.

bio_short_en: Imran ibn Husayn (RA) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) known for his exceptional piety, spiritual experiences, and commitment to transmitting prophetic knowledge. He settled in Basra and became one of its most important religious teachers, guiding the Muslim community there for many years. He narrated numerous hadiths on topics including prayer, worship, and the Prophet's character and conduct. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections and he is regarded as a reliable and distinguished narrator.
bio_en: Imran ibn Husayn (RA) — this entry is an alternate transliteration of the same companion as entry 35, documented fully there. He was from the tribe of Khuza'a, embraced Islam in 7 AH, and settled in Basra where he was the most learned religious authority of his time. He was known for his piety, his spiritual experiences, and his patience in chronic illness. He narrated approximately 180 hadiths and passed away in Basra around 52–53 AH.
bio_short_id: Imran bin Husain adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan dikenal atas kedudukannya yang tinggi. Para malaikat pun konon turut hadir dalam majelisnya.

bio_short_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who dedicated himself wholeheartedly to preserving and transmitting the Sunnah after the Prophet's death. He narrated hundreds of hadiths on topics including worship, legal rulings, eschatology, and the signs of the Last Day, and was one of the most active and devoted narrators among the companions. His narrations are found extensively in all the major hadith collections and he is regarded as a highly trustworthy and authoritative narrator of prophetic tradition.
bio_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA) — this entry 'Abu Sa'id Al Khudri' is another transliteration of the same Ansari companion documented under entries 11, 15, 17, and 59. Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan narrated over 1,170 hadiths, participated in twelve military expeditions, and was one of the most active teachers in Medina after the Prophet's death. He passed away around 74 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir.

bio_short_en: Khabbab ibn al-Aratt (RA) was among the earliest Muslims and suffered some of the most severe persecution at the hands of the Quraysh for his refusal to renounce Islam. He was a blacksmith by trade and endured brutal torture, yet remained steadfast in his faith. He narrated a number of hadiths preserved in the major collections, including narrations about the early hardships of the Muslim community and various aspects of Islamic practice. He is revered as a towering example of perseverance and sincerity in the path of faith.
bio_en: Khabbab ibn al-Aratt al-Tamimi (RA) was one of the very early Muslims in Mecca, having embraced Islam in approximately the fourth or fifth year of the prophethood. He was a blacksmith and iron-worker who was enslaved and thus had no tribal protection, making him particularly vulnerable to the Quraysh's brutal persecution of early Muslims. He endured some of the most severe torture recorded: hot irons were pressed to his back, he was made to lie on burning coals, and his back was permanently scarred. He came to the Prophet ﷺ and asked him to pray for their relief, and the Prophet ﷺ gave him the famous response reminding him of the even greater tribulations borne by the believers before them. He narrated approximately 32 hadiths. He participated in the Hijra to Medina and all subsequent campaigns. He passed away in Kufa around 37 AH. His scarred body and steadfast faith made him a legendary figure of perseverance among the companions.
bio_short_id: Khabbab adalah perawi hadith yang meriwayatkan 15 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Amr is a common name among early Muslim narrators, borne by several Companions and Successors. The most prominent include Amr ibn al-As (may Allah be pleased with him), the renowned Companion, military commander, and conqueror of Egypt, and Amr ibn Dinar, a leading Successor scholar of Mecca. Without additional context, this entry refers to one of several narrators of this name who transmitted hadith in the early Islamic tradition.
bio_en: Amr ibn al-As (RA) — this entry 'Amr' primarily refers to the famous companion and general Amr ibn al-As al-Sahmi al-Qurashi, though the name was common among multiple narrators. Amr ibn al-As was one of the most brilliant military minds of early Islam: he embraced Islam in 8 AH, shortly before the conquest of Mecca, and was immediately entrusted by the Prophet ﷺ with military and diplomatic missions. He led the conquest of Egypt in 19–20 AH under Caliph Umar (RA). He narrated approximately 39 hadiths. He was known for his sharp intellect, strategic thinking, and, in his later years, deep personal piety. He reportedly said on his deathbed that he had passed through three states: unbelief, Islam without full sincerity, and finally true sincere submission to Allah. He passed away in Egypt around 42–43 AH as its governor.
bio_short_id: 'Amr adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 15 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who embraced Islam before the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. He was a skilled diplomat and military leader, serving as governor of Basra and Kufa during the caliphates of Umar and Uthman. He narrated numerous hadiths and was known for his intelligence and political acumen. He passed away around 50 AH.
bio_en: Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba al-Thaqafi (RA) was from the tribe of Thaqif in Ta'if and embraced Islam before the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah in 6 AH. He was one of the most politically astute companions, serving as a diplomat, military commander, and governor under multiple caliphs. He served as governor of Basra under Umar (RA) and later as governor of Kufa under Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan, a post he held until his death. He was renowned for his wit and intelligence, with many witty exchanges attributed to him in classical literature. He narrated approximately 136 hadiths on various topics including prayer, contracts, and prophetic conduct. He performed Hajj with the Prophet ﷺ, participated in many campaigns, and was involved in the conquest of Iraq and Persia. He passed away in Kufa around 50 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Mughirah bin Syu'bah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal atas kecerdikan dan kemampuan diplomatiknya. Ia menjabat sebagai gubernur Basra dan Kufah.

bio_short_en: Abu Salama ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf was a leading Successor scholar of Medina and one of the seven great jurists of the city. He was the son of the Companion Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf (may Allah be pleased with him). Abu Salama transmitted extensively from Aisha, Abu Huraira, and other Companions, making him a key link in numerous hadith chains. He was highly regarded for his piety and legal knowledge.
bio_en: Abu Salama ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina and is counted among the Seven Jurists of Medina (al-fuqaha al-sab'a). He was the son of the companion Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf (RA) — one of the ten companions promised Paradise — which gave him access to some of the most authoritative companions from his earliest years. He narrated extensively from Aisha (RA), Abu Huraira (RA), and other senior companions. His relationship with Abu Huraira was particularly close and productive, and he transmitted a large portion of Abu Huraira's narrations. He was regarded by hadith critics as fully trustworthy and was one of the most important transmitters in the Medinan scholarly tradition. Imam al-Zuhri narrated from him extensively. He passed away around 94 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Salama adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 15 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Rafi bin Khadij (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina. He participated in several battles and is known for narrating hadiths on agricultural contracts, particularly the prohibition of certain share-cropping arrangements. He was a reliable narrator whose reports are found in the major hadith collections. He lived until approximately 74 AH.
bio_en: Rafi ibn Khadij al-Ansari (RA) was from the Ansar of Medina, from the tribe of Aws. He was one of the companions who was initially turned away from the Battle of Badr for being too young, but he participated in subsequent campaigns including Uhud and Khandaq. He narrated approximately 78 hadiths. He is most well known for his narrations on agricultural contracts (muzara'a and mukhabara) — the Prophet ﷺ forbade certain share-cropping arrangements that might lead to injustice, and Rafi's narrations on this topic are the primary source for the relevant legal rulings in Islamic jurisprudence. He also narrated hadiths on hunting and permissible slaughter. He settled in Medina after the Prophet's death and was a respected narrator there. He passed away around 73–74 AH.
bio_short_id: Rafi' bin Khadij adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith tentang pertanian, transaksi, dan ibadah. Ia dikenal atas kehati-hatiannya dalam meriwayatkan hadith.

bio_short_en: Abu Is-haq al-Sabi'i (Amr ibn Abdullah al-Hamdani) was a prominent Successor scholar from Kufa who lived through the era of the senior Companions. He narrated from numerous Companions including Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Bara ibn Azib, and Zaid ibn Arqam (may Allah be pleased with them). He was considered a highly reliable narrator in his earlier years and transmitted a large number of hadiths. He passed away around 127 AH.
bio_en: Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i, whose full name was Amr ibn Abdullah ibn Ubaid al-Hamdani al-Sabi'i, was a prominent Tabi'i scholar from Kufa. He narrated from a large number of companions including Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Bara ibn Azib, Zaid ibn Arqam, and Hubaysh ibn Junadah. He was considered highly reliable (thiqah) by hadith critics in his earlier years of narration. He was also a significant authority in Quranic recitation and Islamic law. In his later years some critics noted that his memory may have been affected, and a small number of later narrations attributed to him were treated with additional scrutiny. He was the teacher of Sufyan al-Thawri, Shu'ba ibn al-Hajjaj, and other leading scholars of the following generation. He passed away in Kufa around 127–129 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu 'Is-haq adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 15 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) was a distinguished Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina. He participated in twelve battles alongside the Prophet and narrated over one thousand hadiths, making him among the most prolific Companion narrators. He was particularly known for his hadiths on Islamic jurisprudence, eschatology, and ethical conduct. He passed away in Medina around 74 AH.
bio_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA) — this entry 'Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA)' appearing in the Bulugh al-Maram collection refers once again to Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari, the prolific Ansari companion documented under entries 11, 15, 17, 59, and 79. He participated in twelve battles with the Prophet ﷺ, narrated over 1,170 hadiths, and was a resident of Medina until his death around 74 AH. His narrations in Bulugh al-Maram reflect his broad legal knowledge and his eyewitness proximity to the Prophet ﷺ.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir.

bio_short_en: Tawus ibn Kaysan al-Yamani was a leading Successor scholar and jurist from Yemen. He narrated from numerous Companions including Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, and Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with them), and was considered among the most learned men of his generation. He was known for his asceticism, refusal of gifts from rulers, and independent legal opinions. He passed away in Mecca around 106 AH during the Hajj season.
bio_en: Tawus ibn Kaysan al-Yamani al-Hamdani was a leading Tabi'i scholar and jurist from Yemen. He was born around 33 AH in Yemen and narrated from a wide range of companions including Abdullah ibn Abbas, Abdullah ibn Umar, Abu Huraira, Zayd ibn Thabit, and Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with them all). He was considered one of the most learned men of his generation, combining mastery of hadith with deep legal acumen. He was famous for his independence: he repeatedly refused gifts and financial support from rulers and governors, and famously refused to stand up for Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik (the Umayyad Caliph) at Hajj, which caused a commotion. He was known for his serious demeanor and profound asceticism. He passed away in Mecca around 106 AH during the Hajj season and was buried there.
bio_short_id: Thawus bin Kaysan adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Yaman dan murid Ibn Abbas. Ia dikenal atas kezuhudan dan kehati-hatiannya dalam berfatwa.

bio_short_en: Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who embraced Islam at the conquest of Mecca. He served as a scribe of the Quranic revelation and later became the founder of the Umayyad Caliphate, ruling for nearly twenty years. He narrated hadiths from the Prophet and is regarded as a Companion within Ahl us-Sunnah, though his political role remains a subject of historical discussion. He passed away in 60 AH.
bio_en: Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan (RA) was the son of Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, the Qurayshi leader who opposed Islam for many years before embracing it. Muawiya himself accepted Islam at the Conquest of Mecca in 8 AH and served as a scribe recording the Quranic revelations — a position of great trust near the end of the Prophet's life. He narrated approximately 163 hadiths. He became the governor of Syria under Caliph Umar (RA) and later under Uthman (RA). After the civil war following Uthman's assassination, he became the founder of the Umayyad Caliphate and ruled for nearly twenty years (41–60 AH). Ahl us-Sunnah regards him as a companion whose political role is accepted as part of the turbulent realities of early Islamic history, without engaging in cursing or condemnation. He passed away in Damascus in 60 AH.
bio_short_id: Muawiyah bin Abi Sufyan adalah Sahabat Nabi dan pendiri Dinasti Umayyah. Ia menjabat sebagai sekretaris wahyu Nabi ﷺ dan meriwayatkan beberapa hadith.

bio_short_en: Zaid bin Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the tribe of Juhayna. He participated in the Battle of Hudaybiyyah and is particularly known for narrating hadiths related to found property (luqata), purification, and prayer. He settled in Medina and later Kufa, where he transmitted hadith until his passing around 68 AH. His reports appear in all major hadith collections.
bio_en: Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (RA) was from the tribe of Juhayna and was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who participated in the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah in 6 AH. He was known for his piety and devotion to worship — there is a report that he observed the Prophet ﷺ leading the dawn prayer after a night of rain at Hudaybiyyah and then addressing the people about the spiritual states of those who attributed the rain to Allah versus those who attributed it to the stars. This narration is one of his most frequently cited. He narrated approximately 81 hadiths on topics including found property (luqata), prayer, and purification. He settled in Medina after the Prophet's death. He passed away around 68 AH.
bio_short_id: Zaid bin Khalid al-Juhani adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith tentang ibadah dan hukum Islam. Ia tinggal di Kufah pada masa tuanya.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Az-Zubair (may Allah be pleased with him) was the son of the Companion Al-Zubair ibn al-Awwam and the grandson of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq. He was the first child born to the Muslim emigrants (Muhajirun) in Medina after the Hijra, and his birth was considered a blessed sign for the community. He narrated hadiths from the Prophet and from senior Companions, and later became the Caliph in Mecca. He was martyred in 73 AH.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam al-Asadi al-Qurashi (RA) was born in Medina in 1 AH — the first child born to the Muslim emigrants (Muhajirun) after the Hijra, and his birth was considered a blessed sign since the Jews of Medina had spread a rumor that they had bewitched the Muslims so they would have no children. The Prophet ﷺ performed the tahnik (softening a date and touching it to the newborn's palate) for him and named him. He was the son of al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA) and the grandson of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (RA) through his mother Asma. He narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ and from senior companions including Umar (RA) and Uthman (RA). He later became the Caliph in Mecca after refusing to pledge allegiance to Yazid ibn Muawiya, and he ruled from 64 AH until he was besieged and martyred by al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf in 73 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Az-Zubair adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 14 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: An-Nu'man bin Bashir (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad whose father Bashir ibn Sa'd was also a Companion. He was brought to the Prophet as an infant, making him among the youngest Companions. He later served as governor of Kufa and Homs under Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan. He is renowned for narrating the famous hadith on the halal and haram being clear and the importance of avoiding doubtful matters. He was martyred around 65 AH.
bio_en: Al-Nu'man ibn Bashir al-Ansari (RA) was born in Medina in 2 AH, making him one of the youngest companions. His father Bashir ibn Sa'd was also a companion, and his mother Amra bint Rawaha was the sister of the poet-companion Abdullah ibn Rawaha. When he was a few years old his mother brought him to the Prophet ﷺ, intending to make a gift of some property to him — the Prophet ﷺ asked his father whether he had given all his children the same gift, and when the answer was no, the Prophet ﷺ declined to be a witness to an unequal bequest, providing the basis for one of the most important legal principles on equal treatment of children. He narrated approximately 114 hadiths. He served as governor of Kufa and Homs under Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan. He was martyred around 65 AH during the period of strife after Yazid's death.
bio_short_id: An-Nu'man bin Basyir adalah putra seorang Sahabat dan dirinya sendiri juga seorang Sahabat muda. Ia meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting termasuk hadith tentang halal dan haram.

bio_short_en: Abu Hazim al-Ashja'i (Salmaan, the freed slave of Azda) was a Successor who transmitted hadiths from several Companions. A second narrator of the same name, Abu Hazim ibn Dinar al-Araj, was a notable Medinian Successor scholar who narrated extensively from Abu Huraira and Sahl ibn Sa'd. Both were reliable transmitters whose reports appear in the Sahihayn and Sunan collections. The name Abu Hazim was borne by several trustworthy scholars of the early generations.
bio_en: Abu Hazim al-Ashja'i was a Tabi'i narrator who transmitted hadiths from several companions. The name Abu Hazim was borne by two notable Tabi'i scholars. The first, Salmaan — freed slave of Azda — narrated from companions in Kufa and the Hijaz and was generally regarded as reliable. The second and better-known scholar by this name was Abu Hazim Salama ibn Dinar al-Araj al-Madani, a Tabi'i of Medina who died around 135 AH. He was particularly notable for narrating extensively from Abu Huraira (RA) and Sahl ibn Sa'd (RA), and served as the teacher of Imam Malik ibn Anas. He was known for his deep asceticism, his philosophical sayings on life and death, and his frank speech with rulers. Both narrators sharing this name were trustworthy transmitters whose reports appear in the Sahihayn and Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Abu Hazim adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 14 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Salama bin Al-Akwa (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad renowned for his exceptional speed and skill in foot-racing. He participated in numerous battles, including Hudaybiyyah, Khaybar, and Hunayn, and gave his pledge of allegiance under the tree at Hudaybiyyah. He narrated many hadiths related to warfare, hunting, and the Prophet's conduct. In his later years he settled in the Rabadha region and passed away around 74 AH.
bio_en: Salama ibn al-Akwa al-Aslami (RA), whose full name was Salama ibn Amr ibn al-Akwa, was from the tribe of Aslam and was renowned among the companions for his exceptional speed in running on foot — a speed that reportedly allowed him to keep pace with a horse. He participated in many military expeditions, including Hudaybiyyah, where he gave his pledge under the tree. He was at Khaybar, Hunayn, and other major campaigns. He narrated approximately 77 hadiths, many relating to warfare, hunting, and the Prophet's conduct during expeditions. He is known for hadiths describing the pursuit of raiders and his personal exploits in protecting the Muslim community. In his later years he withdrew from the main population centers and settled in the Rabadha area, preferring a simpler life. He passed away around 74 AH.
bio_short_id: Salama bin Al-Akwa' adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 14 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with him) was the most prolific narrator of hadith among all the Companions of the Prophet Muhammad, transmitting over five thousand reports. He embraced Islam in 7 AH and remained closely attached to the Prophet, dedicating himself to memorizing his sayings. He served as governor of Bahrain and later settled in Medina, where he taught thousands of students. He passed away around 57-59 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of the same Companion.
bio_en: Abu Huraira (RA) — this entry 'Abu Huraira (rad)' is yet another transliteration variant of the same most-prolific companion narrator, here appearing in the Bulugh al-Maram collection. His full biography is documented under entry 1. He narrated over 5,374 hadiths, served as governor of Bahrain, and taught thousands of students in Medina until his death around 57–59 AH. This designation 'rad' is an abbreviation of 'radiyallahu anhu' (may Allah be pleased with him).
bio_short_id: Abu Huraira (rad) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 14 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Bakrah Nufay ibn al-Harith (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who came to Islam during the Siege of Ta'if, descending from the fortress to join the Muslims. He settled in Basra, where he became one of the leading Companions in that region and a respected transmitter of hadith. He is well known for narrating the Prophet's prohibition against fighting in civil strife. He passed away in Basra around 51-52 AH.
bio_en: Abu Bakra al-Thaqafi (RA) — this entry 'AbuBakrah' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion documented under entry 27, Nufay ibn al-Harith, who descended from the walls of Ta'if on a pulley and embraced Islam. He settled in Basra and became one of its most respected companions. He narrated approximately 132 hadiths and is particularly known for the narration forbidding participation in civil strife. He passed away in Basra around 51–52 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Bakrah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith, termasuk hadith masyhur tentang dilarangnya membunuh pemimpin dan fitnah di akhir zaman. Ia tinggal di Basra.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) was the cousin of the Prophet Muhammad and one of the greatest scholars among the Companions. The Prophet supplicated for him, asking Allah to grant him deep understanding of religion and knowledge of the Quran's interpretation. He became the foremost authority on Quranic exegesis (tafsir) and Islamic jurisprudence, earning the title Hibr al-Umma (Scholar of the Nation) and Turjuman al-Quran (Interpreter of the Quran). He narrated over one thousand six hundred hadiths and passed away in Ta'if around 68 AH.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) — this entry 'Abdullah Ibn Abbas' represents the same companion as entries 5, 16, and 21, appearing in the Abu Dawud collection under this specific spelling. He was the cousin of the Prophet ﷺ, the foremost Quranic exegete among the companions, and a narrator of over 1,660 hadiths. The Prophet ﷺ prayed specifically for him to receive deep understanding of the religion. He passed away around 68 AH in Ta'if. His full biography is under entry 5.
bio_short_id: Abdullah Ibn Abbas adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 13 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Shihab) was one of the most important Successor scholars in Islamic history, considered a master of hadith science and one of the earliest systematic collectors of the Prophet's traditions. He narrated from numerous Companions including Anas ibn Malik and Sahl ibn Sa'd, and served as a teacher to Imam Malik and many other leading scholars. He was among the first to formally compile hadiths on the instruction of Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz. He passed away around 124 AH.
bio_en: Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri (Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Shihab) — this entry represents the same Tabi'i scholar documented fully under entry 34, appearing here in Bukhari, Abu Dawud, and Tirmidhi under the shortened name 'Ibn Shihab.' He was one of the most important figures in the history of hadith compilation, the teacher of Imam Malik and many others, and a narrator from numerous companions. He passed away around 124 AH. His chains appear in virtually every chapter of the major collections.
bio_short_id: Ibnu Syihab az-Zuhri adalah tabiin terkemuka dan salah satu perawi hadith paling penting dalam sejarah Islam. Ia berperan dalam kodifikasi hadith secara resmi pada masa Khalifah Umar bin Abdul Aziz.

bio_short_en: Qatadah ibn Di'ama al-Sadusi was a prominent Successor scholar from Basra, renowned as a memorizer of the Quran and an authority in hadith and Quranic exegesis. Despite being blind from birth, he possessed an extraordinary memory and narrated from Anas ibn Malik, Abdullah ibn Sarjas, and other Companions. He was a student of al-Hasan al-Basri and a leading representative of the Basran scholarly tradition. He passed away around 117 AH.
bio_en: Qatada ibn Di'ama al-Sadusi — this entry 'Qatadah' is an alternate transliteration of the same Tabi'i scholar documented under entry 55, appearing in Abu Dawud and Tirmidhi. He was the blind Basran scholar with an extraordinary memory who narrated from Anas ibn Malik and other companions. He was a student of al-Hasan al-Basri and a leading authority in tafsir and hadith. He passed away around 117–118 AH.
bio_short_id: Qatadah bin Di'amah adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Basra yang dikenal atas hafalan dan penguasaannya dalam ilmu Al-Quran, tafsir, dan hadith.

bio_short_en: Abu Uthman al-Nahdi (Abd al-Rahman ibn Mall) was a senior Successor scholar who lived an exceptionally long life, reportedly reaching 130 years of age. He narrated from several Companions including Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, Salman al-Farsi, and Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with them). He was based in Basra and was considered a highly reliable and trustworthy narrator. He passed away around 95-100 AH.
bio_en: Abu Uthman al-Nahdi, whose full name was Abd al-Rahman ibn Mall (or Mihl) al-Nahdi, was a senior Tabi'i scholar from Basra who reportedly lived to an extraordinary age — some accounts say he reached 130 years of age. He narrated from several companions of the Prophet ﷺ including Abu Musa al-Ashari, Salman al-Farsi, Umar ibn al-Khattab, and Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf. He was considered fully reliable (thiqah) by all hadith critics, and his long life allowed him to serve as a direct witness to the companion generation and then transmit that knowledge well into the Tabi'i era. He was based in Basra. He passed away around 95–100 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu 'Uthman adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 13 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Salama is a name shared by several early Muslim narrators. The most notable is Salama bin Al-Akwa (may Allah be pleased with him), the Companion renowned for his bravery and speed in battle. Another is Salama ibn Kuhail, a Successor scholar from Kufa who narrated from several Companions and Successors and was considered reliable by hadith critics. Without further context, this entry refers to one of the several narrators named Salama in the early Islamic tradition.
bio_en: Salama — the name Salama was borne by several companions and Tabi'in. The most prominent is Salama ibn al-Akwa al-Aslami (RA), documented under entry 93, renowned for his speed in running and his participation in major military expeditions. Another is Salama ibn Kuhail al-Hadrami, a Tabi'i scholar of Kufa who narrated from companions and Successors and was considered reliable by critics, dying around 121–122 AH. Without further context, narrations attributed to 'Salama' in Bukhari likely refer to Salama ibn al-Akwa. Both narrators were trustworthy transmitters whose reports appear in the major collections.
bio_short_id: Salama adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 13 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: As-Sa'ib ibn Yazid (RA) was a Companion who, according to his own account, was brought before the Prophet ﷺ as a young child and the Prophet ﷺ passed his hand over his head in blessing. He participated in Hajj with the Prophet ﷺ at the age of seven during the Farewell Pilgrimage. He went on to serve in the administration during the caliphate of Umar (RA) and later Uthman (RA). He narrated hadiths relating to the Prophet's physical description, conduct, and acts of worship. He lived to approximately 91 AH and was one of the last Companions to survive in Madinah.
bio_en: Al-Sa'ib ibn Yazid al-Kindi (RA) was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who, by his own account, was brought before the Prophet ﷺ as a child of seven years during the Farewell Pilgrimage, and the Prophet ﷺ passed his hand over his head in blessing. He participated in the Hajj al-Wada' as a young child, making him one of the few companions who could claim direct personal contact with the Prophet ﷺ from the very earliest age. He served in the administration during the caliphates of Umar (RA) and Uthman (RA). He narrated hadiths about the Prophet's physical appearance, his conduct in prayer, and accounts from the early community. He was reportedly one of the last surviving companions in Medina and lived to approximately 91 AH — dying around 91 or 92 AH.
bio_short_id: As-Sa'ib bin Yazid adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 13 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Um Atiyya al-Ansariyyah (Nusayba bint al-Harith, may Allah be pleased with her) was a female Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from Medina. She participated in seven military expeditions, providing medical care and support to the wounded. She narrated hadiths related to the washing of the deceased, funeral rites, and women's religious practice, and her reports are particularly important in Islamic jurisprudence. She is among the most cited female Companions in the hadith literature.
bio_en: Umm Atiyya al-Ansariyyah (RA), whose full name is given as Nusayba bint al-Harith or Nusayba bint Ka'b, was a female companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina. She was one of the women who participated in military expeditions, providing medical care, tending the wounded, and preparing food. She reportedly participated in seven expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ. She is particularly important for her narrations on the ritual washing of deceased women, the preparation of the female body for burial, and women's religious practice more generally. Her narrations on the Prophet's instructions for preparing the body of his daughter Zainab for burial are among the most detailed and important sources in Islamic law on funerary rites. She is one of the most frequently cited female companions in the hadith literature.
bio_short_id: Ummu Athiyyah al-Anshariyyah adalah Sahabiyah (perempuan Sahabat) yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting tentang hukum wanita, termasuk tentang memandikan jenazah.

bio_short_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was a Companion from the Ansar of Madinah who participated in eighteen military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ. Initially too young to join the Battle of Badr, he later participated in all major campaigns. He narrated approximately 305 hadiths and settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death, where he became a key transmitter of prophetic traditions in Iraq. His reports cover prayer, fasting, funeral rites, and other pillars of Islamic practice.
bio_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) — this entry 'Al-Bara bin Azib' in Tirmidhi and Bukhari is yet another transliteration of the same Ansari companion documented under entries 14, 38, 43, and 48. He participated in eighteen military expeditions, narrated approximately 305 hadiths, and settled in Kufa. His narrations cover prayer, descriptions of the Prophet, funeral rites, and battlefield accounts. He passed away around 72 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Bara' bin Azib adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang peperangan dan ibadah. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Ibn Abi Mulaika (Abdullah ibn Ubaydullah ibn Abi Mulaika al-Tamimi) was a Successor scholar and judge from Mecca who served as a judge during the caliphate of Ibn al-Zubair. He narrated extensively from Aisha, Ibn Abbas, and other senior Companions (may Allah be pleased with them), and was considered a highly reliable narrator by hadith scholars. He is known for transmitting many hadiths on Islamic law, ethics, and the conduct of the Prophet. He passed away around 117 AH.
bio_en: Ibn Abi Mulaika — this refers to Abdullah ibn Ubaydullah ibn Abi Mulaika al-Tamimi al-Makki, a Tabi'i scholar and judge from Mecca. He served as a qadi (judge) during the caliphate of Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr and was held in high esteem for his legal knowledge and moral integrity. He narrated from a wide range of senior companions including Aisha (RA), from whom he heard many narrations directly, as well as from Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, and other major companions. Scholars of hadith regarded him as fully reliable (thiqah). His narrations appear frequently in Bukhari and the other major collections, and he is counted as one of the important Tabi'i authorities in the Meccan scholarly tradition. He passed away around 117 AH.
bio_short_id: Ibn Abi Mulaika adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 13 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Samurah ibn Jundab (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who narrated a number of hadiths. He served as governor of Basra under Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan and was known as an administrator. He narrated hadiths on prayer, commercial transactions, and prophetic supplications. His reports appear in the Sunan collections. This entry may overlap with Samurah bin Jundab listed separately; both refer to the same Companion.
bio_en: Samurah ibn Jundab (RA) — this entry 'Samurah' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion documented under entry 50, appearing in Abu Dawud and Tirmidhi. He was a companion of the Prophet ﷺ who served as governor of Basra and narrated approximately 128 hadiths. His reports appear in the Sunan collections and include narrations on prayer, commercial dealings, and prophetic visions. He passed away around 58–59 AH.
bio_short_id: Samurah bin Jundub adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith. Ia menjabat sebagai gubernur Basra.

bio_short_en: Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (Uqba ibn Amr al-Badri, may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina. He participated in the Battle of Badr, which gave him his well-known epithet. He narrated numerous hadiths and later settled in Kufa, where he became a respected religious authority. He is known for transmitting the famous hadith on the connection between the prayer and charity (zakat) and is recognized as a reliable narrator in all major collections.
bio_en: Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (RA) — this entry 'Abu Mas'ud Al-Ansari' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion documented under entry 51, Uqba ibn Amr al-Badri al-Ansari. He participated in the Second Pledge of Aqabah and earned the epithet 'al-Badri.' He narrated approximately 103 hadiths, settled in Kufa, and served as a religious authority there. His narrations appear in Bukhari and Tirmidhi. He passed away during the caliphate of Muawiya, around 40–42 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Mas'ud al-Anshari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting tentang shalat dan fikih.

bio_short_en: Al-Miswar bin Makhrama (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad, born in Mecca two years before the Hijra. He was the nephew of Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf and narrated hadiths from the Prophet, Abu Bakr, and Umar (may Allah be pleased with them). He is known for his narrations related to the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and the Prophet's conduct in diplomacy. He died at the time of Ibn al-Zubair's siege of Mecca around 64 AH.
bio_en: Al-Miswar ibn Makhrama ibn Nawfal al-Zuhri al-Qurashi (RA) was born in Mecca approximately two years before the Hijra and came to Medina as a young child. He was the nephew of the companion Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf (RA), being his sister's son. He narrated from the Prophet ﷺ and from senior companions including Abu Bakr, Umar (RA), and others. He is particularly known for his narrations about the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, where he was present and observed the negotiations. His narrations on this event are among the most detailed accounts of that pivotal episode in Islamic history. He also narrated important hadiths on the prohibition of combining multiple wives within forbidden degrees. He was a resident of Medina and later died during the siege of Mecca by al-Hajjaj in approximately 64 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Miswar bin Makhrama adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 12 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Zaid bin Arqam (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina who participated in seventeen military expeditions. He is known for narrating the famous hadith of Ghadir Khumm in which the Prophet emphasized the importance of the Quran and his household. He transmitted many hadiths on theology, prayer, and the Prophet's virtues, and settled in Kufa where he was held in high esteem. He passed away around 65-68 AH.
bio_en: Zaid ibn Arqam al-Ansari al-Khazraji (RA) was a young companion who participated in seventeen military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ. He is known for one particularly important act of honesty: as a young man he overheard the leader of the hypocrites, Abdullah ibn Ubayy, making a statement of betrayal against the Prophet ﷺ. He reported it to the Prophet ﷺ despite pressure from his uncle to stay quiet. The subsequent revelation of Surah al-Munafiqun vindicated his report. He narrated approximately 66 hadiths. His famous narration of the Hadith of Ghadir Khumm — in which the Prophet ﷺ emphasized the importance of the Quran and his household — is one of his most cited narrations. He settled in Kufa and was held in high esteem. He passed away around 65–68 AH.
bio_short_id: Zaid bin Arqam adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting termasuk tentang peristiwa Ghadir Khum dan sifat-sifat Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Sa'id bin Al-Musaiyab was one of the seven great jurists of Medina and the foremost Successor scholar of his generation. His father and grandfather were Companions of the Prophet. He narrated extensively from Abu Huraira, whose daughter he married, as well as from Umar, Uthman, Ali, and Aisha (may Allah be pleased with them). He was renowned for his legal acumen, his refusal to accept gifts from rulers, and his profound piety. He passed away around 94 AH.
bio_en: Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab ibn Hazn al-Makhzumi al-Qurashi was the foremost jurist of the Tabi'i generation and is universally regarded as the most learned of the Seven Jurists of Medina. His father and grandfather were both companions of the Prophet ﷺ. He learned hadith primarily from Abu Huraira (RA) — whose daughter he married — and also from Umar, Uthman, Ali, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and Aisha (may Allah be pleased with them). He was distinguished above all others of his generation for the breadth of his legal scholarship and the depth of his piety. He consistently refused gifts from rulers and was imprisoned and flogged for refusing to pledge allegiance to al-Walid ibn Abd al-Malik but maintained his principles throughout. He was the teacher of Imam al-Zuhri, Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Ansari, and other major scholars. He passed away around 94 AH.
bio_short_id: Sa'id bin Al-Musaiyab adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 12 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Sa'eed is likely a variant reference to Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him), the prolific Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina. He narrated over one thousand hadiths and was a key transmitter of prophetic traditions on jurisprudence, eschatology, and ethics. Alternatively, this may refer to another narrator sharing this kunya. His reports are found in all major hadith collections and are highly relied upon by Islamic scholars.
bio_en: Abu Sa'eed — this entry likely refers to Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (RA), the prolific Ansari companion documented under entries 11, 15, 17, 59, 79, and 86, appearing here in the Tirmidhi collection under a shortened form of his kunya. Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari was one of the most devoted narrators of hadith after the Prophet's death, transmitting over 1,170 hadiths on all areas of Islamic practice. He passed away around 74 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir.
