bio_short_en: Abu Umamah al-Bahili (Sudayy ibn Ajlan, may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who was the last surviving Companion in Syria. He narrated a large number of hadiths and was renowned for his frequent recitation of the Quran. He settled in Syria and became a leading religious authority there. He is known for hadiths encouraging the recitation of Surah al-Baqarah to ward off evil, and for numerous narrations on worship and virtue. He passed away around 86-88 AH.
bio_en: Abu Umamah al-Bahili (RA), whose full name was Sudayy ibn Ajlan ibn Wahb al-Bahili, was from the tribe of Bahila and embraced Islam during the Prophet's lifetime. He settled in Syria after the Prophet's death and became one of the most important companions in the Levant region. He was the last surviving companion in Syria and lived to around 86–88 AH — making him the final link in the entire companion generation in that important province. He narrated a significant number of hadiths — over 250 — and was particularly devoted to the recitation of Quran and the teaching of religion. He is known for hadiths encouraging the recitation of Surah al-Baqarah to protect the home from shaytan, and for numerous narrations on the virtues of various acts of worship. His narrations appear in Abu Dawud and other collections. This entry 'AbuUmamah' is an alternate transliteration.
bio_short_id: AbuUmamah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 11 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (Abdullah ibn Qays, may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from Yemen. He was a renowned reciter of the Quran, and the Prophet praised his beautiful voice, saying it was like a flute of the family of David. He served as governor of Basra and Kufa and played a significant diplomatic role. He narrated many hadiths on prayer, recitation, and Islamic conduct. He passed away around 42-52 AH.
bio_en: Abu Musa al-Ashari (RA) — this entry 'AbuMusa al-Ash'ari' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion documented fully under entry 10. Abdullah ibn Qays al-Ashari was from Yemen, known for his beautiful Quran recitation, served as governor of Basra and Kufa, and narrated approximately 360 hadiths. He passed away around 42–52 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Musa al-Asy'ari adalah Sahabat Nabi dari Yaman yang terkenal dengan suara indahnya dalam membaca Al-Quran. Ia menjabat sebagai gubernur Basra dan Kufah, dan meriwayatkan banyak hadith.

bio_short_en: Abu al-Darda (Uwaymir ibn Zayd, may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina, known for his wisdom, asceticism, and deep knowledge of religion. He embraced Islam after the Battle of Badr and became one of the learned scholars among the Companions. He served as a judge in Damascus and was sent by Caliph Uthman to teach the people of Syria. He narrated many hadiths on wisdom, piety, and spiritual conduct. He passed away around 32 AH.
bio_en: Abu al-Darda' (RA), whose full name was Uwaymir ibn Malik ibn Zayd al-Ansari al-Khazraji, was from the Ansar of Medina. He embraced Islam after the Battle of Badr — reportedly having been the last of the Khazraj leaders to convert. Despite his late conversion he quickly distinguished himself through his extraordinary wisdom, deep piety, and asceticism. The Prophet ﷺ established a bond of brotherhood between him and Salman al-Farsi (RA), and Salman's famous counsel to Abu al-Darda' about balancing worship with the rights of the body became one of the most celebrated statements on moderation in Islamic tradition. He narrated approximately 179 hadiths. He was sent by Caliph Uthman to Damascus as a teacher and judge, where he remained until his death. He passed away around 32 AH. He is revered as one of the great ascetics and sages of the companion generation.
bio_short_id: AbudDarda' adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 11 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Asma bint Abi Bakr (RA) was the elder sister of Aisha (RA) and the daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (RA). She played a crucial role in the Prophet's migration to Madinah by carrying food to the Prophet ﷺ and her father as they hid in the Cave of Thawr. She is known as 'Dhat al-Nitaqayn' — the Lady of the Two Girdles — because she tore her girdle to tie the provisions for the Hijra. She narrated approximately 58 hadiths and lived to 100 years of age, dying shortly after the death of her son Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr in 73 AH.
bio_en: Asma bint Abi Bakr al-Siddiq (RA) was the elder sister of Aisha (RA) and the daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq. She is known by the epithet Dhat al-Nitaqayn — the Lady of the Two Girdles — because she tore her girdle in two to tie the food provisions she carried to the Prophet ﷺ and her father as they hid in the Cave of Thawr during the Hijra. This act of courage and sacrifice at one of the most critical moments in Islamic history earned her enduring honour. She was married to al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA) and was the mother of Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr. She narrated approximately 58 hadiths. She lived to 100 years of age — it is said her teeth never fell out and her mind remained sharp until the end. She passed away in 73 AH in Mecca, shortly after the martyrdom of her son Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr.
bio_short_id: Asma' binti Abu Bakr adalah putri Khalifah Abu Bakr dan saudari Aisyah. Ia dikenal atas keberanian dan keteguhannya, serta meriwayatkan beberapa hadith penting.

bio_short_en: Abu Umamah al-Bahili (Sudayy ibn Ajlan, may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who became the last surviving Companion in the Levant region. He narrated a significant number of hadiths and was well known for his dedication to Quranic recitation and religious teaching in Syria. He is cited in the Sunan and Musnad collections for hadiths on worship, ethics, and the virtues of specific deeds. He passed away around 86-88 AH. This entry is a variant of AbuUmamah.
bio_en: Abu Umamah al-Bahili (RA) — this entry 'Abu Umamah' is the same companion documented under entry 111, Sudayy ibn Ajlan al-Bahili, appearing in Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, and Riyad al-Salihin under a slightly different transliteration. He was the last surviving companion in Syria, narrated over 250 hadiths, and passed away around 86–88 AH. His reports in Tirmidhi include important narrations on the virtues of worship and the Day of Judgment.
bio_short_id: Abu Umamah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 11 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Hasan al-Basri (al-Hasan ibn Abi al-Hasan Yasar) was one of the greatest Successor scholars and spiritual figures in Islamic history. Born in Medina in 21 AH, he narrated from numerous Companions including Anas ibn Malik, Abu Huraira, and Aisha (may Allah be pleased with them). He became the leading scholar of Basra, renowned for his eloquence, profound piety, and asceticism. He was a major transmitter of hadiths and a foundational figure in Islamic spirituality and ethics. He passed away in 110 AH.
bio_en: Al-Hasan al-Basri, whose full name was Abu Sa'id al-Hasan ibn Abi al-Hasan Yasar al-Basri, was one of the greatest Tabi'i scholars and spiritual figures in Islamic history. He was born in Medina in 21 AH, the son of a freed slave, and spent his early life in the household of Umm Salama (RA) — one of the Mothers of the Believers — which gave him access to the companion generation in the most intimate way. He narrated from many companions including Anas ibn Malik, Abu Huraira, and Aisha. He later settled in Basra and became its undisputed leading scholar in hadith, tafsir, legal opinion, and — above all — spiritual and moral guidance. His sermons and sayings on death, the afterlife, and the dangers of the worldly life are among the most powerful in Islamic literature. He was also a teacher of Tawus ibn Kaysan, Qatada ibn Di'ama, and many others. He passed away in Basra in 110 AH.
bio_short_id: Al-Hasan al-Bashri adalah tabiin terkemuka yang dikenal atas kezuhudan dan nasihat-nasihatnya yang mendalam. Ia bertemu banyak Sahabat Nabi dan menjadi ulama besar Basra.

bio_short_en: Uqbah bin Amir al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who served as governor of Egypt under Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan. He was skilled in archery and the Prophet encouraged his practice of it. He narrated numerous hadiths on prayer, charity, and the virtues of Islamic practice, and is known for preserving traditions related to archery and horsemanship. He passed away in Egypt around 58 AH. This entry may overlap with Uqba bin Amir listed separately.
bio_en: Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (RA) — this entry 'Uqbah bin Amir' is an alternate transliteration of the same companion documented under entry 73, appearing in Tirmidhi and Abu Dawud. He was from the tribe of Juhayna, served as governor of Egypt under Muawiyah, was skilled in archery and Quranic recitation, and narrated approximately 55 hadiths. He passed away in Egypt around 58 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 11 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Amr bin Dinar al-Makki was a prominent Successor scholar and jurist from Mecca. He narrated from the Companions Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, Jabir ibn Abdullah, and others (may Allah be pleased with them), and was one of the principal teachers of Imam al-Shafi'i's teacher, Ibn Uyayna. He was considered among the most reliable and widely respected narrators of his generation. His hadiths appear prominently in the Sahihayn and the Sunan collections. He passed away around 126 AH.
bio_en: Abu Bakra al-Thaqafi (RA) — various transliterations of this companion (Abu Bakra, AbuBakrah) all refer to the same person documented under entries 27 and 95. Nufay ibn al-Harith descended from the walls of Ta'if on a pulley to join the Muslims, was freed by the Prophet ﷺ, settled in Basra, and narrated approximately 132 hadiths. He is particularly known for the narration forbidding Muslims from fighting each other in civil strife. He passed away around 51–52 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Amr bin Dinar adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 11 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ibn Juraij (Abd al-Malik ibn Abd al-Aziz ibn Juraij) was a major Successor scholar from Mecca and one of the early systematizers of hadith collection. He was among the first scholars to compile hadiths in written form and narrated from Ata ibn Abi Rabah, Amr ibn Dinar, and Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri among others. He is considered a foundational figure in the development of Islamic jurisprudence in the Meccan school. He passed away around 150 AH.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA) — this entry 'Abdullah bin Abbas' (without the RA marker) is the same companion documented under entries 5, 16, 21, and 96. He was the cousin of the Prophet ﷺ, the foremost Quranic exegete among the companions, and narrated over 1,660 hadiths. His knowledge was so vast that the companions themselves — despite being his seniors — sought his counsel on difficult questions of tafsir and law. He passed away around 68 AH in Ta'if.
bio_short_id: Ibn Juraij adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 11 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Uqba ibn Amir al-Juhani (RA) was a Companion who embraced Islam in the early Medinan period and participated in numerous expeditions. He is known for his expertise in archery — the Prophet ﷺ encouraged Muslims to learn archery and Uqba was particularly skilled. He served as the governor of Egypt during the caliphate of Muawiya (RA) from 44–47 AH. He narrated approximately 55 hadiths relating to prayer, archery, conduct, and the virtues of reciting the Quran. He died in Egypt around 58 AH and is buried there.
bio_en: Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i — this entry is an alternate appearance of the same Tabi'i scholar documented under entry 85, Amr ibn Abdullah al-Hamdani al-Sabi'i of Kufa. He narrated from numerous companions including Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Bara ibn Azib, and Zaid ibn Arqam, and was the teacher of Sufyan al-Thawri and Shu'ba. He was considered highly reliable in hadith criticism. He passed away around 127–129 AH in Kufa.
bio_short_id: Uqbah bin Amir adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, Al-Quran, dan ibadah lainnya. Ia menjadi gubernur Mesir.

bio_short_en: Al-Bara ibn Azib (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who embraced Islam as a youth in Medina and participated in many military campaigns. He narrated numerous hadiths on topics including prayer, the funeral rites, the Quran, and the Prophet's physical description and conduct. He lived to a considerable age and continued transmitting prophetic knowledge to the scholars of the next generation. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections where he is regarded as a reliable and sincere narrator.
bio_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her), known by the kunya Umm Abdillah, was the daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq and wife of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. She was born in Mecca and was among the earliest members of the Muslim community. She married the Prophet ﷺ in Medina and lived with him until his death, during which time she accumulated an incomparable depth of knowledge about his daily conduct, private worship, and personal character. She narrated over two thousand hadiths, making her one of the seven most prolific Companion narrators, and her reports are particularly authoritative on matters of the Prophet's household life, purification, prayer, and women's affairs. After the Prophet's passing, she became a primary religious authority for the Muslim community. Companions and Tabi'in traveled to consult her on religious rulings, and she was known to correct narrations she believed were inaccurate. She issued fatwas on a vast range of topics and was considered by many to be the most learned of all the Companions in Islamic law, Quranic interpretation, Arabic poetry, and medicine. Among her most important students were Urwa ibn al-Zubayr, her nephew, and Amra bint Abd al-Rahman. She passed away in Medina in 58 AH at approximately sixty-six years of age and is buried in al-Baqi cemetery.
bio_short_id: Al-Bara' bin Azib adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang peperangan dan ibadah. Ia mengikuti banyak ekspedisi militer bersama Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her) was the beloved wife of the Prophet Muhammad and one of the greatest scholars of Islam. She narrated over two thousand hadiths and was a primary source of knowledge about the Prophet's private life, worship, and character. After the Prophet's passing, she became a leading teacher and jurist, with Companions and Successors traveling to consult her on religious matters. She passed away in Medina in 58 AH at approximately sixty-six years of age.
bio_en: Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah al-Thaqafi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who embraced Islam before the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, around 5–6 AH. His full name was al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah ibn Abi Amir al-Thaqafi, and he was from the tribe of Thaqif in Ta'if. He was known for his extraordinary intelligence, sharp diplomatic instincts, and skill in negotiation, earning him the nickname al-Mughirah al-Dahiya (the cunning one). He participated in major military campaigns and served as governor of Basra under Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab and later of Kufa under Uthman and Muawiya. He narrated approximately 136 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on topics including the wiping over leather socks during ablution, the prayers at the graveyard, and various aspects of the Prophet's worship and conduct. A famous narration preserved in Bukhari and Muslim describes in detail the Prophet's habit of wiping over his footwear during wudu, which became a foundational reference in Islamic jurisprudence. He passed away in Kufa around 50 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of the narrator also listed as Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih.

bio_short_en: Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah (may Allah be pleased with him) was a distinguished Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who embraced Islam before the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. He was a gifted diplomat and military commander who served as governor of Basra and Kufa. He narrated numerous hadiths and was known for his intelligence and political skill. He is cited in the major hadith collections for reports on prayer, the Sunnah, and the conduct of the Prophet. He passed away around 50 AH. This is a variant spelling of Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba.
bio_en: An-Nu'man ibn Bashir al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina. He was born approximately two years before the Hijra and was brought as an infant to the Prophet ﷺ by his parents, making him among the youngest Companions. His father Bashir ibn Sa'd was a Companion who participated in the Battle of Badr. An-Nu'man grew up in Medina within the prophetic community and narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ including what is considered one of the most foundational traditions in Islamic ethics: the hadith on the halal and haram being clear and the importance of avoiding doubtful matters, which forms the basis of the legal principle of ihtiyat (precaution). He later served as a governor of Kufa and Homs under the Umayyad Caliph Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan. He was known for his knowledge of Islamic law and his role as an administrator. He was martyred around 65 AH during the upheaval following the death of Yazid ibn Muawiya. His narrations are cited across the major hadith collections, particularly Bukhari, Muslim, and the Sunan works.
bio_short_id: Al-Mughirah bin Syu'bah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal atas kecerdikan dan kemampuan diplomatiknya. Ia menjabat sebagai gubernur Basra dan Kufah.

bio_short_en: An-Nu'man ibn Bashir (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad and one of the youngest among them. His father Bashir ibn Sa'd was a Companion who participated in the Battle of Badr, and he was brought to the Prophet as an infant. He later served as governor of Kufa and Homs. He is most famous for narrating the foundational hadith: 'The halal is clear and the haram is clear, and between them are doubtful matters.' He was martyred around 65 AH. This is a variant spelling of An-Nu'man bin Bashir.
bio_en: Wa'il ibn Hujr al-Hadrami (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from a noble lineage in Hadramawt, Yemen. He was a king among his people before embracing Islam, and when he came to the Prophet ﷺ, the Prophet received him warmly, spread his own cloak for him to sit on, and assigned him land in Yemen. Wa'il ibn Hujr is best known for narrating some of the most detailed and precise descriptions of the physical form of the Prophet's prayer, including the exact manner of raising the hands at the beginning of prayer, how the Prophet placed his right hand over his left, the way he pointed his index finger during tashahhud, and how he turned his head for the taslim at the end. These narrations are foundational references in Islamic jurisprudence for the detailed rulings of prayer posture and form, and different legal schools cite his reports when establishing their positions on these matters. He settled in Kufa after the Prophet's death and his descendants became notable scholars of hadith. His narrations are preserved in the Sunan of Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah.
bio_short_id: An-Nu'man bin Basyir adalah putra seorang Sahabat dan dirinya sendiri juga seorang Sahabat muda. Ia meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting termasuk hadith tentang halal dan haram.

bio_short_en: Wa'il ibn Hujr (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from a noble Hadrami family in Yemen. He was received warmly by the Prophet, who gave him land and sent Companions to accompany him back to Yemen. He narrated hadiths primarily on the physical description of the Prophet's prayer, including the placement of hands and the raising of the finger in tashahhud, which are foundational for Islamic prayer practice. He settled in Kufa and his descendants became notable scholars.
bio_en: Ka'b ibn Malik al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and one of the three Companions who remained behind from the Expedition of Tabuk without a valid excuse. His full account of repentance — how he and his two companions, Murarah ibn al-Rabi and Hilal ibn Umayyah, were boycotted by the Muslim community for fifty days until Allah revealed their forgiveness — is narrated at great length in Sahih al-Bukhari and is among the most detailed personal accounts preserved in the hadith literature. Ka'b was also a gifted poet who composed verse defending the Prophet ﷺ and the early Muslim community, a role the Prophet ﷺ encouraged. He participated in the Second Pledge of Aqabah and in the battles of Uhud, al-Khandaq, and many subsequent expeditions. He narrated approximately eighty hadiths on worship, the Prophet's biography, and ethical conduct. He is known for the Prophet's statement to him upon his repentance being accepted: 'This is the best day of your life since your mother gave birth to you.' He passed away in Medina around 50 AH.
bio_short_id: Wail bin Hujr adalah Sahabat Nabi dari Yaman yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith tentang tata cara shalat. Ia kembali ke Yaman dan mengajarkan Islam di sana.

bio_short_en: Ka'b bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) was a renowned Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina and one of the three Companions who remained behind from the Battle of Tabuk. His account of repentance and Allah's acceptance, narrated at length in Sahih al-Bukhari, is one of the most detailed stories in the hadith literature. He was also a poet who defended the Prophet through verse. He narrated many hadiths and passed away around 50 AH.
bio_en: Humaid al-Tawil ibn Abi Humaid al-Basri was a prominent Tabi'i hadith scholar of Basra. He was a freed slave (mawla) of Talha ibn Ubaydullah and was given the epithet 'al-Tawil' (the Tall) to distinguish him from others bearing the same name. He had extended and direct access to Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) for many years, having spent time in his company and narrated extensively from him. This proximity made him one of the most important transmitters of Anas's vast hadith corpus, and his narrations from Anas appear with great frequency in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. Imam al-Bukhari alone includes many chains going through Humaid on the authority of Anas, which scholars of rijal (narrator criticism) recognized as representing accurate and well-preserved transmission. He also narrated from other Tabi'in scholars of Basra. He was considered reliable (thiqa) by the major hadith critics including Yahya ibn Ma'in, Ali ibn al-Madini, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal. He passed away around 142–143 AH and is counted among the essential links in Basran hadith transmission.
bio_short_id: Ka'ab bin Malik adalah Sahabat Anshar yang terkenal dengan kisahnya yang tidak ikut dalam Perang Tabuk. Ia adalah seorang penyair ulung yang sering membela Islam melalui syairnya.

bio_short_en: Humaid al-Tawil (Humaid ibn Abi Humaid) was a Successor scholar from Basra who narrated extensively from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him), to whom he had direct access for many years. He is among the most important transmitters of Anas's hadiths and his narrations appear frequently in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He was considered reliable and trustworthy by hadith scholars. He passed away around 142-143 AH.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Dinar al-Madani was a Tabi'i hadith scholar and a freed slave (mawla) of Abdullah ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him). Having grown up in the household of Ibn Umar and served him closely, he became one of the most reliable transmitters of Ibn Umar's vast hadith corpus. His narrations from Ibn Umar appear prominently in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, and scholars of hadith criticism consistently rated him as thiqa (trustworthy) and dabut (precise). Imam Malik ibn Anas narrated from him extensively, and his reports form an important part of the Muwatta. He also narrated from Anas ibn Malik and other Companions. The chains running from Malik from Abdullah ibn Dinar from Ibn Umar are among the well-known Medinan transmission routes preserved in the major collections. He was based in Medina throughout his scholarly career and was deeply embedded in the circle of scholars who preserved the Sunnah in that city. He passed away around 127 AH and is regarded as an authoritative and trustworthy link between the Companion generation and the scholars of the second century of Islam.
bio_short_id: Humaid adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Dinar was a Successor scholar from Medina who was a freed slave (mawla) of Abdullah ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him). He narrated extensively from Ibn Umar, whose household he served, and his hadiths appear prominently in the Sahihayn. He was considered a highly reliable narrator by the scholars of hadith criticism and was a key transmitter of Medinan traditions. He passed away around 127 AH.
bio_en: This entry refers to hadiths transmitted jointly or in parallel chains by two distinguished Companions: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri and Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with them both). Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, whose full name was Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari, was a prolific Ansari Companion who narrated over one thousand hadiths, particularly on eschatology, purification, and Islamic conduct. Abu Huraira was the single most prolific narrator of hadith in the Companion generation, having memorized over five thousand traditions through his constant companionship with the Prophet ﷺ after embracing Islam in 7 AH. When hadiths are attributed to both together, it indicates that both Companions heard or witnessed the same statement or event, and both transmitted it. This dual transmission strengthens the authenticity of the narration and demonstrates the widespread preservation of the Sunnah across multiple Companion witnesses. Such joint narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, particularly in the chapters on prayer and purification. The combined testimony of two senior Companions from different backgrounds adds considerable weight to the reports they share.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Dinar adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

name_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Huraira
bio_short_en: This entry refers to hadiths narrated jointly or within the same chain by two prominent Companions: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri and Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with them both). Abu Sa'id was an Ansari Companion who narrated over one thousand hadiths, while Abu Huraira was the most prolific Companion narrator with over five thousand hadiths. When their names appear together, the hadith typically has a combined or parallel chain from both authorities, strengthening its authenticity.
bio_en: Asma is a name borne by several female Companions and early Muslim women. The most notable is Asma bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her), the elder daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq and elder sister of Aisha. She earned the celebrated title Dhat al-Nitaqayn (She of the Two Belts) because she tore her belt in two to tie up the provisions she carried to the Prophet ﷺ and her father during their concealment in the Cave of Thawr at the outset of the Hijra. Another prominent Asma is Asma bint Yazid al-Ansariyyah, a female Companion who narrated from the Prophet ﷺ on matters pertaining to women's religious practice. Without further context to identify which Asma is intended, this entry likely refers primarily to Asma bint Abi Bakr, who narrated approximately fifty-eight hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ and participated in the founding events of the Muslim community. She witnessed the early years of Islam in Mecca, the Hijra, the major events of Medina, and outlived most of her contemporaries. She passed away at nearly one hundred years of age shortly after the death of her son Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr in 73 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Huraira adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Asma is a name shared by several female Companions and early Muslim women. The most prominent is Asma bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her), daughter of the first Caliph Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, who earned the title Dhat al-Nitaqayn (the one of the two belts) for her role during the Hijra. She narrated hadiths from the Prophet and from her father, and lived to nearly one hundred years. Another is Asma bint Yazid, a Companion who narrated from the Prophet on matters affecting women.
bio_en: Asma bint Abi Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with her) was the elder daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, the elder sister of Aisha, and one of the most notable female Companions of the Prophet ﷺ. She was born approximately twenty-seven years before the Hijra and embraced Islam among the earliest converts in Mecca. She earned the famous epithet Dhat al-Nitaqayn (She of the Two Belts) for tearing her own belt in two in order to tie the provisions she carried to the Prophet ﷺ and her father Abu Bakr while they hid in the Cave of Thawr during the Hijra — one of the most pivotal moments in early Islamic history. She married al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA), one of the ten companions promised Paradise, and their son Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr later became a significant figure in Islamic history. Asma narrated approximately fifty-eight hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ covering prayer, fasting, charity, and household conduct. She was known for her generosity and her dignified bearing under hardship. She witnessed the death of her son Abdullah in 73 AH and passed away herself shortly after at an advanced age of nearly one hundred years, having retained all her faculties to the end.
bio_short_id: Asma' binti Abu Bakr adalah putri Khalifah Abu Bakr dan saudari Aisyah. Ia dikenal atas keberanian dan keteguhannya, serta meriwayatkan beberapa hadith penting.

bio_short_en: Asma bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad and the daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, the first Caliph. She earned the renowned title Dhat al-Nitaqayn (She of the Two Belts) for splitting her belt to provide food for the Prophet and her father during their migration to Medina. She narrated hadiths from the Prophet and lived to nearly one hundred years, witnessing the era of her son Abdullah ibn al-Zubair. She passed away in 73 AH.
bio_en: Jarir ibn Abdullah al-Bajali (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the tribe of Bajila in Yemen who embraced Islam in 10 AH, the Year of Delegations, near the end of the prophetic period. Despite his late conversion, he was honored greatly by the Prophet ﷺ, who was reported to have smiled whenever he saw Jarir and described him as resembling the prophets of the Bani Isra'il in appearance. Shortly after his conversion, the Prophet ﷺ sent him on a mission to destroy the idol Dhu al-Khalasa in Yemen, which Jarir accomplished with speed and efficiency, an action the Prophet ﷺ prayed for him in return. He narrated approximately 114 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ, among the most famous of which is the tradition on nasihah (sincere counsel) — that religion is sincere counsel to Allah, His Book, His Messenger, the leaders of the Muslims, and the general community. He settled in Kufa and became one of the respected elder Companions there. He was known for his loyalty, his noble character, and his commitment to the Sunnah. He passed away in Kufa around 51–54 AH.
bio_short_id: Asma' binti Abu Bakr adalah putri Khalifah Abu Bakr dan saudari Aisyah. Ia dikenal atas keberanian dan keteguhannya.

bio_short_en: Jarir bin Abdullah al-Bajali (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who embraced Islam in 10 AH, near the end of the prophetic era. The Prophet praised his handsome appearance and his goodness. He was sent by the Prophet to destroy the idol Dhu al-Khalasa in Yemen. He settled in Kufa and narrated many hadiths, including the famous tradition on the obligation to advise and show sincerity to Muslim leaders. He passed away around 51-54 AH.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abi Qatada al-Ansari was a Tabi'i scholar and the son of the famous Companion Abu Qatada al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), one of the Prophet's most celebrated horsemen known for his courage in battle. Growing up as the son of a distinguished Companion in Medina, Abdullah had direct access to his father's extensive store of prophetic knowledge and transmitted many hadiths from him. His father Abu Qatada narrated approximately 170 hadiths covering prayer, fasting, hunting, and many other aspects of Islamic life, and Abdullah became one of the primary chains through whom those narrations reached the wider scholarly community. He also narrated from other Companions he met in Medina. His transmission of Abu Qatada's report on the manner in which the Prophet ﷺ carried his granddaughter Umama during prayer — remaining calm and composed while holding an infant — is among his most well-known narrations and is cited in discussions of what invalidates prayer. He is considered a reliable Tabi'i narrator by scholars of hadith criticism, and his reports serve as an important bridge between the Companion generation and the Tabi'in scholars.
bio_short_id: Jarir bin Abdullah al-Bajali adalah Sahabat Nabi dari kabilah Bajilah. Ia masuk Islam dan dikirim Nabi ﷺ untuk menghancurkan berhala Dzul Khalasah.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Abi Qatada was a Successor scholar and the son of the Companion Abu Qatada al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), one of the Prophet's horsemen known for his bravery in battle. Abdullah narrated hadiths from his father and other Companions, transmitting traditions related to prayer, fasting, and the conduct of the Prophet. His reports serve as an important link between the Companion generation and the Successor scholars.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Mughaffal al-Muzani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the Muzayna tribe and one of the distinguished members of the Ahl al-Suffa — the poor Companions who lived near the mosque of Medina, dedicating themselves entirely to learning the religion and serving the Prophet ﷺ. He was among those present at the Treaty of al-Hudaybiyyah and participated in the Conquest of Mecca. On the day of the conquest, he is known to have been holding the curtain of the Ka'ba when the Prophet ﷺ entered it, a detail preserved in narrations describing the opening of Mecca. He narrated approximately eighty hadiths on topics including prayer, purification, the recitation of Bismillah before meals, and the prohibition of urinating in standing water. He is particularly known for narrations on Islamic etiquette surrounding food and domestic conduct. He settled in Basra after the Prophet's death and became one of the important Companion authorities there. He died around 57–60 AH. His narrations are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Abi Qatada adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Mughaffal al-Muzani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad and one of the distinguished members of the Ahl al-Suffa, the poor Companions who lived in the mosque of Medina. He participated in the conquest of Mecca and was among those who carried the banner during battles. He narrated hadiths on prayer, purification, and Islamic etiquette, and later settled in Basra. He passed away around 57-60 AH.
bio_en: Uqba ibn al-Harith (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who embraced Islam at the Conquest of Mecca. He was from the Quraysh tribe and narrated a relatively small number of hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ. Among the most notable narrations attributed to him is a report preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari concerning a legal case involving breastfeeding (rada'a). A woman claimed that she had suckled Uqba and a woman he wished to marry, which would make them milk-siblings and the marriage impermissible. The Prophet ﷺ, upon hearing the claim, advised caution: 'How can you marry her when it has been said?' — indicating that such claims, even unverified, should make one cautious in matters affecting family law. This narration became a reference point in discussions of the conditions and legal weight of breastfeeding claims in Islamic jurisprudence. His reports are found in the Sunan collections and cited in chapters on family law and marriage. He is considered a reliable Companion narrator for the hadiths he transmitted.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Mughaffal adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Uqba bin Al-Harith (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from Mecca who embraced Islam at the conquest. He narrated a relatively small number of hadiths, among the most notable being a report related to the prohibition of intoxicants following an incident where a man claimed a certain woman had suckled him and the man he sought to marry her to. His reports appear in the Sunan collections and are cited in matters of Islamic family law.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) was among the very first to embrace Islam in Mecca, doing so while still a young shepherd boy. His full name was Abdullah ibn Mas'ud ibn Ghafil al-Hudhali. He was so close to the Prophet ﷺ that he was permitted to enter the Prophet's quarters without prior permission, carried his sandals and prepared his water for ghusl, and was described as resembling the Prophet ﷺ in his walk and bearing. The Prophet ﷺ declared: 'Whoever wishes to recite the Quran as fresh as when it was revealed, let him recite it as Ibn Umm Abd recites it.' He was appointed by Umar ibn al-Khattab as a teacher and judge in Kufa, where he became the principal scholarly authority for the Iraqi tradition that later developed into the Hanafi school of jurisprudence. He narrated 848 hadiths and is recognized as one of the four Companions most authoritative on Quranic recitation and tafsir. He resisted any change to his own mushaf when the standard Uthmanic copy was compiled and remained firm on matters he was convinced about. He passed away in Medina around 32–33 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Uqba bin Al-Harith adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA) was one of the earliest converts to Islam, embracing the faith while still a young man in Makkah. He was so close to the Prophet ﷺ that he was given permission to enter the Prophet's home without seeking permission and described as one who resembled the Prophet ﷺ most closely in his walk and conduct. He narrated 848 hadiths and was appointed by Umar (RA) as a teacher and judge in Kufa. He is particularly renowned for his Quranic recitation and is considered one of the four great authorities on the Quran among the Companions.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and one of the most prolific narrators among the Companions, transmitting approximately 1,540 hadiths. His father, Abdullah ibn Amr ibn Haram, was martyred at the Battle of Uhud. Jabir participated in nineteen military expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ and was present at the Second Pledge of Aqabah, where his father was one of the twelve Ansari leaders who pledged allegiance. He lived to an advanced age — reportedly losing his eyesight near the end of his life — which allowed him to transmit prophetic knowledge directly to major Tabi'in scholars including Ata ibn Abi Rabah and Muhammad ibn al-Munkadir. His detailed narration of the Prophet's Farewell Pilgrimage is one of the most comprehensive preserved accounts of the rites of Hajj and remains a foundational reference. He was the last surviving Companion in Medina, passing away around 73–78 AH. This entry is a variant listing of Jabir ibn Abdullah and refers to the same Companion.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Masud adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Jabir bin Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from Medina who participated in nineteen military expeditions. His father Abdullah ibn Amr was martyred at Uhud. Jabir narrated approximately one thousand five hundred hadiths and is known for detailed accounts of the Prophet's Farewell Pilgrimage, acts of worship, and daily conduct. He passed away in Medina around 73-78 AH and was the last surviving Companion in that city.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, the son of Samurah ibn Jundab and nephew of Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, one of the ten promised Paradise. He grew up in close proximity to the Prophet ﷺ and directly witnessed his conduct, prayer, and sermons. He narrated approximately one hundred and forty hadiths covering a wide range of topics, most notably detailed descriptions of the Prophet's physical appearance — his build, his hair, his face, and his manner of walking. He is particularly known for a narration describing the Prophet's Friday sermon, delivered while standing at the pulpit, with the congregation seated around him — a description foundational to the practice of the khutba. He also narrated accounts of the Last Hour, including the Prophet's statement about twelve caliphs from Quraysh. He settled in Kufa where he was regarded as a respected elder Companion and reliable transmitter. He passed away around 74 AH. His narrations appear in Sahih Muslim, the Sunan of Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin 'Abdullah (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 10 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Jabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad, the son of Samurah ibn Jundab and nephew of Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas. He narrated approximately one hundred and forty hadiths and is known particularly for his narrations describing the physical appearance of the Prophet, accounts of Friday prayer, and the signs of the Hour. He settled in Kufa and was considered a reliable narrator. He passed away around 74 AH.
bio_en: Salman al-Farsi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a remarkable Companion of the Prophet ﷺ whose spiritual journey to Islam was one of the most extraordinary in early Muslim history. Born in a Persian Zoroastrian family in Isfahan, he abandoned his ancestral religion after witnessing a Zoroastrian priest in prayer and feeling drawn to monotheism. He then passed through years of service among Christian monks in Syria and Iraq, each dying guide directing him toward a prophet to come in Arabia. After being enslaved and then freed through the Prophet's help in Medina, he was accepted into the Muslim community and the Prophet ﷺ honored him with the words: 'Salman is from us, the People of the House.' His suggestion during the Battle of the Trench to dig a defensive ditch — a Persian military strategy — was adopted by the Prophet ﷺ and proved decisive. He narrated approximately sixty hadiths and became one of the most spiritually revered Companions, known for his profound wisdom and asceticism. He later served as governor of al-Mada'in (Ctesiphon) in Iraq. He passed away around 36 AH.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin Samurah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith, termasuk hadith-hadith tentang sifat fisik Nabi ﷺ dan tentang para khalifah.

bio_short_en: Salman al-Farsi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who had journeyed from Persia in search of the true religion, passing through Christianity before reaching Islam. The Prophet said about him: 'Salman is from us, the People of the House.' He suggested digging the trench during the Battle of the Trench, an innovative military strategy that proved decisive. He served as governor of al-Mada'in and was known for his profound knowledge and asceticism. He passed away around 36 AH.
bio_en: Ubadah ibn al-Samit al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a senior Companion from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and one of the twelve representatives (nuqaba) chosen at the Second Pledge of Aqabah before the Hijra, demonstrating his early and prominent standing in the Ansari community. He participated in the Battle of Badr and all subsequent major campaigns and was among the most learned of the Companions in Quranic studies and the Sunnah. He is known for transmitting one of the most foundational hadiths in Islamic jurisprudence: the conditions of the testimony of faith and its requirements, as well as hadiths on the prohibition of riba (usury) and the importance of reciting Surah al-Fatiha in every unit of prayer. He served as a judge and teacher in Palestine after the Islamic conquest of the Levant and was among the first Muslim educators in that region. He narrated approximately 181 hadiths. He passed away in Palestine around 34 AH and is buried there. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and all the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Salman al-Farisi adalah Sahabat Nabi dari Persia yang terkenal dengan kisah perjalanan spiritualnya mencari kebenaran. Ia mengemukakan ide parit dalam Perang Khandaq.

bio_short_en: Ubadah ibn as-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina and one of the twelve representatives (nuqaba) selected at the Second Pledge of Aqaba. He participated in the Battle of Badr and transmitted important hadiths on the conditions of the shahada, the obligation of prayer, and the prohibition of usury. He served as a judge in Palestine and narrated hadiths that form foundational principles in Islamic jurisprudence. He passed away around 34 AH.
bio_en: Buraidah ibn al-Husayb al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the tribe of Aslam. He embraced Islam when the Prophet ﷺ passed through his territory during the Hijra to Medina, and he and his entire tribe converted as a group, making his conversion one of the early collective acceptances of Islam. He participated in many military expeditions and served the Prophet ﷺ faithfully. He narrated approximately 164 hadiths on topics including prayer and its times, the rules of jihad and the treatment of prisoners, and general conduct. He is particularly known for the hadith on reciting specific supplications before going to sleep and for narrations on proper conduct in warfare. After the Prophet's death, he settled in Marw (in present-day Turkmenistan), becoming one of the first to bring the Sunnah to the eastern regions of the expanding Islamic world. He became a major religious authority in Khurasan and his presence there played an important role in establishing Islamic learning in that region. He passed away in Marw around 62–63 AH.
bio_short_id: Ubadah bin ash-Shamit adalah Sahabat Anshar yang termasuk di antara pemimpin Bai'at Aqabah. Ia meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting dan menjadi hakim di Palestina.

bio_short_en: Buraidah ibn al-Husayb al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the tribe of Aslam. He embraced Islam when the Prophet passed through his territory during the Hijra to Medina. He narrated numerous hadiths on jihad, prayer, and the conduct of the Prophet, and his reports are found in all major hadith collections. He settled in Marw (in present-day Turkmenistan) where he became a religious authority and passed away around 62-63 AH.
bio_en: Rafi ibn Khadij al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina. He initially requested permission to participate in the Battle of Uhud and was turned away by the Prophet ﷺ for being too young, but insisted and was eventually permitted after demonstrating his ability. He participated in numerous campaigns thereafter. He narrated approximately 78 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ, and is particularly well known for his narrations on agricultural share-cropping arrangements (muzara'a and mukhabara) — specifically the prohibition of certain contractual forms involving uncertain shares of produce. These narrations became fundamental references in Islamic commercial law, and the various schools of jurisprudence relied on his reports when formulating their rulings on land-lease and share-farming contracts. He lived until approximately 74 AH in Medina. This entry is a variant spelling of the narrator listed separately as Rafi bin Khadij, and both refer to the same Companion.
bio_short_id: Buraidah bin al-Hushaib adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, puasa, dan jihad. Ia tinggal di Marw pada masa tuanya.

bio_short_en: Rafi ibn Khadij (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina who participated in numerous battles. He is particularly known for narrating hadiths related to agricultural share-cropping arrangements and the conditions under which they are permissible or prohibited. His reports are fundamental in Islamic commercial law. He lived until approximately 74 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of Rafi bin Khadij.
bio_en: Samurah ibn Jundab al-Fazari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the Fazara tribe. Like al-Bara ibn Azib, he was initially considered too young to participate in battles during the early years of Islam but was eventually permitted after the Prophet ﷺ recognized his maturity. He participated in several expeditions and later served as a governor of Basra during the caliphate of Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan. He narrated approximately 128 hadiths. Among his most famous narrations is the detailed account of the Prophet's long dream in which the Prophet was shown scenes of various people undergoing the consequences of their deeds — including those who neglected the Quran and those who committed sins — a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari. He also narrated important hadiths on prayer times, the eclipse prayer, and prophetic supplications. Some scholars noted caution regarding a small number of his narrations, but he is generally accepted as a reliable Companion narrator. He passed away around 58–60 AH.
bio_short_id: Rafi' bin Khadij adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith tentang pertanian, transaksi, dan ibadah.

bio_short_en: Samurah bin Jundab (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who narrated a number of hadiths on prayer, dreams, and prophetic conduct. He served as governor of Basra during the caliphate of Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan and was known as an administrator. He is cited in the major hadith collections for traditions including the Prophet's account of a long dream describing the states of various people in the afterlife. He passed away around 58-60 AH.
bio_en: Abu Qilaba al-Jarmi, whose full name was Abdullah ibn Zayd al-Azdi, was a prominent Tabi'i hadith scholar from Basra. He narrated from Anas ibn Malik, Malik ibn al-Huwayrith, Thabit ibn al-Dahhak, and other Companions (may Allah be pleased with them), and was known for his deep commitment to the Sunnah and his cautious approach to issuing legal opinions. He deliberately relocated to Syria at one point to distance himself from the political and doctrinal controversies of his time in Iraq, reflecting his principled stance on remaining neutral in civil strife. He is cited in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim for hadiths on prayer, purification, and Islamic conduct. Imam Ayyub al-Sakhtiyani, himself a leading Tabi'i scholar, spoke highly of Abu Qilaba and considered him among the reliable scholars of his generation. The scholars of hadith criticism unanimously considered him thiqa (trustworthy). He passed away around 104–107 AH in Syria. His narrations are found in all the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Samurah bin Jundab adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 9 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Qilaba al-Jarmi (Abdullah ibn Zayd al-Azdi) was a prominent Successor scholar from Basra who narrated from Anas ibn Malik, Malik ibn al-Huwayrith, and other Companions. He was known for his strong adherence to the Sunnah and his caution in narrating hadiths. He eventually relocated to Syria to distance himself from the political turbulence of his era. He is cited in the Sahihayn and Sunan for hadiths on prayer, purification, and Islamic conduct. He passed away around 104-107 AH.
bio_en: Hakim ibn Hizam al-Asadi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ with the remarkable distinction of being the only person in history known to have been born inside the Ka'ba. He was the nephew of Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, the first wife of the Prophet ﷺ, and was fifteen years older than the Prophet himself. He grew up among the Quraysh and was known before Islam for his extraordinary generosity, having freed one hundred slaves and provided for great numbers of pilgrims. He embraced Islam at the Conquest of Mecca in 8 AH. Despite his late conversion, the Prophet ﷺ treated him with great respect and gave him one hundred camels on the Day of Hunayn from the spoils — making him one of those referred to as al-Mu'allafa qulubuhum (those whose hearts were being reconciled). After his conversion he continued his tradition of generosity, eventually freeing another hundred slaves in Islam. He narrated approximately sixty hadiths, particularly on commercial ethics and the principles of honest dealing. He passed away in Medina around 54–60 AH at a very advanced age.
bio_short_id: Abu Qilabah al-Jarmi adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Basra yang bertemu banyak Sahabat Nabi. Ia dikenal atas ketaqwaannya dan penguasaan hadith.

bio_short_en: Hakim bin Hizam (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who had the distinction of being born inside the Ka'ba, making him the only person in history known to have been born in that sacred place. He was the nephew of Khadijah, the Prophet's first wife. He embraced Islam at the conquest of Mecca and went on to perform extensive acts of charity and freeing of slaves. He narrated hadiths on commerce and generosity and passed away around 54-60 AH at a very advanced age.
bio_en: Ibn Abi Mulaika, whose full name was Abdullah ibn Ubaydullah ibn Abi Mulaika al-Tamimi al-Makhzumi, was a prominent Tabi'i scholar of Mecca. He served as a judge under the Caliph Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr, as a muezzin for the Masjid al-Haram, and as a prayer leader — three significant positions that attest to his standing in the community. He narrated from Aisha, Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, Ibn al-Zubayr, and numerous other senior Companions, making him one of the most connected Tabi'i scholars to the Companion generation. He was regarded as highly reliable (thiqa) by the scholars of hadith, and Imam al-Bukhari and Imam Muslim both relied on him extensively in their Sahih collections. His narrations cover a wide range of topics including the description of the Day of Judgment, matters of prayer, and reports from the Prophet's household. He passed away around 117 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of the narrator also listed as Ibn Abi Mulaika.
bio_short_id: Hakim bin Hizam adalah keponakan Khadijah, istri pertama Nabi, dan masuk Islam pada hari penaklukan Mekah. Ia dikenal atas kedermawanannya yang luar biasa.

bio_short_en: Ibn Abu Mulaika (Abdullah ibn Ubaydullah ibn Abi Mulaika) was a Successor scholar from Mecca who served as a judge under Caliph Ibn al-Zubair and as a muezzin and prayer leader. He narrated from Aisha, Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, and other senior Companions (may Allah be pleased with them), and was regarded as a highly reliable narrator. His hadiths appear prominently in the Sahihayn. He passed away around 117 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of Ibn Abi Mulaika.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abi Aufa al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him), also referred to as Ibn Abi Aufa, was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the Aslam tribe. He was present at the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and gave his pledge of allegiance under the tree (bay'at al-ridwan), a distinction of great honor. He participated in numerous battles including Khaybar and the Conquest of Mecca. He settled in Kufa and became the last surviving Companion in that city, living to a very advanced age which made him an invaluable direct link to the prophetic era for the scholars of Iraq. His narrations include hadiths on prayer, charitable giving, and the conduct of the Prophet ﷺ in military campaigns. He is particularly known for narrations about the takbir (the opening call) in the prayer for the deceased and the rules of voluntary charity. He passed away around 86–87 AH in Kufa. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Ibn Abu Mulaika adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 9 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ibn Abi Aufa (Abdullah ibn Abi Aufa al-Aslami, may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who participated in the Battle of Hudaybiyyah and numerous other expeditions. He was among the last Companions to pass away in Kufa, living to a very advanced age, and was considered the last surviving Companion in that city. He narrated hadiths on prayer, charity, and the Prophet's conduct in battle. He passed away around 86-87 AH.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Abi Aufa al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the tribe of Aslam who participated in the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and pledged allegiance under the tree. His participation in Hudaybiyyah placed him among those specifically mentioned in Quranic verse 48:18 with Allah's pleasure: 'Allah was pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree.' He participated in six military expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ and settled in Kufa after the Prophet's passing, where he became the last surviving Companion in the city. He narrated hadiths on prayer, the funeral prayer, charitable giving, and the Prophet's military conduct. The scholars of Kufa and subsequent generations sought his narrations for their directness to the prophetic source. His reports appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections. He passed away in Kufa around 86–87 AH. This entry is a fuller listing of the same Companion also recorded as Ibn Abi Aufa.
bio_short_id: Abdullah bin Abi Aufa adalah Sahabat Nabi dari kabilah Aslam. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan menjadi salah satu Sahabat terakhir yang meninggal di Kufah.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Abi Aufa al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who participated in the Battle of Hudaybiyyah and gave the pledge under the tree. He settled in Kufa, where he became the last surviving Companion in the city, living well into old age. He narrated hadiths on prayer, war, and the conduct of the Prophet, and his reports appear in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He passed away around 86-87 AH. This is a fuller version of the Ibn Abi Aufa entry.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud al-Hudhali (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the earliest converts to Islam, embracing the faith in Mecca as a young shepherd boy who worked for Uqba ibn Abi Mu'ayt. The Prophet ﷺ permitted him to enter his private quarters without prior permission and described him as one whose guidance should be followed, saying: 'Hold fast to the guidance of Ibn Umm Abd.' He was the Prophet's personal attendant in private matters and one of the first to recite the Quran openly in Mecca, enduring the beatings of the Quraysh with patience. He participated in the Battle of Badr and all major campaigns. He narrated approximately 848 hadiths and was appointed by Umar ibn al-Khattab as a teacher and judge in Kufa, where he spent decades shaping the Iraqi scholarly tradition that eventually gave rise to the Hanafi school. He is considered one of the four Companions most authoritative on the Quran and recitation. He passed away in Medina around 32–33 AH. This entry is a variant listing of the same Companion.
bio_short_id: Abdullah bin Abi Aufa adalah Sahabat Nabi dari kabilah Aslam. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan menjadi salah satu Sahabat terakhir yang meninggal di Kufah.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the most eminent Companions of the Prophet Muhammad and among the earliest to embrace Islam. He was the Prophet's close personal attendant, trusted to carry his sandals and prepare his bath. He was a master of Quranic recitation and is known for his own mushaf. He became the primary religious scholar and judge in Kufa, where thousands of students learned from him. He narrated approximately eight hundred hadiths. He passed away around 32-33 AH.
bio_en: Anas ibn Malik al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who served him personally for ten years in Medina, brought to that role by his mother Umm Sulaym when the Prophet ﷺ arrived in Medina after the Hijra. Anas was approximately ten years old at the time. The Prophet ﷺ made a special supplication for him: 'O Allah, give him wealth and children and bless him therein,' a supplication Anas later said was answered manifestly. He narrated over two thousand hadiths and is counted among the seven most prolific Companion narrators. His narrations provide an intimate record of the Prophet's daily conduct, worship, manners, and appearance. After the Prophet's death, he settled in Basra where he remained as a major scholarly authority for decades, becoming the last surviving Companion in that city. Thousands of students traveled to Basra to transmit hadiths from him. He lived to approximately ninety or more years of age, passing away around 91–93 AH. This entry is a variant listing of the same Companion.
bio_short_id: Ibn Mas'ud (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 9 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Anas bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion who served the Prophet Muhammad for ten years in Medina, beginning when he was about ten years old. He was the personal servant of the Prophet and narrated over two thousand hadiths, making him one of the most prolific Companion narrators. He was the last surviving Companion in Basra, where he settled and taught for decades. His narrations cover virtually every aspect of the Prophet's daily life and practice. He passed away around 91-93 AH.
bio_en: Zayd ibn Arqam al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina who participated in seventeen military expeditions. He is renowned for his narration of the Hadith al-Thaqalayn — the Prophet's statement at the pond of Ghadir Khumm: 'I leave among you two weighty things: the Book of Allah and my household.' This narration, which emphasizes the importance of the Quran and the Prophet's family, is preserved in Sahih Muslim and the Sunan and has been the subject of extensive scholarly commentary. He also narrated the famous hadith on the signs distinguishing sincere du'a and the timing of accepted supplications. He transmitted many reports on theological questions, the merits of the Companions, and practical religious rulings. He settled in Kufa where he was held in high esteem, and continued to teach until late in life. He passed away around 65–68 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of the narrator also listed as Zaid bin Arqam.
bio_short_id: Anas bin Malik (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 9 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina who participated in seventeen military expeditions. He is renowned for narrating the hadith of Ghadir Khumm in which the Prophet spoke about the Quran and his household. He transmitted many hadiths on theology, prayer, and the Companions' merits, and settled in Kufa. He passed away around 65-68 AH. This is a variant spelling of Zaid bin Arqam.
bio_en: Anas ibn Malik al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was the personal servant of the Prophet ﷺ for ten years and one of the most prolific Companion narrators with over two thousand hadiths. His mother Umm Sulaym brought him to serve the Prophet ﷺ when the Prophet arrived in Medina, and from that point Anas remained in close proximity to him for the remainder of the prophetic era. The Prophet ﷺ said of him: 'O Allah, increase his wealth and his children and bless him in what You give him.' His narrations cover virtually every dimension of the Prophet's daily life — how he ate, prayed, slept, spoke, and treated those around him. He settled in Basra after the Prophet's death and became the last surviving Companion there. His longevity allowed him to transmit the Sunnah to scholars who lived well into the second century of Islam, making him a bridge of extraordinary value. He passed away around 91–93 AH. This entry is a variant romanization of the name Anas ibn Malik.
bio_short_id: Zaid bin Arqam adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting termasuk tentang peristiwa Ghadir Khum dan sifat-sifat Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Anas b. Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) was among the most prolific narrators of hadith among the Companions, having served the Prophet Muhammad personally for ten years in Medina. He was entrusted by his mother Umm Sulaym to serve the Prophet when he arrived in Medina. His narrations provide intimate accounts of the Prophet's habits, worship, character, and daily life. He was the last Companion to pass away in Basra and died around 91-93 AH. This entry is a variant form of Anas bin Malik.
bio_en: Ubayy ibn Ka'b al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the most distinguished Companions of the Prophet ﷺ in Quranic knowledge and was among the scribes who recorded the Quranic revelation during the prophetic era. His full name was Ubayy ibn Ka'b ibn Qays al-Khazraji al-Ansari. The Prophet ﷺ specifically instructed his followers to learn the Quran from four Companions — one of whom was Ubayy ibn Ka'b. The Prophet himself recited the Quran to Ubayy and told him that Allah had commanded the Quran be recited to him by name, an honor that placed Ubayy in a singular position. He was the imam of Tarawih prayer in Medina during the caliphate of Umar ibn al-Khattab and is credited with unifying the Muslims behind a single imam in those prayers. He narrated hadiths on the Quran, prayer, and Islamic conduct, and his reports are found in the major Sunan collections. He passed away around 19–32 AH.
bio_short_id: Anas b. Malik adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the foremost Companions of the Prophet Muhammad in Quranic knowledge and recitation. He was one of the four Companions whom the Prophet specifically instructed his followers to learn the Quran from. He served as a scribe of the Quranic revelation and was the Imam of the Muslims in Medina in Tarawih prayer during the caliphate of Umar. He narrated hadiths on the Quran, worship, and the Prophet's character. He passed away around 19-30 AH.
bio_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her), wife of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and one of the Mothers of the Believers, was the most scholarly of the Prophet's wives and one of the leading authorities on prophetic traditions across all areas of Islamic knowledge. Married to the Prophet ﷺ in Medina, she lived in the prophetic household for approximately nine years, acquiring first-hand knowledge of his worship, character, and daily conduct that no other person could fully share. She narrated over two thousand hadiths — making her one of the seven most prolific Companion narrators — and her reports are particularly indispensable on topics that only a member of the household could observe, such as the Prophet's night prayer, his fasting, his physical appearance, and his domestic conduct. After the Prophet's death, she taught in Medina for nearly fifty years and was consulted by the foremost scholars of the Tabi'in generation. She passed away in 58 AH. This entry is an alternate descriptive listing for Aisha.
bio_short_id: Ubay bin Ka'ab adalah salah satu Sahabat ahli Al-Quran dan sekretaris wahyu Nabi ﷺ. Ia dikenal sebagai "Sayyid al-Qurra'" (Pemimpin Ahli Quran) di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her), wife of the Prophet Muhammad, was one of the greatest scholars and narrators in Islamic history. She narrated over two thousand hadiths and was the primary authority on the Prophet's private worship, domestic life, and personal conduct. Companions and Successors frequently consulted her on religious rulings and she issued fatwas on matters affecting women and the broader Muslim community. She passed away in Medina in 58 AH. This entry is an alternate descriptive form of Aisha.
bio_en: Abu Rafi' (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who served as his freed slave and personal attendant. His birth name is given variously as Aslam or Ibrahim, and he accepted Islam in Mecca. He was initially a slave of the Prophet's uncle al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib, who gave him to the Prophet ﷺ as a gift; the Prophet then freed him. Abu Rafi' lived in the Prophet's household and was entrusted with various tasks and missions, including acting as an emissary. His narrations reflect intimate knowledge of the Prophet's daily habits and household conduct. He is sometimes confused with other narrators sharing the kunya Abu Rafi'. His reports appear in the Sunan of Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah, and deal with topics including prayer, marital conduct, and the etiquette of serving food. He settled in Medina after the Prophet's death and transmitted traditions to the Tabi'i scholars who visited him. He is regarded as a reliable narrator whose proximity to the Prophet gave his narrations direct authority.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih.

bio_short_en: Abu Rafi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who served as his freed slave and close attendant. He is known for narrating hadiths about the Prophet's personal habits, household conduct, and acts of worship. He is sometimes confused with other narrators sharing the same kunya. His reports appear in the Sunan collections and are cited in matters of Islamic law and the Prophet's character. He settled in Medina and narrated traditions to the Successor generation.
bio_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas al-Zuhri (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the earliest converts to Islam and one of the ten Companions given the explicit glad tidings of Paradise by the Prophet ﷺ. His full name was Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Uhaib al-Zuhri, and he was a maternal uncle of the Prophet ﷺ through the Zuhra branch of Quraysh. He embraced Islam at approximately seventeen years of age, reportedly the third or sixth person to do so. He was one of the most skilled archers among the Companions and the first to shed blood in the path of Islam. He led the Muslim armies at the decisive Battle of al-Qadisiyyah in 15–16 AH, completing the conquest of Persia — one of the most consequential victories in Islamic history. He narrated approximately 271 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on faith, prayer, and sincere reliance on Allah. He was famous for the acceptance of his supplications, which the Prophet ﷺ prayed for specifically. He passed away in al-Aqiq near Medina around 55–58 AH. This is a variant listing of the Companion also recorded as Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas.
bio_short_id: Abu Rafi' adalah maula (budak yang dimerdekakan) Nabi Muhammad ﷺ yang setia. Ia meriwayatkan beberapa hadith dan tinggal bersama Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the ten Companions given the glad tidings of Paradise and among the earliest to embrace Islam, reportedly the third or sixth person to do so. He was a maternal uncle of the Prophet Muhammad and a skilled archer. He led the Muslim army in the decisive Battle of al-Qadisiyyah, completing the conquest of Persia, and became the first to shed blood in the cause of Islam. He narrated many hadiths and passed away around 55-58 AH.
bio_en: Ibrahim al-Nakha'i was a leading Successor (Tabi'i) scholar and jurist from Kufa whose legal methodology became the foundation upon which the Hanafi school of jurisprudence was built. He was born around 46–50 AH and was the nephew of the great scholar Alqama ibn Qays. He studied under senior Tabi'in scholars including al-Aswad ibn Yazid and Masruq, and also narrated from his aunt Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) and Anas ibn Malik. He was the principal teacher of Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman, who in turn taught Imam Abu Hanifa. Ibrahim was deeply learned in the legal positions of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud and the Kufan scholarly tradition, and his approach to legal reasoning — balancing hadith with principled analogy — shaped the subsequent development of Islamic fiqh. He was known for his cautious approach to fatwa and his unwillingness to narrate anything he was not certain of. He passed away in Kufa around 95–96 AH. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan works.
bio_short_id: Sa'd ibn AbuWaqqas adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ibrahim al-Nakha'i was a leading Successor scholar and jurist from Kufa, considered one of the most important authorities in the Kufan legal school that later developed into the Hanafi madhhab. He narrated from his aunt Aisha, from Anas ibn Malik, and from senior Successor scholars. He was the primary teacher of Hammad ibn Abi Sulayman, who in turn taught Imam Abu Hanifa. He was known for his deep legal knowledge and cautious approach to hadith narration. He passed away around 95-96 AH.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and one of the most prolific narrators among the Companions, transmitting approximately 1,540 hadiths. He was present at the Second Pledge of Aqabah and participated in many military campaigns. His father Abdullah ibn Amr ibn Haram was martyred at Uhud, and Jabir devoted himself to transmitting the Prophet's knowledge in his father's memory. His detailed account of the Farewell Pilgrimage is among the most comprehensive preserved records of the rites of Hajj, with precise descriptions of each action the Prophet ﷺ performed. He lived to an advanced age in Medina, losing his sight near the end of his life, and was considered the last surviving Companion in Medina. Major Tabi'in scholars including Ata ibn Abi Rabah and Muhammad ibn al-Munkadir traveled to him to transmit knowledge. This entry is a variant form of Jabir ibn Abdullah and refers to the same Companion. He passed away around 73–78 AH.
bio_short_id: Ibrahim an-Nakha'i adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah dan murid Alqamah dan Masruq. Ia adalah tokoh utama mazhab Kufah dalam fikih.

bio_short_en: Jabir b. Abd Allah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Khazraj tribe of Medina. He participated in many battles and narrated approximately one thousand five hundred hadiths covering worship, pilgrimage, commerce, and the Prophet's character. His detailed account of the Farewell Pilgrimage is among the most comprehensive preserved descriptions of the rites of Hajj. He passed away in Medina around 73-78 AH. This is a variant form of Jabir bin Abdullah.
bio_en: Ikrimah (may Allah have mercy on him) was a leading Tabi'i scholar and the freed slave (mawla) of Abdullah ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him). Born into slavery, he was acquired by Ibn Abbas as a young man and trained under him in Quranic exegesis and Islamic sciences for many years. He memorized an enormous corpus of tafsir narrations from Ibn Abbas, reportedly having read the entire Quran to him multiple times and questioned him on every verse. He became one of the foremost authorities on Quranic interpretation of his generation and traveled widely — to North Africa, Khurasan, and other regions — to teach and transmit hadith. Some early hadith scholars, including Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, expressed reservations about certain of his positions, but the majority of the major hadith critics including Imam al-Bukhari, Yahya ibn Ma'in, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal accepted him as reliable. His narrations appear in all six of the major hadith collections. He passed away around 105–107 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of the narrator also listed as Ikrima.
bio_short_id: Jabir b. 'Abd Allah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ikrimah was a leading Successor (Tabi'i) and the freed slave of Abdullah ibn Abbas (RA), from whom he learned the Quran and its interpretation extensively. He became one of the foremost authorities on Quranic exegesis of his generation, traveling widely across the Islamic world and narrating from numerous Companions. He is considered an important link in many chains of Quranic tafsir. Some scholars had reservations about his reliability in certain transmitted reports, but the majority of hadith scholars accepted his narrations, and he is found extensively in the collections of Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi, and Ibn Majah.
bio_en: Jundub ibn Abdullah al-Bajali (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who is known for his firm stance against sorcery and prohibited practices. He is reported to have witnessed or carried out the execution of a sorcerer in early Islamic history, an act that became a point of detailed scholarly discussion on the legal ruling regarding magic practitioners in Islamic law. His narrations include hadiths on the prohibition of sorcery (sihr), the importance of sincerity in acts of worship, and the proper conduct of the Muslim. A significant narration attributed to him in Sahih al-Bukhari deals with the principle that deeds are judged by their intentions and warned against the loss of sincerity. He participated in several battles and settled in Basra. He is counted among the reliable Companion narrators and his reports are preserved in the major hadith collections. The scholarly discussion around his act against the sorcerer influenced classical rulings in the Hanbali and other legal schools. He passed away in the early Umayyad period.
