bio_short_id: Ikrimah adalah maula (budak yang dimerdekakan) Abdullah bin Abbas dan perawi hadith terpercaya dari generasi tabiin. Ia dikenal atas penguasaan ilmu tafsir.

bio_short_en: Jundub ibn Abdullah al-Bajali (or al-Azdi, may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad who participated in several battles. He is known for narrating hadiths related to the prohibition of sorcery and the obligation to act upon one's faith. He is also reported to have executed a sorcerer, which became a point of discussion among jurists regarding the legal ruling on magic practitioners. His reports appear in the major hadith collections. He passed away in the early Umayyad period.
bio_en: Amra bint Abd al-Rahman al-Ansariyyah was one of the most distinguished female scholars of the Tabi'in generation and the foremost transmitter of the hadith corpus of Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). She was raised in the household of Aisha, serving and learning from her for years, and absorbed an extraordinary volume of prophetic knowledge from that unique proximity. The Caliph Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz — himself known for his deep commitment to the Sunnah — designated her as the primary authority on Aisha's narrations, instructing scholars to write down her knowledge before it was lost. Imam al-Zuhri is reported to have acknowledged that she was a vessel of knowledge and that he came to her and found in her a sea of learning. She narrated hadiths on the Prophet's prayer, fasting, purification, and the conduct of the Prophet's household, and was particularly valued for hadiths pertaining to women's religious practice. Her reports appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and all the major Sunan works. She passed away in Medina around 98–106 AH.
bio_short_id: Jundub bin Abdullah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan hadith tentang iman, ibadah, dan peringatan tentang sihir. Ia hidup lama hingga era tabiin.

bio_short_en: Amra bint Abdur-Rahman was a leading female scholar of the Tabi'in generation, known for her exceptional mastery of hadith. She was a student of Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) and transmitted a large body of prophetic traditions, particularly regarding matters of family life, worship, and women's affairs. The great imam Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz considered her one of the foremost authorities on Aisha's narrations. She is among the most trusted female hadith narrators in Islamic scholarship. She is counted among the indispensable scholars of her generation for the preservation of Aisha's prophetic legacy.
bio_en: This entry refers to hadiths transmitted jointly or in parallel chains by two Companions: Abu Huraira and Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with them both). Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with him) was the most prolific narrator of hadith among the Companions, having memorized over five thousand traditions through his devoted companionship with the Prophet ﷺ after embracing Islam in 7 AH. He sacrificed material comfort for knowledge, remaining in the mosque to learn rather than working for sustenance. Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion from the Juhaina tribe who participated in the Battle of Hudaybiyyah and narrated over eighty hadiths on worship, found property, and practical legal rulings. Their combined narration in Sahih al-Bukhari on the rules of found property (luqata) and the punishment for fornication is among the most cited joint transmissions in the hadith literature. When their names appear together in a chain, it demonstrates corroboration between independent witnesses, strengthening authenticity.
bio_short_id: Amrah binti Abdurrahman adalah tabiin perempuan yang terkemuka dan murid utama Aisyah RA. Ia dianggap sebagai otoritas utama dalam hadith-hadith dari Aisyah.

name_en: Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid Al-Juhani
bio_short_en: Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with him) was the most prolific narrator of hadith among the Companions, having memorized thousands of traditions through his close companionship with the Prophet. Zaid bin Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who participated in major campaigns and narrated numerous hadith on worship and legal matters. In certain narrations both Companions transmitted together, their combined testimony lending additional weight to the report. Their dual narration illustrates how Companions often corroborated each other's reports, strengthening the reliability of prophetic traditions.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Samurah al-Amiri (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who grew up in his presence and had the privilege of praying behind him regularly. He was the son of Samurah ibn Jundab (RA) and nephew of Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, one of the ten promised Paradise. His narrations are particularly valued for their descriptive precision about the Prophet ﷺ — his appearance, his mannerisms, the texture of his hair, his seated posture, and the lightness of his footstep. He is specifically known for reporting that the Prophet's sermon on Fridays was delivered while standing, with a concise style that was neither excessively long nor too brief, and that the Prophet ﷺ read Quranic verses during it. He also narrated the hadith about twelve caliphs from Quraysh, which has been the subject of extensive scholarly commentary. He settled in Kufa and was regarded as a trusted elder Companion there. He passed away around 74 AH. His narrations appear in Sahih Muslim and the major Sunan collections.
name_ar: أبو هريرة وزيد بن خالد الجهني
bio_short_id: Abu Huraira and Zaid bin Khalid Al-Juhani adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Jabir bin Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who grew up in his presence and narrated numerous hadith. He was among those who prayed behind the Prophet regularly and transmitted descriptions of the Prophet's appearance, prayer, and conduct. His narrations are preserved across the major hadith collections and he is considered a reliable witness to prophetic practice. He lived in Kufa and remained an important source of prophetic traditions for later scholars. His account of the Prophet's Friday sermon conducted while sitting at times is one of his most often-cited narrations.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Zayd al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina, best known for his role in the establishment of the adhan (call to prayer). He narrated that he saw the words of the adhan in a dream, came to the Prophet ﷺ and relayed it to him, and the Prophet ﷺ recognized it as a true vision and instructed Bilal ibn Rabah to learn it and call with it. This narration is preserved in the Sunan of Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and others, and forms the primary textual basis for the wording and establishment of the adhan in Islamic jurisprudence. He participated in major battles including Badr, Uhud, and al-Khandaq. He is counted among the reliable Ansari Companions and his narration on the adhan remains one of the most practically consequential hadith in the entire literature, given that the adhan is called five times daily by hundreds of millions of Muslims worldwide. He passed away in Medina in 32 AH.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin Samura adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Zaid (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet, best known for his narration describing the adhan (call to prayer). He reported that he saw the wording of the adhan in a dream, which he relayed to the Prophet, who confirmed it as divinely inspired. He participated in the major early battles of Islam and is counted among the Ansar of Medina. His most famous narration on the adhan is transmitted in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the Juhaina tribe who participated in the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and pledged allegiance under the tree. He settled in Medina and later in Kufa and narrated approximately eighty-one hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ. He is particularly well known for the jointly narrated hadith (with Abu Huraira) on the rules of found property (luqata) and the prescribed punishments for fornication — a narration of foundational importance in Islamic criminal and property law. He also narrated the famous hadith on how the Prophet ﷺ led the morning prayer after a night of rain, then turned to the congregation and asked who among them knew what their Lord had said that night — referring to Allah's statement that some had given thanks and some had committed kufr through attributing the rain to the stars rather than to Allah. His narrations are included in all six major hadith collections. He passed away around 68 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Zaid adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Zaid bin Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who settled in Medina and later in Kufa. He was present at the treaty of Hudaybiyyah and participated in important events of early Islamic history. He narrated many hadith on matters of prayer, purification, charity, and legal rulings, and is particularly known for his narration on the rules regarding found property. His narrations are included in all six major hadith collections, and he is regarded as a trustworthy Companion narrator.
bio_en: Thabit ibn Aslam al-Bunani was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator from Basra. He was a devoted student of Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him), spending extended time in his company and transmitting a large volume of his narrations. His closeness to Anas made him one of the most important conduits through which Anas's knowledge was preserved and passed to subsequent generations. Thabit was deeply pious and known for his intense attachment to worship — he reportedly spent much of his night in prayer and often wept in remembrance of Allah. Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal praised him as one of the most reliable narrators of Anas's hadiths, and both al-Bukhari and Muslim relied on him extensively. He also narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla and other senior scholars. His narrations cover prayer, the Prophet's character, supplications, and the manners of Islamic conduct. He was considered thiqa (trustworthy) by all major critics of hadith. He passed away in Basra around 127 AH. His reports are found throughout the six major collections.
bio_short_id: Zaid bin Khalid adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Thabit al-Bunani was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator from Basra. He was a devoted student of Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) and transmitted many traditions from him, making him one of the principal preservers of Anas's hadith. Thabit was known for his intense piety, frequent weeping in prayer, and deep attachment to worship. He narrated a large body of hadith covering prayer, the Prophet's character, and Islamic manners. His reports are found extensively in the Sahih collections and other major works of hadith.
bio_en: Salim ibn Abdullah ibn Umar al-Adawi was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina and one of the seven renowned jurists of the city whose collective legal opinions shaped the foundations of classical Islamic jurisprudence. He was the son of the Companion Abdullah ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him), one of the most scrupulous and prolific Companion narrators. Growing up in his father's household, Salim absorbed an enormous body of prophetic narrations and the detailed Sunnah-based practice of his father, making him one of the most reliable transmitters of Ibn Umar's hadith corpus. Imam Malik ibn Anas, who relied on Salim extensively in his Muwatta, considered the chain of Malik from Nafi from Ibn Umar as among the most authentic in the science of hadith — and Salim represents another primary route for Ibn Umar's narrations. Salim was known for his strict adherence to the Sunnah, his refusal to compromise his religious convictions, and his deep knowledge of Islamic law. He passed away around 106 AH. His narrations appear prominently in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, the Muwatta, and all major Sunan.
bio_short_id: Tsabit al-Bunani adalah tabiin terkemuka dan murid Anas bin Malik. Ia dikenal atas tangisan dan khusyuknya dalam ibadah.

bio_short_en: Salim bin Abdullah bin Umar was a leading scholar of the Tabi'in generation and one of the seven famous jurists of Medina. The son of the Companion Abdullah bin Umar (may Allah be pleased with him), he transmitted a vast number of hadith from his father and is regarded as one of the most accurate narrators of Ibn Umar's traditions. He was known for his scrupulous adherence to the Sunnah and his father's practice. His narrations are central to the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Muadh ibn Jabal al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Khazraj tribe of Medina who embraced Islam at the Second Pledge of Aqabah while still a teenager. He was singled out by the Prophet ﷺ as the most learned of the Companions in what is halal and haram — knowledge of religious permissibility — a distinction that placed him in the highest rank of Companion scholars. The Prophet ﷺ sent him to Yemen as a teacher and judge, demonstrating extraordinary confidence in his legal judgment and character. He is known for a foundational hadith on the sources of Islamic law: when asked how he would judge matters, he replied he would judge by the Quran, then the Sunnah, then by his own considered reasoning — a hadith cited in discussions of ijtihad and legal methodology. He narrated approximately 157 hadiths and died during the plague of Amwas in Syria around 18 AH at a young age, mourned as one of the greatest scholars the Muslim community had known. This is a variant listing of the Companion also recorded as Muadh ibn Jabal.
bio_short_id: Salim bin 'Abdullah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Muadh bin Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) was among the most learned of the Companions in the knowledge of halal and haram. The Prophet described him as the most knowledgeable of his community in jurisprudence and sent him as a teacher and judge to Yemen. He was a young Ansari Companion who embraced Islam early and accompanied the Prophet in all major campaigns. His narrations on prayer, knowledge, and the obligatory duties of Muslims are preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah al-Thaqafi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet ﷺ renowned for his political intelligence, diplomatic skill, and astute judgment. He embraced Islam around 5–6 AH, before the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, and participated in major campaigns including the Conquest of Mecca and the Battle of Hunayn. He later served as governor of Basra under Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab and of Kufa under Uthman and Muawiya. He narrated approximately 136 hadiths on topics including the wiping over footwear in ablution, the funeral prayer, and various aspects of the Prophet's worship. He was known for using his diplomatic intelligence to open negotiations before battle during the Islamic conquests and was sent on several important missions. His narrations include detailed accounts of the Prophet's prayer practice and personal conduct that became reference points in legal discussions. He passed away in Kufa around 50 AH. This entry refers to the same Companion listed elsewhere as Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba.
bio_short_id: Muadz bin Jabal adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal sebagai ahli halal dan haram. Nabi mengutusnya ke Yaman sebagai guru dan hakim.

bio_short_en: Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet, known for his intelligence and diplomatic acumen. He accepted Islam early and participated in the major campaigns of the Prophet's era. He later served as a governor under the caliphs and narrated numerous hadith on matters of purification, prayer, and the Prophet's conduct. His narrations are found across all the major hadith collections, and he is counted among the most politically astute of the Companions.
bio_en: Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr al-Siddiq was a leading Tabi'i scholar of Medina and one of the seven great jurists of the city. He was the grandson of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with him), and after his father Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr died, he was raised under the guardianship of his aunt Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). This gave him unparalleled access to Aisha's knowledge of the Prophet ﷺ, and he became one of her most important transmitters. Scholars considered him among the most accurate narrators of Aisha's hadith corpus. He was known for his deep knowledge, his humility, and his strict adherence to the Sunnah. Imam Malik narrated from him extensively, and his narrations are found throughout the Muwatta and in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He was one of the foundational figures in Medinan jurisprudence and his scholarly opinions carried great weight among his contemporaries and those who came after. He passed away around 107–108 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Al-Mughira adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Qasim bin Muhammad bin Abi Bakr was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the seven great jurists of Medina. He was the grandson of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with him) and a devoted student of Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), from whom he transmitted a large number of hadith. He was renowned for his knowledge, piety, and mastery of jurisprudence. His narrations are relied upon in the major hadith collections as a bridge to Aisha's traditions.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Ka'b was a Tabi'i narrator and scholar who appears in the chains of transmission of several hadiths in the major collections. He transmitted from Companions including Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) and is mentioned in chains relating to Islamic conduct and daily practice. As a member of the Tabi'in generation, he occupied the crucial role of preserving and relaying prophetic knowledge from the Companion generation to later scholars. Scholars of rijal (narrator biography and criticism) have generally treated him as an acceptable narrator in the chains where he appears. His narrations touch on topics of daily Islamic life, ethics, and the Prophet's general guidance. Without additional biographical context linking him to a specific well-known figure of the same name, he represents one of the many reliable but less-documented Tabi'i transmitters whose collective contribution was essential to the preservation of the Sunnah. His reports appear in the Sahih collections.
bio_short_id: Al-Qasim bin Muhammad adalah tabiin terkemuka, cucu Abu Bakr ash-Shiddiq, dan salah satu dari tujuh fuqaha Madinah. Ia adalah guru Imam Malik.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Ka'b was a Tabi'i narrator and scholar who transmitted hadith from the Companions, particularly on matters of daily life and worship. He is mentioned in the chains of transmission of several hadith in the major collections. As a member of the Tabi'in generation, he helped preserve and relay prophetic traditions to subsequent scholars, contributing to the continuity of the Sunnah through reliable transmission. His narrations are cited by later scholars in the context of Islamic legal discussions. Scholars of hadith criticism have generally treated him as acceptable in the chains where he appears.
bio_en: Alqama ibn Qays al-Nakha'i was one of the most senior and eminent Tabi'i jurists and hadith scholars of Kufa. He was among the closest and most devoted students of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him), having attached himself to Ibn Mas'ud and absorbed his legal methodology, Quranic understanding, and prophetic narrations deeply. Scholars of Kufa treated Alqama's narrations from Ibn Mas'ud as authoritative, and his students — including Ibrahim al-Nakha'i and others — built their legal scholarship on his foundations. He participated in the Hajj with Ibn Mas'ud and memorized his practice of the rites in detail. He was known for his scrupulous honesty in narration and his strict personal piety. His legal positions were central to the Kufan tradition that eventually developed into the Hanafi madhhab. He narrated hadiths on prayer, pilgrimage, the Quran, and Islamic ethics. He passed away around 61–62 AH in Kufa. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and all the major Sunan works.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Ka'b adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 8 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Alqamah bin Qays al-Nakha'i was a leading Tabi'i jurist and hadith scholar of Kufa. He was among the closest students of Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) and transmitted a great many traditions from him. Alqamah was known for his exceptional understanding of the Quran and the Sunnah, and Ibrahim al-Nakha'i and other major scholars considered him a primary authority. His narrations are found in the Sahih collections and other major works. His students carried his scholarship forward, making him an enduring influence on Kufan jurisprudence and hadith scholarship.
bio_en: Sa'id in hadith chains most likely refers to Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib al-Makhzumi, the foremost jurist and hadith scholar of the Tabi'in generation in Medina and often described as the greatest of the seven jurists of Medina. His father and grandfather were both Companions of the Prophet ﷺ. He studied under numerous Companions, most prominently Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with him), whose daughter he married, and Umar ibn al-Khattab. He was renowned for his unmatched mastery of the Sunnah, his fierce independence, his refusal to accept gifts from rulers, and his willingness to suffer punishment rather than compromise on religious principles — he was flogged for refusing to give the oath of allegiance to Hisham ibn Abd al-Malik's son while the previous caliph was still alive. He narrated thousands of hadiths and his legal opinions were authoritative across the Muslim world. He passed away in Medina around 93–94 AH. His narrations appear throughout all six major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Alqamah bin Qais adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah dan murid utama Abdullah bin Masud. Ia adalah salah satu fuqaha besar Kufah.

bio_short_en: Sa'id, as referenced in hadith chains, most likely refers to Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, the foremost jurist and hadith scholar of the Tabi'in generation in Medina. He studied under numerous Companions, particularly Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with him) and Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him), and was renowned for his unmatched mastery of the Sunnah and Islamic law. He was known for his independence and refusal to compromise his principles even before rulers. His legal opinions were authoritative across the Muslim world, and his narrations appear throughout the six major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abu al-Minhal was a Tabi'i narrator identified in several sources as Abu al-Minhal Sayyar ibn Salamah al-Riyahi, a Basran scholar who narrated from his father and from Abu Barza al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him), a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections, particularly Sahih al-Bukhari, in chapters on commercial transactions, prayer, and Islamic daily practice. He is regarded as a reliable narrator by the scholars of rijal criticism. His most cited narration involves a question he asked his father and Abu Barza together about currency exchange (sarf) and the prohibition of riba in such dealings, which became a reference point in Islamic commercial law. His position as a reliable Tabi'i transmitter from the Basran school placed him within the network of scholars who preserved the Sunnah in Iraq. He is considered trustworthy and his narrations in the collections are accepted by the scholarly tradition.
bio_short_id: Sa'id adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu al-Minhal was a Tabi'i narrator who transmitted hadith from Companions and senior Tabi'in scholars. He is identified in some sources as Abu al-Minhal Sayyar ibn Salamah, a Basran narrator who transmitted from his father and from other Companions. He is mentioned in the chains of narration in several hadith collections and is regarded as a reliable transmitter. His narrations touch on matters of commerce, prayer, and daily Islamic practice. He is a valuable link in the transmission of hadith from the Companion generation to the scholars who came after them.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud al-Hudhali (may Allah be pleased with him), often referenced simply as Abdullah in hadith chains, was one of the earliest and most revered Companions of the Prophet ﷺ. He was given singular honors by the Prophet ﷺ including permission to enter his private quarters without announcement and the praise: 'Hold fast to the Sunnah of Ibn Umm Abd.' He was among the first to recite the Quran openly in Mecca, accepting the beatings of the Quraysh with patience. His knowledge of the Quran was unparalleled — he claimed to have received seventy surahs directly from the Prophet ﷺ — and he maintained his own mushaf with certain differences from the Uthmanic standard, a fact that scholars have discussed extensively. He narrated approximately 848 hadiths and his legal positions became the bedrock of the Kufan tradition. Umar ibn al-Khattab sent him to Kufa as a teacher and treasurer, and he remained a central scholarly authority there until the end of his life. He passed away in Medina around 32–33 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu Al-Minhal adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah ibn Masud (RA) was one of the earliest and most revered companions of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). He was known for his deep knowledge of the Quran and his meticulous care in narrating hadith, refusing to narrate anything he was not fully certain about. The Prophet singled him out as a model to be followed in religious matters. He became the leading teacher in Kufa during the caliphate of Umar and left behind a rich legacy of narrations and students who became foundational scholars of Islamic jurisprudence.
bio_en: Abu Jamra al-Duba'i, whose full name was Nasr ibn Imran al-Duba'i, was a Tabi'i hadith narrator from Basra who spent extended time in close proximity to Abdullah ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him). He narrated a large body of traditions from Ibn Abbas covering Quranic exegesis, Islamic jurisprudence, and prophetic biography. He is counted among the reliable transmitters of Ibn Abbas's knowledge and his narrations appear in the major hadith collections including Sahih al-Bukhari. A notable narration attributed to him involves Ibn Abbas's account of the delegation from the Abd al-Qays tribe visiting the Prophet ﷺ, in which the Prophet ﷺ praised them for their faith and urged them to uphold four religious obligations — a hadith frequently cited in discussions of the pillars of Islamic practice. Abu Jamra's proximity to Ibn Abbas allowed him to transmit both hadiths and detailed Quranic commentary, making him an important bridge for the preservation of Ibn Abbas's scholarly legacy. He is regarded as thiqa (trustworthy) by the scholars of rijal criticism.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah (bin Mas'ud) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Jamra al-Duba'i was a Tabi'i hadith narrator from Basra who transmitted extensively from Abdullah bin Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him). He is counted among the reliable narrators of the Tabi'in generation and his reports from Ibn Abbas appear in the major hadith collections. He was known for his close association with Ibn Abbas, from whom he transmitted both hadith and Quranic exegesis. His narrations cover a wide range of topics including prayer, commerce, and prophetic manners. He is particularly valued for his closeness to Ibn Abbas, whose vast Quranic knowledge he helped transmit to the next generation.
bio_en: Ubayy ibn Ka'b al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), also written as Ubai ibn Ka'b, was the foremost Companion in Quranic recitation and one of the most distinguished narrators of prophetic knowledge. The Prophet ﷺ singled him out as the best reciter of the Quran among the Companions: 'The best reader of my ummah is Ubayy ibn Ka'b.' He was also one of the official scribes of the Quranic revelation and helped preserve the text during the Prophet's lifetime. The Prophet ﷺ recited the entire Quran to Ubayy and told him that Allah had named him specifically in a divine address — an extraordinary honor. He served as the imam of Tarawih prayer in Medina during the era of Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him), unifying the congregation under a single imam. His narrations on the virtues of specific Surahs, the proper recitation of the Quran, and Islamic worship are preserved throughout the major collections. He passed away around 19–32 AH. This entry is a variant romanization of Ubayy ibn Ka'b.
bio_short_id: Abu Jamra adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ubai bin Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the foremost Companions in knowledge of the Quran and hadith. The Prophet described him as the best reciter of the Quran among the Companions. He was a senior Ansari Companion and one of the few scribes who wrote down the Quranic revelation during the Prophet's lifetime. His narrations on the Quran, prayer, and the virtues of Surahs are preserved in the major hadith collections. He is universally respected across all schools of Islamic thought as a towering authority on the Quran and its recitation.
bio_en: Ayyub al-Sakhtiyani Abu Bakr ibn Kaysan al-Basri was one of the greatest Tabi'i hadith scholars and one of the most rigorous transmitters of the Sunnah in the second generation of Islam. He was born around 66 AH and spent his scholarly career in Basra, narrating from Ibn Sirin, Ata ibn Abi Rabah, Amr ibn Dinar, al-Hasan al-Basri, Nafi mawla Ibn Umar, and many other leading scholars of his era. He was profoundly committed to the Sunnah and was among the firmest opponents of innovation in religious matters. Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal praised him in the highest terms as a scholar and a man of principle. He was known to weep when he heard the adhan out of awe of Allah. His narrations are found extensively throughout Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and all the major Sunan collections, and the scholars of hadith criticism unanimously rated him as thiqa thiqa (doubly trustworthy). He passed away in 131 AH. The entry appears in the collections under the rendering Aiyub.
bio_short_id: Ubay bin Ka'ab adalah salah satu Sahabat ahli Al-Quran dan sekretaris wahyu Nabi ﷺ. Ia dikenal sebagai "Sayyid al-Qurra'" di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Ayyub al-Sakhtiyani was a leading Tabi'i hadith scholar and jurist of Basra. He was among the foremost scholars of his generation, known for his precise transmission of hadith, his deep piety, and his steadfast adherence to the Sunnah. He narrated from senior Tabi'in including Ibn Sirin and Ata ibn Abi Rabah, as well as from Companions. Imam Ahmad, Yahya ibn Ma'in, and others described him as one of the most reliable narrators of his era. His narrations appear throughout the six major hadith collections.
bio_en: Al-A'mash, whose full name was Sulayman ibn Mihran al-Asadi al-Kahili, was one of the most prolific and reliable Tabi'i hadith scholars of Kufa and a central figure in the transmission of the Kufan hadith tradition. He was born around 60–61 AH and narrated from Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Shaqiq ibn Salama (Abu Wail), Anas ibn Malik, and many other Companions and senior Tabi'in. His cognomen al-A'mash (the bleary-eyed) referred to a weakness in his eyesight. Despite this, his memory was extraordinary and he is among the most-cited narrators in both Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He was known for his simple and ascetic lifestyle — reportedly wearing patched garments and living modestly despite being the leading hadith scholar of Kufa. He was also known for a dry wit and an independent spirit. He narrated hadiths on all aspects of Islamic practice including prayer, fasting, the virtues of the Companions, and legal rulings. He passed away in Kufa around 147–148 AH.
bio_short_id: Ayyub as-Sikhtiyani adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Basra yang dikenal atas kezuhudan dan kekuatan hafalannya dalam hadith.

bio_short_en: Al-A'mash, Sulayman ibn Mihran, was one of the most prolific and reliable Tabi'i hadith scholars of Kufa. He narrated from numerous Companions and senior Tabi'in and is among the most frequently cited narrators in the Sahih collections. Despite his wide scholarship, he was known for his simple and ascetic lifestyle. His memory and precision in hadith transmission were praised by the leading scholars of his time, making him an indispensable figure in the chains of hadith narration. He is one of the most cited narrators in the six major hadith collections and his precision set the standard for Kufan hadith scholarship.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a senior Companion from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and one of the most prolific narrators among the Companions, with approximately 1,540 transmitted hadiths. He was present at the Second Pledge of Aqabah, participated in nineteen military expeditions, and witnessed many of the foundational events of early Islamic history. He was among the most accessible Companions for Tabi'in scholars in Medina, and major scholars including Ata ibn Abi Rabah made regular visits to transmit from him. His detailed narration of the Farewell Pilgrimage is the most comprehensive preserved description of the rites of Hajj in the hadith literature. He lived to an advanced age, reportedly losing his sight near the end, and was considered the last surviving Companion in Medina, passing away around 73–78 AH. This entry is a variant listing specifying his Ansari identity and refers to the same Companion as Jabir ibn Abdullah.
bio_short_id: Al-A'masy (Sulaiman bin Mihran) adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah yang dikenal atas hafalannya yang luar biasa. Ia meriwayatkan hadith dari banyak tabiin senior.

bio_short_en: Jabir bin Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a prominent Companion of the Prophet and one of the most prolific narrators of hadith among the Ansar. He accompanied the Prophet on numerous expeditions and narrated over one thousand five hundred traditions on worship, ethics, and law. He lived to a very old age and continued to teach hadith in Medina, making him a key transmitter for the Tabi'in generation. His narrations are found extensively in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid al-Nakha'i al-Kufi was a leading Tabi'i jurist and hadith scholar of Kufa and the uncle of the great scholar Ibrahim al-Nakha'i. He was among the most devoted students of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) and accompanied him on at least one pilgrimage, memorizing his practice of Hajj in detail — a narration that became a primary reference for the rites of Hajj according to the Kufan tradition. He transmitted many traditions from Ibn Mas'ud and from other senior Tabi'in scholars of Kufa. His narrations are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections, particularly on matters of prayer and pilgrimage. He was considered thiqa (trustworthy) by the hadith critics and was recognized as one of the reliable authorities of the Kufan school. He represented the generation immediately following the Companions who preserved their practical knowledge and transmitted it faithfully. He passed away in Kufa around 83 AH.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin 'Abdullah Al-Ansari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdur-Rahman bin Yazid al-Nakha'i was a leading Tabi'i jurist and hadith scholar of Kufa, and the uncle of the great scholar Ibrahim al-Nakha'i. He was among the close students of Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) and transmitted many traditions from him. He accompanied Ibn Mas'ud on the pilgrimage and memorized his practice in detail. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections, particularly on matters of prayer, pilgrimage, and legal rulings. He was a model of Kufan scholarship in the generation immediately following the Companions.
bio_en: Usama ibn Zayd ibn Haritha (may Allah be pleased with him) was deeply beloved by the Prophet ﷺ, who called him 'my beloved, and the son of my beloved.' His father Zayd ibn Haritha was the freed slave and adoptive son of the Prophet ﷺ before the prohibition of adoption as full kinship, and Usama grew up in the Prophet's household. He was appointed by the Prophet ﷺ to lead a military expedition to the northern Arabian frontier at approximately eighteen years of age — a young age that prompted some criticism from older Companions, which the Prophet ﷺ firmly rejected, saying that Usama was worthy of the command just as his father had been before him. He narrated approximately 128 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on theology, prayer, and ethical conduct. He is known for a narration on the prohibition of killing someone who has already uttered the testimony of faith even in battle. He settled in al-Jurf near Medina after the Prophet's death and passed away around 54–59 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abdur-Rahman bin Yazid adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Usama bin Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) was the son of Zayd bin Haritha and was deeply beloved by the Prophet, who called him and his father among the most beloved people to him. He narrated hadith directly from the Prophet on matters of faith, ethics, and law. He was appointed by the Prophet to lead a military expedition at a young age, reflecting the Prophet's trust in him. His narrations are preserved in the major collections and he is among the well-known Companion narrators.
bio_en: Aslam al-Adawi was a freed slave (mawla) of Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) and a reliable Tabi'i narrator who spent many years in close personal service to Umar. His proximity to the second Caliph gave him first-hand access to Umar's rulings, judgments, conversations, and daily conduct, making him an important transmitter of knowledge about the Caliph's practice and statements. He narrated from Umar as well as from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq and other Companions, and his reports serve as a primary source for understanding Umar's governance and religious methodology. His narrations are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari and the Sunan collections. Scholars of hadith criticism considered him reliable (maqbul or thiqa) within the Medinan tradition. He also narrated from his son Zayd ibn Aslam, who became a prominent hadith scholar in his own right and from whom Imam Malik transmitted extensively. His eyewitness accounts of Umar's conduct are uniquely valuable for later scholars seeking to understand the second Caliph's legacy.
bio_short_id: Usama adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Aslam was a freed slave and close companion of Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him), from whom he transmitted many hadith and accounts of the caliph's life and rulings. He is a reliable Tabi'i narrator whose reports from Umar are preserved in the major hadith collections. His close proximity to Umar made him an important transmitter of prophetic traditions and the caliph's practices. He is considered trustworthy by the scholars of hadith criticism. His eyewitness proximity to Umar made his narrations about the second caliph's conduct uniquely valuable for later scholars.
bio_en: Abu Talha al-Ansari, whose full name was Zayd ibn Sahl ibn al-Aswad al-Khazraji, was one of the most prominent Companions from the Ansar of Medina. He was among the richest of the Ansar and was known for an extraordinary act of charity when the Quranic verse 'You will not attain righteousness until you spend from what you love' was revealed: he immediately donated his most beloved garden, Bayruha, to the poor — a gesture the Prophet ﷺ praised warmly. He was one of the finest archers among the Companions and at the Battle of Uhud shielded the Prophet ﷺ with his own body, losing function in his hand to protect the Prophet's face. He participated in all the major campaigns and narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on prayer, charity, and the conduct of the Muslims. He was one of those Companions of whom the Prophet ﷺ said the sound of their footsteps could be heard in Paradise. He passed away around 31–34 AH. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari and the Sunan.
bio_short_id: Aslam adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 7 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Talha al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the leading Ansari Companions of the Prophet and one of the strongest archers among the Muslims. He narrated hadith from the Prophet on matters of worship, charity, and daily conduct. He was a devoted Companion who participated in all the major expeditions and was known for his generosity and courage. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections and he is counted among the noble Companions. The Prophet prayed over him and praised his dedication, making him one of the honored Companions of Medina.
bio_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab al-Adawi (may Allah be pleased with him) was the second Caliph of Islam and one of the most influential and transformative figures in Islamic history. He embraced Islam in Mecca after initially being a fierce opponent of the Muslims, and his conversion was considered a turning point for the early community — the Prophet ﷺ reportedly made du'a for either Umar or Abu Jahl to embrace Islam, and it was Umar. He served as the closest advisor to Abu Bakr al-Siddiq during his caliphate and then ruled for ten years (13–23 AH) during which the Islamic state expanded dramatically into Persia, the Levant, and Egypt. He narrated approximately 537 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ and his juristic opinions are cited as foundational references across all schools of Islamic law. He was assassinated in 23 AH. This entry is a variant listing of Umar ibn al-Khattab, with the appended (RA) indicating the standard invocation of divine pleasure.
bio_short_id: Abu Thalhah al-Anshari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal atas keberaniannya dalam jihad dan kedermawanannya. Ia menyumbangkan kebunnya yang paling dicintai di jalan Allah.

bio_short_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) was the second Caliph of Islam and one of the most influential Companions of the Prophet. He embraced Islam and his conversion greatly strengthened the early Muslim community. He narrated numerous hadith from the Prophet on all aspects of Islamic life, and his own judgments and practice became foundational references in Islamic jurisprudence. His narrations are among the most relied upon in all the major hadith collections. He is considered one of the two greatest Companions after Abu Bakr and a central pillar of Islamic history.
bio_en: Imran ibn Husayn al-Khuza'i (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ known for his exceptional piety and for what companions described as his spiritual experiences — including reportedly hearing the greetings of angels. He embraced Islam before the Khaybar expedition and participated in subsequent campaigns. He narrated approximately 180 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on prayer, purification, legal rulings, and the Prophet's general guidance. He settled in Basra where he became one of the most respected religious authorities, teaching the Muslim community there for many years. He is reported to have suffered from a severe illness for a long period of his life yet remained patient and grateful, a quality the Companions and Tabi'in admired. His narrations are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections. This entry, with the appended (RA), is a variant listing of the Companion also recorded as Imran ibn Husain. He passed away in Basra around 52–53 AH.
bio_short_id: Umar bin al-Khattab adalah Khalifah kedua dalam Islam dan salah satu Sahabat paling berpengaruh. Ia meriwayatkan ratusan hadith.

bio_short_en: Imran bin Husain (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who embraced Islam and became one of his devoted followers. He narrated numerous hadith on prayer, purity, and the Prophet's conduct. He was known for his piety and for reportedly experiencing spiritual visions. He served as a teacher in Basra in his later years and is counted among the reliable Companion narrators whose reports are preserved in the major hadith collections. He remained a revered source of hadith in Basra and his narrations are widely cited across the classical hadith literature.
bio_en: Ubayy ibn Ka'b ibn Qays al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was the pre-eminent Quranic reciter among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ. The Prophet ﷺ told him that Allah had commanded the Quran be recited to Ubayy by name — an honor granted to no other Companion in the same way. He was one of the principal scribes of the Quranic revelation during the Prophet's lifetime and an acknowledged authority on Quranic text, recitation, and interpretation. He served as the imam of Tarawih prayer for the congregation in Medina during the caliphate of Umar ibn al-Khattab, and his approach to the communal Tarawih helped establish this prayer as a regular Ramadan practice. He narrated hadiths on the Quran, prayer, the description of the Day of Judgment, and many other topics. He was also known as master of the Companions in Quranic matters. He passed away around 19–32 AH. This is a variant listing of the Companion also recorded as Ubayy ibn Ka'b.
bio_short_id: Imran bin Husain adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan dikenal atas kedudukannya yang tinggi.

bio_short_en: Ubayy bin Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) was among the most distinguished Companions in Quranic recitation and hadith narration. The Prophet praised him as the master reciter of the Quran and he was one of the official scribes of the revelation. He was a senior Ansari Companion who transmitted many traditions on the Quran, prayer, and Islamic conduct. His narrations appear throughout the major hadith collections and he is recognized as one of the foremost authorities among the Companions.
bio_en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr al-Asadi al-Walibi was a leading Tabi'i scholar, jurist, and hadith narrator of Kufa. He was a devoted student of Abdullah ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) and transmitted a vast corpus of tafsir narrations and hadith from him. He was renowned across the Islamic world for his deep knowledge of Quranic exegesis, Islamic jurisprudence, and hadith. He also studied under Abdullah ibn Umar and other Companions. He was known for his extraordinary courage and principled refusal to compromise his convictions before rulers — a quality that ultimately led to his martyrdom. He was captured by the orders of the tyrant Hajjaj ibn Yusuf, who executed him in 95 AH for refusing to endorse what Sa'id considered unjust and impermissible. His death was mourned deeply by the scholarly community, and Sufyan al-Thawri reportedly said that Sa'id ibn Jubayr died while all the people of the earth were in need of his knowledge. His narrations appear in all six major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Ubay bin Ka'ab adalah salah satu Sahabat ahli Al-Quran dan sekretaris wahyu Nabi ﷺ. Ia dikenal sebagai "Sayyid al-Qurra'" di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Sa'eed bin Jubair was a leading Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator from Kufa. He studied extensively under Abdullah bin Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) and is regarded as one of the most knowledgeable and pious scholars of his generation. He was executed by the Umayyad governor al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf for refusing to compromise his principles, becoming a symbol of scholarly integrity. His narrations are found in all the major hadith collections and are highly regarded by scholars. He is remembered as one of the most courageous scholars of the Tabi'in for his refusal to submit to unjust authority.
bio_en: Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, whose full name was Khalid ibn Zayd al-Khazraji, was a senior Companion from the Ansar of Medina of distinguished nobility. He was the host of the Prophet ﷺ when the Prophet ﷺ arrived in Medina after the Hijra — his house was the first the Prophet stayed at while awaiting the building of his mosque and quarters, an honor Abu Ayyub was deeply proud of. He participated in the Battle of Badr and all subsequent major campaigns. Even in his advanced years, he continued participating in military expeditions out of love for jihad, and he died during the Arab siege of Constantinople around 52 AH at a very advanced age. His dying wish was to be buried as close to the walls of Constantinople as possible. He narrated approximately 155 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ covering prayer, supplication, fasting, and the Prophet's conduct. His tomb in Istanbul remains a visited site to this day. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and all the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Sa'id bin Jubair adalah tabiin terkemuka dan murid Ibn Abbas. Ia dikenal sebagai ahli tafsir dan fikih, dan syahid dibunuh oleh al-Hajjaj bin Yusuf.

bio_short_en: Abu Ayyub al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a noble Companion of the Prophet who hosted the Prophet in his home upon the Prophet's arrival in Medina, an honor he treasured greatly. He participated in the major battles and campaigns, from Badr onward, and narrated numerous hadith on prayer, worship, and the Prophet's conduct. He continued to join military expeditions even in old age and was martyred near Constantinople, where his grave became a site of visitation. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ uniquely entrusted with knowledge of hypocrites and future trials (fitan). His full name was Hudhayfah ibn Husayl ibn Jabir al-Absiy. The Prophet ﷺ shared with him the names of the hypocrites in Medina — knowledge he kept secret — and disclosed to him details of coming tribulations that were not shared with other Companions. This made him a singular source for narrations on civil strife, the signs of the Hour, and the characteristics of hypocrites. He served as a trusted governor of al-Mada'in under Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab and was consulted by Uthman ibn Affan on the standardization of the Quranic mushaf. He narrated approximately 261 hadiths and was known for his frankness and his refusal to flatter rulers. He passed away in al-Mada'in in 36 AH, just months after Uthman's assassination. This entry is a variant spelling of the Companion also listed as Hudhaifa ibn al-Yaman.
bio_short_id: Abu Ayyub al-Anshari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang rumahnya menjadi tempat tinggal pertama Nabi ﷺ di Madinah.

bio_short_en: Hudhaifah ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the closest and most trusted Companions of the Prophet, entrusted with the secrets of the hypocrites. The Prophet confided in him information about trials and tribulations to come, making him the Companion most knowledgeable about future fitna. He narrated numerous important hadith on faith, leadership, and eschatology. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections and are particularly cited in discussions of Islamic governance and the end times.
bio_en: Abu Burda ibn Abi Musa al-Ashari was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and jurist, the son of the distinguished Companion Abu Musa al-Ashari (may Allah be pleased with him). He inherited his father's deep knowledge of the Sunnah and narrated a substantial body of hadiths from him, making him one of the most important transmitters of Abu Musa's prophetic knowledge. He also narrated from other Companions including Ali ibn Abi Talib and Abu Huraira. He served as a judge in Kufa, where he was respected for his knowledge, integrity, and sound legal judgment. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections on topics including prayer, good character, the reading of the Quran, and Islamic ethics. He was considered a highly reliable Tabi'i narrator by the scholars of hadith criticism. He passed away around 103–104 AH in Kufa. This entry is a variant of the narrator also listed as Abu Burda.
bio_short_id: Hudzaifah bin al-Yaman adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dipercaya menyimpan rahasia tentang orang-orang munafiq. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang fitnah.

bio_short_en: Abu Burdah bin Abi Musa al-Ash'ari was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and son of the Companion Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him). He narrated extensively from his father and other Companions, and served as a judge in Kufa. He was known for his knowledge, reliability, and sound judgment. His narrations appear in all the major hadith collections and he is considered one of the trustworthy bridges between the Companions and the Tabi'in scholars. He played a vital role in transmitting his father's knowledge of the Prophet to the scholars of the Tabi'in generation.
bio_en: Abu Qatada al-Ansari, whose full name was al-Harith ibn Rib'i al-Khazraji, was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina renowned for his bravery and skill in battle. The Prophet ﷺ referred to him as 'one of the best horsemen of the Ansar.' He participated in many military expeditions and is known for several specific acts of personal courage. He narrated approximately 170 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on topics including prayer, purification, fasting, hunting, and the Prophet's conduct. Among his best-known narrations is the account of the Prophet ﷺ praying while carrying his granddaughter Umama bint Abi al-As — placing her down during prostration and picking her up when he rose — a narration frequently cited in discussions of what does or does not interrupt the prayer. He also narrated hadiths on the beginning times of the five daily prayers. He settled in Damascus after the Prophet's death and later in Kufa. He passed away around 54 AH. This is a variant spelling of the Companion also listed as Abu Qatada.
bio_short_id: Abu Burdah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Qatadah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet renowned for his bravery in battle. He was among the horsemen of the Prophet and was commended for his valor in multiple expeditions, including Uhud and Hunayn. He narrated numerous hadith on prayer, fasting, hunting, and the Prophet's conduct in worship. His narrations on voluntary fasting, the greeting of the masjid prayer, and manners in daily life are well-known and preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abu Rafi' (may Allah be pleased with him), also written as AbuRafi', was a freed slave of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and one of his close personal attendants. His birth name is given variously as Aslam or Ibrahim in classical sources. He was originally a slave of al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib, who gave him to the Prophet ﷺ as a gift, and the Prophet ﷺ freed him. He served in the Prophet's household and was present at many of the intimate moments of the Prophet's daily life. He is known for transmitting narrations about the Prophet's household conduct, his marital practices, and specific etiquette observed in the prophetic household. He is sometimes confused with other Abu Rafi' figures in the tradition, but the narrations attributed to the Prophet's freed slave are generally considered reliable. His reports are found in the Sunan collections. This entry is a variant spelling of the narrator also listed as Abu Rafi'.
bio_short_id: Abu Qatadah al-Anshari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal sebagai "Penunggang Kuda Rasulullah." Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith tentang shalat, puasa, dan jihad.

bio_short_en: Abu Rafi' (may Allah be pleased with him) was a freed slave of the Prophet who served him closely for many years. He was among the early converts to Islam and witnessed many aspects of the Prophet's private life, worship, and household conduct. He narrated traditions about the Prophet's prayer, fasting, and daily habits, making his reports particularly valuable for understanding the Prophet's personal practice. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections and he is considered a reliable transmitter among the Companions.
bio_en: Ammar ibn Yasir al-Makhzumi (may Allah be pleased with him) was among the very earliest converts to Islam and one of the most severely persecuted Companions in Mecca. He was the son of Yasir and Sumayya, who became the first martyrs of Islam when they were killed by their Meccan masters for refusing to renounce their faith. Ammar himself was tortured to the point where he uttered words of apparent apostasy under extreme duress, after which the Prophet ﷺ revealed that Allah had exempted those coerced with no genuine change in their hearts. He emigrated to Medina, participated in the Battle of Badr and all subsequent major campaigns, and was known for his intense devotion to the Prophet ﷺ and his courageous honesty. The Prophet ﷺ said of him: 'Ammar is filled with faith from his head to his feet.' He narrated approximately sixty-two hadiths and was killed at the Battle of Siffin around 37 AH. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Abu Rafi' adalah maula (budak yang dimerdekakan) Nabi Muhammad ﷺ yang setia. Ia meriwayatkan beberapa hadith.

bio_short_en: Ammar ibn Yasir (may Allah be pleased with him) was among the earliest converts to Islam and among those who suffered the most severe persecution for their faith. He and his parents were tortured by the Quraysh; his mother Sumayyah was the first martyr in Islam. The Prophet declared that Ammar would be killed by the transgressing party, which was fulfilled at the Battle of Siffin. He narrated hadith on faith, prayer, and Islamic conduct, and his narrations are found in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Jabir ibn Samurah al-Amiri (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who regularly attended the Prophet's prayer and council gatherings and directly witnessed his conduct and sermons. He was the son of Samurah ibn Jundab and nephew of Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas. He narrated approximately one hundred and forty hadiths, known particularly for his vivid descriptions of the Prophet's physical appearance: his hair — that it was neither curly nor completely straight — his build, and the way he walked. He narrated the important hadith about the Prophet's Friday sermon style and delivered the well-known report about twelve rulers (caliphs) from Quraysh, which has been extensively commented upon by scholars. He settled in Kufa and was regarded as a reliable Companion authority. He passed away around 74 AH. This is a variant spelling of the Companion also listed as Jabir bin Samurah, and both entries refer to the same person.
bio_short_id: Ammar bin Yasir adalah Sahabat Nabi dari keluarga pertama yang menjadi syahid dalam Islam. Ibunya Sumayyah adalah syahidah pertama. Ia dikenal atas keteguhan imannya.

bio_short_en: Jabir ibn Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who lived in close proximity to him and observed his worship and conduct. He narrated many hadith on prayer, the Friday sermon, and descriptions of the Prophet's appearance. He grew up around the Prophet from a young age and his narrations carry the freshness of direct observation. His reports are preserved in the Sahih collections and other major hadith works. He was one of the early Muslims of Kufa whose testimony bridged the Companion era with the growing scholarly community there.
bio_en: Zayd ibn Thabit al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Khazraj tribe of Medina who served as one of the most trusted scribes of the Quranic revelation. He learned the Quran under the Prophet ﷺ himself, committed it to memory, and wrote it down as it was revealed. After the Battle of Yamama in 12 AH, when many Quran memorizers were killed, Caliph Abu Bakr al-Siddiq commissioned Zayd to compile the revealed verses into a single written volume — a task Zayd carried out with great care, accepting only verses verified by two witnesses. Later, during the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan, he led the committee that produced the standardized Uthmanic mushaf distributed across the Islamic world. He was also a scribe of the Prophet ﷺ for official correspondence. He narrated approximately ninety-two hadiths and was regarded as the highest authority in Medina on Quranic matters, inheritance law, and Islamic jurisprudence. He passed away around 45 AH. This is a variant spelling of the Companion also listed as Zaid bin Thabit.
bio_short_id: Jabir bin Samurah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith, termasuk hadith-hadith tentang sifat fisik Nabi ﷺ dan tentang para khalifah.

bio_short_en: Zayd ibn Thabit (may Allah be pleased with him) was the principal scribe of the Quranic revelation and one of the most learned Companions in matters of the Quran and inheritance law. The Prophet entrusted him with recording the revelations and he later oversaw the compilation of the Quran under Abu Bakr and Uthman (may Allah be pleased with them). He narrated numerous hadith on Quranic recitation, prayer, and Islamic jurisprudence. His narrations are preserved across all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Ka'b ibn Malik al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ from the Khazraj tribe of Medina and one of the three Companions who remained behind from the Expedition of Tabuk without an excuse. Allah revealed their forgiveness after fifty days of social boycott and isolation — an account narrated in extraordinary detail by Ka'b in the hadith collections and described as one of the longest and most complete personal narratives in all of hadith literature. Ka'b was also a gifted poet who composed verse in defense of the Prophet ﷺ and the Muslim community, a role the Prophet actively encouraged among poets of faith. He participated in the Second Pledge of Aqabah and all major battles. He narrated approximately eighty hadiths on worship, ethics, and prophetic biography. He lost his eyesight near the end of his life and passed away around 50–53 AH. This is a variant spelling of the Companion also listed as Ka'b bin Malik.
bio_short_id: Zaid bin Tsabit adalah sekretaris wahyu Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan ahli ilmu waris. Ia memimpin tim penyusunan mushaf Al-Quran.

bio_short_en: Ka'b ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the three Companions who stayed behind from the Expedition of Tabuk, whose repentance and its acceptance by Allah are recorded in the Quran. He was a poet of Islam and narrated many hadith on the Prophet's campaigns, conduct, and rulings. His famous account of his own repentance is one of the most detailed personal narratives in the hadith literature. His narrations are preserved in the major collections. He is also remembered for narrating numerous traditions about the Prophet's battles, manners, and rulings.
bio_en: This entry refers to an unnamed narrator appearing in a hadith chain simply as 'a man' (rajulun). In the science of hadith, when an intermediary in a chain is unnamed, the hadith is classified as having an element of uncertainty (jahala) regarding that narrator. Classical scholars of hadith methodology — including Imam al-Nawawi and Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani — developed detailed protocols for evaluating such narrations: if the unnamed person is a Companion, the hadith is still considered sound, since all Companions are considered reliable ('adil); but if the unnamed person is a Tabi'i or later, the narration has a weakness. In some cases, parallel chains or corroborating evidence strengthen the underlying report. The presence of unnamed narrators in certain chains does not invalidate the content of the hadith but does affect its grading in the technical hadith sciences. This entry acknowledges the reality of such chains in the preserved hadith literature.
bio_short_id: Ka'ab bin Malik adalah Sahabat Anshar yang terkenal dengan kisahnya yang tidak ikut dalam Perang Tabuk. Ia adalah seorang penyair ulung.

bio_short_en: This entry refers to an unnamed narrator appearing in a hadith chain as 'a man,' whose specific identity is not recorded in the source. Such anonymous narrators, known in hadith sciences as majhul (unknown), appear in some chains of transmission and are generally treated with caution by hadith scholars. In many cases the identity of an unnamed transmitter can be determined by examining parallel chains from other collections. The legal weight of such a narration depends on the strength of the surrounding chain and corroborating transmissions from identified narrators.
bio_en: Ata ibn Abi Rabah al-Makki was one of the foremost Tabi'i scholars and jurists and the leading religious authority of Mecca during his lifetime. He was a freed slave of Qurayshi lineage, born in Yemen around 27 AH, and studied under the greatest Companions and Tabi'in scholars of his time including Abdullah ibn Abbas, Abdullah ibn Umar, Abdullah ibn Amr, Jabir ibn Abdullah, and Aisha (may Allah be pleased with them). He became the primary mufti of Mecca for decades and was so esteemed that the Caliph Sulayman ibn Abd al-Malik said to Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri: 'Let us visit the master of the people,' referring to Ata. He narrated a vast number of hadiths and was considered thiqa thiqa (doubly trustworthy) by the hadith critics. He remained in Mecca throughout his life, guiding pilgrims and scholars who came to learn from him. He passed away in Mecca around 114–115 AH. His narrations are found extensively in all six major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: A man adalah perawi hadith yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ata ibn Abi Rabah was a leading Successor (Tabi'i) who lived from approximately 27–114 AH and was the foremost jurist of Makkah in his era. He was an Abyssinian freed slave who became one of the most respected scholars of his time. He narrated from numerous Companions including Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, and Aisha (RA). Imam al-Shafi'i said about him: 'Ata was from those whose word was trusted in matters of religion.' He served as the chief Mufti of Makkah and is extensively cited in the Sunan collections on matters of Hajj, prayer, and fiqh.
bio_en: Abu Bakr al-Siddiq Abdullah ibn Abi Quhafa al-Taymi (may Allah be pleased with him) was the closest Companion of the Prophet ﷺ, the first adult male to embrace Islam, and the first Caliph of Islam after the Prophet's death. He accompanied the Prophet ﷺ during the Hijra and hid with him in the Cave of Thawr. He served as the Prophet's companion, advisor, and trusted deputy throughout the prophetic mission. His daughter Aisha became the Prophet's wife. After the Prophet's passing, he led the Muslim community for two years and three months (11–13 AH), during which he suppressed the apostasy movements, completed the conquest of the Arabian Peninsula, and initiated the compilation of the Quran. He narrated relatively few hadiths — approximately 142 — due to his extreme caution about attributing anything to the Prophet ﷺ without certainty. He passed away in 13 AH and was buried next to the Prophet ﷺ in the Prophet's Mosque in Medina. He is considered the greatest of the Companions by Ahl us-Sunnah.
bio_short_id: 'Ata adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with him) was the closest Companion of the Prophet and the first Caliph of Islam. He was the first adult male to accept Islam and accompanied the Prophet in the migration to Medina. He narrated hadith from the Prophet on all aspects of faith and practice, and his own rulings and practices became foundational references in Islamic jurisprudence. His narrations are among the most trusted in the major hadith collections. His caliphate set the foundational precedents for Islamic governance that subsequent caliphs and scholars relied upon.
bio_en: Thabit ibn al-Dahhak al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who gave his pledge of allegiance under the tree at Hudaybiyyah, placing him among those mentioned with Allah's pleasure in Surah al-Fath. He narrated a number of hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on topics including the prohibition of false oaths, the rules regarding vows made in anger, and various aspects of Islamic ethics and conduct. He is known for a narration preserved in the Sahih collections regarding the prohibition of making a vow by other than Allah and on the consequences of false praise. He settled in Medina after the Prophet's death and narrated to the Tabi'in scholars who visited him. He is considered a reliable Companion narrator and his reports are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections. He passed away in Medina in the early years after the Prophet's passing.
bio_short_id: Abu Bakr ash-Shiddiq adalah Khalifah pertama dalam Islam dan Sahabat paling utama. Ia adalah salah satu orang pertama yang masuk Islam dan selalu setia mendampingi Nabi ﷺ.

bio_short_en: Thabit bin ad-Dahhak (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who was among those who pledged the oath of allegiance at the tree in Hudaybiyyah, one of the most honored gatherings in Islamic history. He narrated hadith from the Prophet on matters of vows, oaths, speech, and ethical conduct. He lived through the early Islamic period and transmitted prophetic traditions to the Tabi'in generation. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections, including Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim.
bio_en: Abu Humaid al-Sa'idi, whose full name was Abd al-Rahman ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Sa'ida branch of the Khazraj tribe of Medina. He is particularly renowned for a detailed hadith in which he described, before a group of ten Companions who confirmed his accuracy, the precise method of the Prophet's prayer — including how the Prophet ﷺ placed his feet, where he put his hands, how he performed the ruku and sujud, and the way he sat in the tashahhud. This narration, found in the Sunan of Abu Dawud and al-Tirmidhi, is one of the most comprehensive descriptions of the prayer's physical form in the entire hadith literature, and all four legal schools reference it when detailing the recommended acts of prayer. He also narrated hadiths on various aspects of Islamic worship and the Prophet's conduct. His narrations are preserved in the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Thabit bin Ad-Dahhak adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Humaid as-Sa'idi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet from the Ansar of Medina. He is particularly known for his detailed narration describing the proper way to perform the prayer, including the positions and movements the Prophet used. This hadith is considered one of the most important descriptions of the prophetic prayer and is widely cited by scholars of all legal schools. His other narrations on worship and conduct are also preserved in the major collections.
bio_en: Ubaydullah ibn Abdullah ibn Utba ibn Mas'ud al-Hudhali was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the seven great jurists of Medina. He was the nephew of the famous Companion Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him), whose legal tradition and scholarly approach he continued. He narrated from Aisha, Ibn Abbas, Abu Huraira, Zayd ibn Thabit, and many other Companions, making him one of the most broadly connected Tabi'i scholars to the Companion generation. He was a master of Quranic exegesis, hadith, and Islamic jurisprudence and was considered one of the most learned scholars of Medina in his time. His students included the great Imam al-Zuhri, to whom he transmitted a large body of knowledge. He also taught Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, who deeply respected him. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the major Sunan collections. He passed away around 94–98 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Abu Humaid As-Sa'idi adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ubaidullah bin Abdullah bin Utbah ibn Mas'ud was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the seven great jurists of Medina. He was a student of major Companions, including Aisha and Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them), and transmitted many traditions from them. He was also a poet and a trusted narrator whose reports appear in the major hadith collections. He is counted among the most reliable scholars bridging the Companions and later Islamic scholarship. His narrations form an important part of the Medinan transmission of prophetic knowledge across multiple fields of Islamic learning.
bio_en: This entry refers to a hadith narrated jointly by two figures: al-Miswar ibn Makhrama (may Allah be pleased with him), a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ, and Marwan ibn al-Hakam, an Umayyad official. Al-Miswar ibn Makhrama was the nephew of Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf and was born two years before the Hijra; he witnessed many events of the prophetic era and narrated important hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ including details of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. Marwan ibn al-Hakam was a prominent Umayyad figure who served as governor of Medina. Their joint transmission of the narration about the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah is one of the most detailed accounts of that treaty in the hadith literature, preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari. The combination of the two narrators — one a direct Companion witness, the other providing additional context — produced one of the richest accounts of that pivotal historical event.
bio_short_id: 'Ubaidullah bin 'Abdullah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Miswar bin Makhrama (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who was born in Mecca and later lived in Medina. Marwan bin al-Hakam was an Umayyad official who also narrated hadith. These two figures appear together in certain narrations concerning the treaty of Hudaybiyyah and related events. Al-Miswar's reports from the Prophet and from Abu Bakr and Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) are preserved in the major hadith collections. Their joint narrations concerning the treaty of Hudaybiyyah are among the most detailed accounts of that pivotal event in Islamic history.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Hisham al-Qurashi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who was brought to the Prophet ﷺ as a young child by his mother, who asked the Prophet to take the oath of allegiance from him. The Prophet ﷺ passed his hand over Abdullah's head in blessing. He is notable as one of the Companions who narrated firsthand accounts of the Prophet's conduct in the market and during travel. He is known for a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari in which he described accompanying the Prophet and Umar ibn al-Khattab while holding their hands, and the Prophet ﷺ teaching Umar the proper form of love for Allah and His Messenger above all else. He also narrated accounts of the Prophet's guidance related to daily supplications and conduct. He settled in Medina and transmitted his narrations to the Tabi'in. His reports are found in Sahih al-Bukhari and are considered reliable Companion transmissions.
bio_short_id: Al-Miswar bin Makhrama and Marwan adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Hisham (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who accompanied him as a child. His mother brought him to the Prophet, who wiped over his head and supplicated for him. He narrated hadith and is counted among the Companions who transmitted prophetic traditions to the next generation. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections, and he is among the younger Companions who witnessed and reported the Prophet's conduct. He is considered a Companion of the Prophet even though he was young during the Prophet's lifetime.
bio_en: This entry refers to a narration jointly attributed to Marwan ibn al-Hakam and al-Miswar ibn Makhrama (may Allah be pleased with him). Al-Miswar ibn Makhrama was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who directly witnessed the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and other significant events of the prophetic period, narrating detailed accounts of them. Marwan ibn al-Hakam was an Umayyad political figure and governor of Medina who, while not a Companion in the strict sense, provided additional context to certain narrations. Their combined account of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah — preserved in the major hadith collections especially Sahih al-Bukhari — is one of the most comprehensive reconstructions of that event. This entry is the reverse ordering of the combined narrators also listed as al-Miswar bin Makhrama and Marwan, and refers to the same joint transmission. The detailed account they preserved remains the primary historical source for the terms and circumstances of the Hudaybiyyah treaty.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Hisham adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Marwan bin al-Hakam was an Umayyad official and narrator of hadith. Al-Miswar bin Makhrama (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet known for his piety and transmission of hadith. The two appear together in several narrations about the treaty of Hudaybiyyah and related events. Al-Miswar's narrations carry the weight of a Companion's witness, while Marwan's reports are evaluated on the basis of his personal reliability as assessed by hadith critics. Together their combined accounts provide some of the most detailed surviving records of the Hudaybiyyah treaty negotiations.
bio_en: Al-Qasim in hadith chains most likely refers to al-Qasim ibn Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr al-Siddiq, one of the seven great jurists of Medina and a leading Tabi'i scholar. He was raised in the household of his aunt Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) after his father died, giving him direct and intimate access to her vast prophetic knowledge. He became one of the most important transmitters of Aisha's hadith corpus and is regarded as one of the most accurate in preserving her narrations. Imam Malik narrated from him extensively in the Muwatta, and his chains are considered among the most reliable in the Medinan tradition.
