He was known for his profound piety, his humility, and his refusal to accept positions that would compromise his integrity. His legal opinions were highly authoritative. He passed away around 107–108 AH in Qudayd, near Medina, and is buried there. His narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, the Muwatta, and all the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Marwan and Al-Miswar bin Makhrama adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Qasim, most likely al-Qasim bin Muhammad bin Abi Bakr, was a leading Tabi'i scholar and one of the seven jurists of Medina. He was the grandson of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with him) and a devoted student of Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). He transmitted many hadith from her and is regarded as one of the most reliable narrators of Aisha's traditions. His narrations appear throughout the major hadith collections. He is widely regarded as one of the most trustworthy transmitters of prophetic knowledge in the generation following the Companions.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Buhaina al-Asdi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who was the stepson of the Companion Malik ibn al-Qushayb. He is particularly known for a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim describing how the Prophet ﷺ, having prayed only two units of a four-unit prayer before sitting in the final tashahhud position, stood up to complete the remaining two units and then performed two prostrations of forgetfulness (sujud al-sahw) at the end of the prayer. This narration is one of the most-cited references in Islamic jurisprudence for the rules governing the prostration of forgetfulness when a prayer element is inadvertently omitted or performed out of order. He narrated hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on prayer and other aspects of Islamic worship. His reports are found in the major hadith collections and he is considered a reliable Companion narrator.
bio_short_id: Al-Qasim bin Muhammad adalah tabiin terkemuka, cucu Abu Bakr ash-Shiddiq, dan salah satu dari tujuh fuqaha Madinah. Ia adalah guru Imam Malik.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Buhaina (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet from the tribe of Azd, allied with the Banu Abd al-Muttalib. He narrated hadith from the Prophet on prayer and worship, including the well-known narration about the Prophet performing two prostrations of forgetfulness after rising from the second rak'ah without sitting for tashahhud. This report is a primary evidence for the prostration of forgetfulness in all the major legal schools. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Shaqiq ibn Salama al-Asadi, known by his kunya Abu Wail, was a senior Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator of Kufa. Despite not meeting the Prophet ﷺ himself, he narrated from many of the most prominent Companions including Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, Abu Musa al-Ashari, and Uthman ibn Affan (may Allah be pleased with them). He lived to approximately 82 AH, a long life that allowed him to serve as a bridge between the Companion generation and many subsequent scholars. He was considered one of the most reliable and respected Tabi'i transmitters in Kufa and a major link in the isnad chains of numerous hadith. Imam al-Bukhari and Imam Muslim relied on him extensively, and his narrations appear with great frequency in the Sahih collections. He was known for his piety and his adherence to the Sunnah. This entry is a variant listing of the narrator also recorded as Abu Wail. He passed away around 82 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin Buhaina adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Shaqiq bin Salama al-Asadi, known as Abu Wa'il, was a leading Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator of Kufa. He was among the close students of Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) and transmitted many traditions from him and other Companions. He was known for his reliability, piety, and depth of knowledge. His narrations appear extensively in all the major hadith collections and he is regarded as one of the most trustworthy narrators of the Tabi'in generation. He lived to an advanced age and remained a primary source of Ibn Mas'ud's traditions for the scholars of Kufa.
bio_en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, whose full name was Sa'd ibn Malik ibn Sinan al-Ansari, was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina who participated in twelve military expeditions. He narrated over 1,170 hadiths, making him one of the most prolific Companion narrators. His narrations span a wide range of topics: detailed rulings on prayer and purification, eschatological narrations on the signs of the Last Day and events in the grave and afterlife, ethical teachings, and reports on the Prophet's physical description and character. He remained in Medina throughout his life and was a primary reference for both companions and Tabi'in scholars on religious questions. He passed away in Medina around 74 AH. This entry is a variant listing of the Companion also recorded in the database as Abu Said al-Khudri and Abu Sa'id al-Khudri. All entries refer to the same Companion.
bio_short_id: Shaqiq bin Salama adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Said al-Khudri (RA) was an Ansari companion of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) who narrated a large number of hadiths, particularly on matters of purification, prayer, and eschatology. He was among the companions who devoted themselves to transmitting prophetic knowledge after the Prophet's death and was known for his piety and commitment to accuracy. He actively discouraged writing hadiths in his early years but was nonetheless a major oral transmitter. His narrations are considered highly reliable and found extensively in the major collections.
bio_en: Al-Sha'bi, whose full name was Amir ibn Sharahil al-Sha'bi al-Hamdani, was one of the foremost Tabi'i scholars and jurists of Kufa and the Muslim world in his era. He was born around 19–20 AH and reportedly met five hundred Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, from whom he transmitted hadiths directly. His breadth of contact with the Companion generation was extraordinary. He was a master of hadith, Islamic law, history, and Arabic language, combining sharp wit with profound scholarship. The Caliph Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan held him in the highest esteem and employed him for diplomatic missions. He was known for discouraging blind deference to scholars when it meant ignoring evidence, and for his independent-minded legal opinions. He narrated from Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, Aisha, and many other Companions. His students included Imam Abu Hanifa and Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri. He passed away around 103–110 AH. His narrations are found in all six major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Abu Said al-Khudri adalah Sahabat Anshar yang meriwayatkan lebih dari 1.000 hadith, termasuk banyak hadith tentang aqidah dan hari akhir. Ia dikenal atas keberaniannya dalam menyampaikan kebenaran.

bio_short_en: Ash-Sha'bi, Amir ibn Sharahil al-Sha'bi, was one of the foremost Tabi'i scholars and hadith narrators of Kufa. He claimed to have met five hundred Companions and transmitted from many of them directly. He was renowned for his encyclopedic memory, sharp wit, and mastery of legal rulings and served as a judge and an ambassador. Scholars praised him as an unparalleled authority in both hadith and jurisprudence, and his narrations appear extensively in all the major hadith collections. He is regarded as one of the greatest Tabi'i scholars ever produced by the city of Kufa.
bio_en: Abbad ibn Tamim al-Ansari al-Mazini was a Tabi'i narrator who transmitted hadiths from his uncle Abdullah ibn Zayd al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) — the Companion best known for his narration about the vision of the adhan. Abbad served as the primary transmitter of his uncle's narrations and is the principal source through whom Abdullah ibn Zayd's accounts reached the major hadith collections. His narration from his uncle about the adhan and the method of the istisqa (prayer for rain) — specifically that the Prophet ﷺ went out to an open area, reversed his upper garment, turned it around as a symbol of hoping conditions would turn around, and prayed — is among the most foundational reports for the rulings on both the adhan and the istisqa prayer. His narrations are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections. He is regarded as a reliable Tabi'i transmitter by the scholars of hadith criticism.
bio_short_id: Ash-Shu'bi adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abbad bin Tamim was a Tabi'i narrator who transmitted hadith from his uncle Abdullah bin Zayd al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), the Companion known for the narration of the adhan. He relayed traditions about prayer and purification, and his chain through his uncle to the Prophet is found in the major hadith collections. He is regarded as a reliable narrator among the Tabi'in and his reports are preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari and other collections. His chain through his uncle to the Prophet is relied upon as sound by the major hadith scholars.
bio_en: Amr ibn Maimun al-Awdi was a senior Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator who lived through the era of the Prophet ﷺ without meeting him, then went on to meet numerous Companions and become one of the connecting links between the prophetic era and the Tabi'in scholarly tradition. He narrated from major Companions including Umar ibn al-Khattab, Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Muadh ibn Jabal, and Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with them). He was present at the funeral prayer of Umar ibn al-Khattab after his assassination and narrated a detailed account of Umar's final moments and the appointment of the shura council — a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari that represents one of the most important historical records of that transition. He settled in Kufa and was considered a highly reliable Tabi'i narrator. His reports are found in Sahih al-Bukhari and the major Sunan collections. He passed away around 74 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Abbad bin Tamim adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Amr bin Maimun al-Awdi was a senior Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator who lived through the early Islamic period and was reportedly present at the assassination of Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him). He narrated from numerous Companions and was regarded as reliable and knowledgeable. He was among the early converts who moved to Kufa and became a transmitter of prophetic traditions to later generations. His narrations appear in the major hadith collections. He is counted among the reliable bridges between the Companion generation and the growing scholarly community of Kufa.
bio_en: Hisham, as referenced in hadith chains, most often refers to Hisham ibn Urwa al-Asadi al-Madani, a prominent Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator of Medina. He was the son of the great Tabi'i jurist Urwa ibn al-Zubayr and a reliable transmitter of both his father's narrations and those of Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). He is among the most frequently cited narrators in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He narrated from his father, from Aisha directly, and from other Companions and Tabi'in. He later moved to Iraq and transmitted there, which allowed the Medinan scholarly tradition to reach the Iraqi scholars. He was considered thiqa (trustworthy) with high precision by the major critics, though some noted that certain of his later Iraqi transmissions showed slight weaknesses compared to his Medinan narrations. He passed away in Baghdad around 145–146 AH. His narrations are foundational in the chains connecting Aisha's knowledge to the major collections.
bio_short_id: 'Amr bin Maimun adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Hisham, as referenced in hadith chains, most often refers to Hisham ibn Urwah, a leading Tabi'i scholar and hadith narrator of Medina. He was the son of Urwah ibn al-Zubayr and transmitted many traditions from his father and from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). He was regarded as highly reliable and knowledgeable, and his narrations are found throughout the six major hadith collections. He is one of the most frequently cited transmitters in the chains of Aisha's hadith.
bio_en: Abd al-Aziz, as referenced in hadith chains, most often refers to Abd al-Aziz ibn Abdullah ibn Abi Salama al-Majishun, a Medinan hadith scholar of the Tabi' al-Tabi'in generation, or to Abd al-Aziz ibn Suhayb al-Basri, a Tabi'i scholar from Basra who narrated extensively from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him). The latter is one of the key transmitters of Anas's hadith and appears frequently in the chains of Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim in narrations from Anas. He was considered reliable by the major hadith critics and his precise transmission of Anas's narrations made him an important link in the Basran scholarly tradition. Without additional context identifying which Abd al-Aziz is meant in a given chain, this entry acknowledges the several scholars of this name who each contributed to the preservation of prophetic knowledge in the second and third centuries of Islam.
bio_short_id: Hisham adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abdul Aziz, as referenced in hadith chains, most often refers to Abdul Aziz ibn Suhayb al-Bunani, a reliable Tabi'i narrator from Basra who transmitted extensively from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him). He is counted among the trustworthy narrators of the Tabi'in generation and his reports appear in the major hadith collections. He preserved a significant portion of Anas's traditions on prayer, daily conduct, and descriptions of the Prophet. His role in preserving the traditions of Anas ibn Malik ensured that Basran prophetic knowledge reached the wider Muslim world.
bio_en: Hafsa bint Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with her) was a wife of the Prophet ﷺ and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She was the daughter of Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him), the second Caliph, and became the Prophet's wife after she was widowed following the Battle of Badr. She was known for her deep piety, her long fasts, and her extended standing in night prayer. She narrated approximately sixty hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on topics including prayer, fasting, and prophetic conduct. She is historically significant as the custodian of the original written compilation of the Quran produced during the caliphate of Abu Bakr: the scrolls were entrusted to her for safekeeping and later served as the basis for the Uthmanic standardization under Uthman ibn Affan. She passed away in Medina around 45 AH. Her narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: 'Abdul 'Aziz adalah tabiin yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Hafsa bint Umar (may Allah be pleased with her) was a wife of the Prophet and daughter of Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him), making her among the Mothers of the Believers. She was known for her piety, fasting, and devotion to prayer. After the Prophet's passing, the first complete written compilation of the Quran was entrusted to her. She narrated numerous hadith on the Prophet's worship, prayer, and household conduct. Her narrations are preserved in the major collections.
bio_en: Asim, as referenced in hadith chains, most likely refers to Asim ibn Kulayb al-Jarmi al-Kufi, a Tabi'i scholar from Kufa who narrated from his father Kulayb and from other Companions and Tabi'in. He is known particularly for narrations describing the physical form of the Prophet's prayer — specifically the placement of the hands during prayer and the details of the tashahhud position — making him a reference in discussions of prayer posture. Another possible reference is Asim ibn Sulayman al-Ahwal, a reliable Basran Tabi'i narrator who transmitted from Anas ibn Malik and other Companions and is cited in the Sahih collections. Without specifying the full chain, the name Asim in isolation most commonly points to one of these two reliable Tabi'i narrators, both of whom are considered thiqa by the scholars of hadith criticism and both of whose narrations contribute to the practical jurisprudence of Islamic worship.
bio_short_id: Hafshah adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan putri Khalifah Umar bin Khattab. Ia menjaga mushaf Al-Quran asli setelah penyusunan pada masa Abu Bakr.

bio_short_en: Asim, as referenced in hadith chains, most likely refers to Asim ibn Kulayb al-Jarmawi or Asim ibn Umar ibn Qatadah al-Ansari, both reliable Tabi'i narrators who transmitted from senior Companions and Tabi'in scholars. Asim ibn Kulayb transmitted from his father and from Companions in Kufa and is regarded as trustworthy by hadith critics. Their reports on worship, daily conduct, and legal matters appear in the major hadith collections, serving as important links in chains connecting the Companions to later Islamic scholarship.
bio_en: Samura ibn Jundab al-Fazari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who participated in several expeditions and later served as a governor of Basra under Muawiya ibn Abi Sufyan. He narrated approximately 128 hadiths on topics including the prayer at the time of eclipse (kusuf), the prayer for rain, the rulings on animals slaughtered without pronouncing the name of Allah, and various prophetic supplications. One of his most-cited narrations is the account of the Prophet's long and detailed dream in which the Prophet ﷺ was shown scenes of people undergoing the consequences of their earthly deeds — a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari of considerable length and religious significance. Some classical hadith scholars had technical discussions about a small number of his narrations regarding continuous chains, but he is generally accepted as a reliable Companion. He passed away around 58–60 AH. This entry is a variant romanization of the Companion also listed as Samurah ibn Jundub.
bio_short_id: 'Asim adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Samura bin Jundab (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who grew up with the Muslims after his father's death and was raised in Medina. He narrated a significant number of hadith on prayer, the remembrance of Allah, prophetic dreams, and aspects of Islamic law. He later served as a governor in Basra during the Umayyad period. His narrations are found in the major hadith collections and he is counted among the reliable Companion narrators who transmitted to the Basran scholars.
bio_en: Ash-Shaybani, as referenced in hadith chains, most likely refers to Abu Ishaq al-Shaybani, whose full name was Sulayman ibn Abi Sulayman al-Shaybani al-Kufi. He was a reliable Tabi'i narrator from Kufa who transmitted from Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa, Zubayda, and other Companions and Tabi'in. He is particularly known for narrations related to the specific practices of the Prophet ﷺ in Hajj and on the rules of warfare. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He was considered thiqa (trustworthy) by the scholars of hadith criticism and his reports are referenced in chapters on prayer, Hajj, and legal rulings in the major collections. Another Shaybani of note is Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Shaybani, a student of Imam Abu Hanifa and one of the founders of Hanafi jurisprudence, though he appears primarily in fiqh works rather than in hadith chains. The former is the more likely reference in isnad contexts.
bio_short_id: Samura bin Jundab adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Ash-Shaybani, most likely Abu Ishaq al-Shaybani (Sulayman ibn Abi Sulayman), was a reliable Tabi'i hadith narrator of Kufa who transmitted from senior Tabi'in scholars including al-Sha'bi and others. He is considered trustworthy by the scholars of hadith criticism and his narrations appear in the major collections including Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. He serves as an important link in the chains of narration connecting the Companions' traditions to later Kufan scholarship, particularly in matters of prayer and conduct. His narrations contribute to the rich corpus of hadith preserved through the Kufan scholarly tradition.
bio_en: Alqama ibn Qays al-Nakha'i was one of the most eminent Tabi'i jurists of Kufa and the devoted companion of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him). His mastery of Ibn Mas'ud's legal positions, Quranic knowledge, and narrations made him the foremost authority in Kufa on the Kufan scholarly tradition after the Companions departed. He accompanied Ibn Mas'ud on at least one Hajj pilgrimage and memorized his practice of the rites in such detail that his account became a primary reference for Hajj rulings in the Kufan school. He was also a narrator from Uthman ibn Affan, Aisha, and Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari. Ibrahim al-Nakha'i — the teacher of Imam Abu Hanifa's teacher — considered Alqama the most reliable authority after Ibn Mas'ud himself. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the major Sunan. He passed away around 61–62 AH in Kufa. This entry is a variant form of the narrator also listed as Alqamah.
bio_short_id: Ash-Shaibani adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Alqama ibn Qays al-Nakha'i was a leading Successor (Tabi'i) and major jurist of Kufa, dying approximately 62 AH. He was the most prominent student of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (RA) and transmitted extensively from him, as well as from Umar, Ali, and Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (RA). He was known for his deep knowledge of the Quran and fiqh and became the chief scholar of Kufa after Ibn Mas'ud's death. Ibrahim al-Nakha'i — who was his nephew — transmitted from him extensively, and through this chain, much of the Kufan jurisprudential tradition was preserved and passed to later generations.
bio_en: Alqamah ibn Qays al-Nakha'i al-Kufi was one of the most senior Tabi'i scholars of Kufa, deeply embedded in the school of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him). He is considered the primary conduit through which Ibn Mas'ud's legal methodology and hadith corpus were preserved and transmitted to the scholars who came after. He was Ibn Mas'ud's companion for many years, learning from him directly and transmitting his narrations with great accuracy. His students, including Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, built upon his foundation and developed what eventually became the Hanafi legal school. He was known for his asceticism, his careful narration, and his principled refusal to accept gifts from rulers. His narrations are relied upon in all the major hadith collections on topics of prayer, Hajj, ethics, and prophetic guidance. He passed away around 61–62 AH. This entry is a duplicate listing referring to the same narrator as Alqamah at index 171.
bio_short_id: Alqamah bin Qais adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah dan murid utama Abdullah bin Masud. Ia adalah salah satu fuqaha besar Kufah.

bio_short_en: Alqamah bin Qays al-Nakha'i was a leading Tabi'i jurist and hadith scholar of Kufa. He was among the foremost students of Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) and narrated many traditions from him. Alqamah was recognized for his exceptional knowledge of the Quran, his mastery of jurisprudence, and his reliable narration of hadith. He is counted among the most important Tabi'in scholars of Kufa and his narrations appear in the major hadith collections. He is regarded as one of the foremost authorities on the prophetic practice as transmitted through the school of Ibn Mas'ud.
bio_en: Abu Usayd al-Sa'idi, whose full name was Malik ibn Rabi'a al-Sa'idi al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Sa'ida branch of the Khazraj tribe of Medina. He participated in the Battle of Badr — a distinction that placed him among the most honored Companions, as those who fought at Badr were specifically praised in the Quran and by the Prophet ﷺ. He narrated a number of hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on topics including the treatment of parents after their death, charitable giving on their behalf, and various aspects of Islamic worship. He is known for a narration in which he asked the Prophet ﷺ whether there was any remaining good he could do for his deceased parents, to which the Prophet ﷺ responded with guidance on praying for them, honoring their friends, and observing ties of kinship they maintained — a foundational reference on posthumous filial piety. He passed away around 60 AH.
bio_short_id: Alqamah bin Qais adalah tabiin terkemuka dari Kufah dan murid utama Abdullah bin Masud. Ia adalah salah satu fuqaha besar Kufah.

bio_short_en: Abu Usaid al-Sa'idi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet from the Ansar of Medina and a participant in the Battle of Badr. He is reported to have been the last surviving Badri Companion in Medina, giving him exceptional authority as a transmitter. He narrated hadith from the Prophet on prayer, the description of the women of Paradise, and various aspects of prophetic conduct. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections and are valued for their direct witness to the Prophet.
bio_en: Zahdam ibn Mudrab al-Jarmi al-Basri was a Tabi'i narrator from Basra who transmitted hadiths from senior Companions including Abu Musa al-Ashari (may Allah be pleased with him). He is known for a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari in which Abu Musa al-Ashari visited a host from the Jarmi tribe and was served chicken, then witnessed the host's refusal to eat it — whereupon Abu Musa explained that he had seen the Prophet ﷺ eating chicken, and expressed surprise at the avoidance, which had no Islamic basis. This narration became a reference point in discussions of the permissibility of chicken and the broader principle that no food is forbidden unless explicitly prohibited in the Quran or Sunnah. Zahdam is considered a reliable Tabi'i narrator and his reports from Abu Musa are accepted in the major collections. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari. He is regarded as a trustworthy link in the Basran chain of transmission.
bio_short_id: Abu Usaid adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Zahdam ibn Mudrab al-Jarmi was a Tabi'i narrator from Basra who transmitted hadith from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) and other senior Companions. He is regarded as a reliable narrator by the scholars of hadith criticism, and his reports are mentioned in the chains of several narrations in the major hadith collections. His narrations include accounts of the Prophet's dietary practice and manners, and he served as an important link in preserving the traditions of Abu Musa for later scholars.
bio_en: Al-Musayyib ibn Hazn al-Makhzumi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ and the father of the great Tabi'i jurist Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, who became the foremost scholar of the Tabi'in generation. Al-Musayyib embraced Islam and was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who witnessed several of the key events of the prophetic era. He is known for a narration preserved in Sahih al-Bukhari related to the final illness and death of Abu Talib, the Prophet's uncle: the Prophet ﷺ came to visit Abu Talib on his deathbed and invited him to accept Islam, but Abu Talib did not take the testimony of faith in his final moments. This narration is among the most cited in Islamic scholarship on the topic of the fate of those who die without faith and the limits of prophetic intercession. His reports are found in the major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Zahdam adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Al-Musayyib bin Hazn was a Companion of the Prophet (may Allah be pleased with him) and the father of the great Tabi'i jurist Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib. He narrated a small number of traditions and his name appears in certain chains of hadith. His most famous mention in the sources relates to the account of the death of the Prophet's uncle Abu Talib. His son Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib became one of the greatest scholars of the Tabi'in generation. His importance in Islamic tradition is inseparable from the legacy of his son Sa'id, whose scholarship shaped Islamic jurisprudence.
bio_en: Anas ibn Malik al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), referred to here with the variant notation 'rad' (short for radiyallahu anhu — may Allah be pleased with him), was the personal servant of the Prophet ﷺ for ten years and one of the most prolific Companion narrators with over two thousand transmitted hadiths. His mother Umm Sulaym brought him as a boy to serve the Prophet ﷺ upon the Prophet's arrival in Medina, and the Prophet ﷺ made supplication for him: 'O Allah, give him wealth and children and bless him.' Anas served the Prophet ﷺ with complete devotion and retained every detail of the prophetic household's daily life in his memory. After the Prophet's passing, he settled in Basra and taught hadith for decades, becoming the last surviving major Companion in that city. His narrations provide unique insight into the Prophet's daily conduct, manners, appearance, and personal character. He passed away around 91–93 AH. This entry is a variant notation of the Companion Anas ibn Malik.
bio_short_id: Al-Musaiyab adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) was the personal servant of the Prophet for approximately ten years and one of the most prolific narrators of hadith among the Companions. He served the Prophet from the time of his arrival in Medina until the Prophet's death and witnessed countless aspects of his daily life and worship. He narrated over two thousand traditions and lived to a very old age, making him a primary source for the Tabi'in generation. His narrations are central to all major hadith collections.
bio_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with her), referred to here as A'ishah (RAA), was the daughter of the first Caliph Abu Bakr, wife of the Prophet ﷺ, and one of the greatest scholars in Islamic history. She was born in Mecca and grew up in the Prophet's household in Medina, acquiring a depth of first-hand knowledge of the Prophet's life that no one else could match. She narrated over two thousand hadiths and is particularly authoritative on matters of the Prophet's private worship, household conduct, physical description, and personal manners. After the Prophet's death she was a primary teacher of the Companions and Tabi'in, issuing fatwas on a wide range of religious questions and correcting narrations she believed were inaccurate. She is regarded by Ahl us-Sunnah as one of the greatest Islamic scholars who ever lived. She passed away in Medina in 58 AH. This entry is a variant notation of Aisha bint Abi Bakr.
bio_short_id: Anas (rad) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) was the beloved wife of the Prophet and one of the greatest scholars among the Companions. She narrated over two thousand hadith and was regarded as the foremost authority on matters of the Prophet's private life, prayer, and household conduct. She was also a distinguished jurist whose legal opinions were sought by Companions and Tabi'in alike. Her narrations are among the most important in all the major hadith collections. She continued to teach and guide the Muslim community for decades after the Prophet's passing and is a primary source for half of Islamic knowledge.
bio_en: Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As al-Sahmi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Quraysh tribe who was unique in having the Prophet's express permission to write down hadith — making him one of the earliest systematic recorders of prophetic traditions. He maintained a written collection he called al-Sahifa al-Sadiqa (the True Scroll), which he preserved and transmitted to his students. He was also uniquely knowledgeable in earlier scriptures, having studied some of the Torah and Gospel, which gave him a broad religious perspective. He narrated approximately 700 hadiths. He was known for his extraordinary devotion to worship — he fasted continually and stood in prayer most of the night — until the Prophet ﷺ gently instructed him to moderate his practice and give his body and family their due rights. He participated in many battles. He passed away around 63–65 AH. This entry is a variant listing of the Companion also recorded as Abdullah ibn Amr.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih.

bio_short_en: Abdullah bin Amr ibn al-As (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet and one of the most prolific narrators of hadith. He had the special permission from the Prophet to write down hadith and maintained a collection known as al-Sahifah al-Sadiqah. He was known for his intense devotion to worship, fasting, and Quran recitation, to the point where the Prophet advised him to take a more moderate approach. His narrations are preserved in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Umm Salamah Hind bint Abi Umayya al-Makhzumiyyah (may Allah be pleased with her) was a wife of the Prophet ﷺ and one of the Mothers of the Believers, notable for her exceptional wisdom, strong character, and religious scholarship. She was among the earliest Muslims and was among those who emigrated first to Abyssinia and then to Medina, enduring great hardship for her faith. After the death of her first husband Abu Salamah, she married the Prophet ﷺ. She was known for her sound judgment — it was she who suggested to the Prophet ﷺ during the difficulty at Hudaybiyyah that he perform his sacrifice first without waiting for the Companions, which he did, and the Companions followed. She narrated approximately 378 hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ covering prayer, fasting, women's matters, the Prophet's physical description, and prophetic supplications. She passed away in Medina around 59–62 AH. This entry is a variant listing of the Companion Umm Salamah.
bio_short_id: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) was a wife of the Prophet and among the Mothers of the Believers. She was known for her wisdom, piety, and knowledge of Islamic rulings, particularly those related to women. She narrated numerous hadith from the Prophet on prayer, dress, mourning, and many other subjects. After the Prophet's passing she continued to be consulted by Companions and Tabi'in scholars on religious matters. Her narrations are preserved in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib al-Hashimi (may Allah be pleased with him), referred to here as Ali (RA), was the cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet ﷺ, raised in the Prophet's household from a young age, and the fourth Caliph of Islam. He was among the very first to embrace Islam — some narrations suggest he was the first male child to do so. His full name was Ali ibn Abi Talib ibn Abd al-Muttalib ibn Hashim. He married the Prophet's daughter Fatima and their sons al-Hasan and al-Husayn are among the most revered figures in Islamic history. He was renowned for his courage in battle, his depth of knowledge, his eloquence, and his spiritual insight. He served as the Prophet's representative on several key occasions and was instructed to remain in Medina in place of the Prophet ﷺ during certain expeditions. He narrated approximately 536 hadiths and his legal opinions and religious judgments are foundational references across all schools of Islamic thought. He was martyred in Kufa in 40 AH.
bio_short_id: Ummu Salamah adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu Ummahatul Mukminin. Ia meriwayatkan banyak hadith.

bio_short_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him) was the cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet, and the fourth Caliph of Islam. He was among the earliest converts to Islam and was deeply knowledgeable in the Quran, Sunnah, and Islamic jurisprudence. He narrated numerous hadith from the Prophet and his own legal rulings are foundational to Islamic scholarship. He was known for his courage, knowledge, and piety. His narrations are preserved throughout the major hadith collections. He is among the most revered of all the Companions and his knowledge of Islam is considered a primary source for the entire community.
bio_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr al-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with her) was the beloved wife of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, daughter of the first Caliph, and one of the most authoritative figures in the transmission of Islamic knowledge. She lived in the Prophet's household from her marriage until his death and gained an intimate knowledge of his daily life, worship, and character that no one else fully shared. She narrated over two thousand hadiths and was a primary religious teacher and jurist in the decades following the Prophet's death. Companions including Umar ibn al-Khattab and Abu Musa al-Ashari consulted her on religious questions, and she corrected narrations she believed were inaccurate on multiple occasions. She was also a skilled poet and had broad knowledge of Arabic genealogy, history, and medicine. She passed away in Medina in 58 AH and was buried in al-Baqi cemetery. This entry is an alternate descriptive notation of the same Companion.
bio_short_id: 'Ali (RA) adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 6 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (RA) was the beloved wife of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and the daughter of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq (RA). She is one of the most prolific narrators of hadith, transmitting over 2,210 traditions, and is regarded as a foundational source for knowledge of the Prophet's private conduct and household life. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Take half of your religion from this Humaira (Aisha).' She was renowned for her intelligence, memory, and deep understanding of Islamic jurisprudence. After the Prophet's death, the Companions would refer to her when they needed clarification on religious matters. She died in 57 AH.
bio_en: Hafsa bint Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with her), also referred to as Hafsah Ummul Mu'minin, was a wife of the Prophet ﷺ and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She was the daughter of Umar ibn al-Khattab, the second Caliph, and was previously married to Khunays ibn Hudhayfah who died after participating in the Battle of Badr. She was known for her intense piety — fasting frequently and standing long in night prayer. She was one of the few Companions who was highly literate, having been taught to read and write by al-Shifa bint Abdallah at Umar's request. She is of great historical significance as the custodian of the first written compilation of the Quran assembled under Abu Bakr: these scrolls were kept with her and later used as the reference text for the Uthmanic standardization. She narrated approximately sixty hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ. She passed away in Medina around 45 AH.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih.

bio_short_en: Hafsa bint Umar (may Allah be pleased with her), Umm al-Mu'minin, was a wife of the Prophet and a Mother of the Believers. She was the daughter of the Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) and was known for her devotion to fasting and night prayer. The original written compilation of the Quran was kept in her custody after Abu Bakr's caliphate. She narrated numerous hadith on the Prophet's worship and conduct. Her narrations are preserved in the major collections.
bio_en: Umm Habiba Ramla bint Abi Sufyan (may Allah be pleased with her) was a wife of the Prophet ﷺ and one of the Mothers of the Believers. She was the daughter of Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, who was at the time the leader of Qurayshi opposition to Islam. She was among the earliest Muslims, emigrating to Abyssinia with her first husband Ubaydullah ibn Jahsh, who subsequently apostasized and died there as a Christian, leaving her a widow in a foreign land. The Prophet ﷺ arranged their marriage through the Abyssinian King Negus (al-Najashi), who acted as the Prophet's proxy in the ceremony — one of the few such remote marriages in early Islamic history. She narrated approximately sixty-five hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ on topics including prayer, fasting, and marital conduct. After the Prophet's death she remained in Medina. She passed away around 44 AH. Her narrations are found in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the major Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Hafshah adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan putri Khalifah Umar bin Khattab. Ia menjaga mushaf Al-Quran asli setelah penyusunan pada masa Abu Bakr.

bio_short_en: Umm Habibah bint Abi Sufyan (may Allah be pleased with her) was a wife of the Prophet and a Mother of the Believers. She was among the early converts who emigrated to Abyssinia to escape persecution in Mecca. She narrated hadith from the Prophet on prayer, worship, and daily conduct. Her father Abu Sufyan later accepted Islam. Her narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections and she is counted among the Companions who contributed to the preservation of the Sunnah.
bio_en: Aisha bint Abi Bakr (may Allah be pleased with her), referred to here with the fuller form 'may Allah be pleased with her,' was the wife of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and one of the greatest scholars in Islamic history. Born in Mecca and raised in the household of her father Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, the first Caliph, she grew up in close contact with the prophetic mission from its earliest days. Her marriage to the Prophet ﷺ gave her unparalleled insight into his private religious practice, his daily life, and his character. She narrated over two thousand hadiths and was a recognized authority on prayer, purification, family law, and Quranic interpretation. She was known to correct inaccuracies in narrations related to her area of knowledge, including famously disputing a narration that the dead can hear. She taught hundreds of students including Urwa ibn al-Zubayr and Amra bint Abd al-Rahman. She passed away in 58 AH. This is an alternate notational form of the same Companion.
bio_short_id: Umm Habibah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

name_en: A'ishah (May Allah be pleased with her)
bio_short_en: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) was the wife of the Prophet and one of the most accomplished scholars among the Companions. She narrated more than two thousand hadith covering all aspects of Islamic life and was regarded as the foremost authority on the Prophet's private conduct and household affairs. Her knowledge of Islamic jurisprudence, Quran, and medicine was unmatched, and scholars from across the Muslim world traveled to Medina to learn from her. Her narrations are central to all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Muadh ibn Jabal al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ from the Khazraj tribe of Medina who was singled out by the Prophet ﷺ as the most knowledgeable of the Companions in matters of what is halal and haram. He embraced Islam at the Second Pledge of Aqabah as a young man and was among the Prophet's closest scholarly students. The Prophet ﷺ sent him as a teacher and judge to Yemen, one of the most significant delegations of the prophetic era. He narrated approximately 157 hadiths on prayer, Islamic law, prophetic methodology, and the foundations of religious knowledge. His most famous narration is the dialogue on the sources of Islamic judgment — Quran, then Sunnah, then ijtihad — which became a cornerstone reference in the science of usul al-fiqh. He died during the plague of Amwas in Syria around 18 AH at a young age. This is a variant romanization of the name also listed as Muadh ibn Jabal and Muadh bin Jabal.
bio_short_id: Aisyah binti Abu Bakr adalah istri Nabi Muhammad ﷺ dan salah satu perawi hadith terbanyak di kalangan wanita. Ia dikenal atas kecerdasan dan penguasaan ilmu fikih.

bio_short_en: Mu'adh bin Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the most learned Companions in halal and haram and in the knowledge of Islamic jurisprudence. The Prophet praised him as the most knowledgeable of his community in these matters and sent him as a teacher and judge to Yemen. He was a young Ansari Companion who embraced Islam in its early days and participated in all the major campaigns. His narrations on the pillars of Islam, prayer, and legal matters are preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Ibn Abi Layla, whose full name was Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla al-Ansari, was a leading Tabi'i scholar and judge of Kufa. He was the son of Abi Layla al-Ansari, a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ, and grew up in Medina before settling in Kufa. He narrated from numerous Companions including Umar ibn al-Khattab, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, and others (may Allah be pleased with them). He served as a judge in Kufa during the Umayyad period and was respected for his legal knowledge and his commitment to applying Islamic law justly. He is known in the Kufan legal tradition for his legal opinions and for the transmission of hadith to the scholars who came after him. His narrations appear in the Sunan collections and he is considered reliable by the scholars of rijal. He participated in legal debates with other leading scholars of his time. He passed away around 82–83 AH.
bio_short_id: Muadz bin Jabal adalah Sahabat Nabi yang dikenal sebagai ahli halal dan haram. Nabi mengutusnya ke Yaman sebagai guru dan hakim.

bio_short_en: Ibn Abi Laila, Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Laila, was a prominent Tabi'i scholar and jurist of Kufa who served as a judge for many years. He transmitted hadith from numerous Companions, including Ali ibn Abi Talib, Hudhaifah, and others (may Allah be pleased with them). His father Abi Laila was also a Companion of the Prophet, giving Ibn Abi Laila direct family access to prophetic tradition. He is counted among the reliable narrators of the Tabi'in generation and his reports appear in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Malik ibn Anas al-Asbahi al-Madani was the founder of the Maliki school of jurisprudence and one of the most eminent scholars in Islamic history. Born in Medina around 93–97 AH, he lived and taught there his entire life. He studied under the greatest scholars of his generation including Nafi mawla Ibn Umar, Ibn Shihab al-Zuhri, Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Ansari, and Rabi'a al-Ra'y. He compiled the Muwatta — the first systematic written collection of hadith and Islamic legal practice — which the Caliph Harun al-Rashid reportedly considered making the law of the entire Islamic state. Imam al-Shafi'i said: 'When scholars are mentioned, Malik is the star.' He was deeply devoted to Medina and its scholarly tradition and regarded the practice of its people as an authoritative source of Islamic law alongside explicit hadith. He was physically present in Medina throughout his life and refused to leave it even when invited by caliphs. He passed away in Medina in 179 AH.
bio_short_id: Ibn Abi Laila adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Malik ibn Anas was the founder of the Maliki school of jurisprudence and one of the greatest hadith scholars of all time. He spent his entire scholarly life in Medina, narrating and teaching the Sunnah of the Prophet with meticulous care. His famous work al-Muwatta is one of the earliest and most authoritative compilations of hadith and Islamic law. Imam al-Shafi'i said that after the book of Allah, there is no book on earth more correct than al-Muwatta. His narrations are foundational across all the major collections.
bio_en: Abu Bakra Nufay ibn al-Harith al-Thaqafi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ who came to Islam during the Siege of Ta'if by descending from the fortress walls on a pulley mechanism to join the Muslims — which gave him the epithet Abu Bakra (father of the pulley). The Prophet ﷺ freed him upon his conversion. He settled in Basra and became one of the most important Companion authorities in that city, transmitting many narrations to the scholars there. He is particularly known for a narration in which the Prophet ﷺ addressed his Companions about avoiding fitna (civil strife), warning against taking up arms against fellow Muslims in political conflicts — a hadith Abu Bakra took so seriously that he refused to take sides during the civil war. He narrated approximately 132 hadiths. He passed away in Basra around 51–52 AH. This entry is a variant spelling of the Companion also listed as Abu Bakra.
bio_short_id: Imam Malik bin Anas adalah imam mazhab Maliki dan salah satu ulama hadith terbesar. Kitabnya al-Muwaththa' adalah salah satu kompilasi hadith tertua yang masih ada.

bio_short_en: Abu Bakrah (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet who embraced Islam at the conquest of Ta'if. He narrated numerous hadith on Islamic ethics, leadership, and the Day of Judgment. His narration that a people who entrust their affairs to a woman will not succeed is among the most widely discussed hadith in Islamic political thought. He settled in Basra and became one of its principal hadith transmitters. His narrations are preserved in the major collections.
bio_en: Adi ibn Hatim al-Ta'i (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ, the son of Hatim al-Ta'i, the legendary pre-Islamic Arab chieftain celebrated throughout Arabic poetry and culture as the greatest exemplar of generosity. Adi was initially a Christian Arab and a leader of his tribe before embracing Islam around 9 AH. After his conversion he became a devoted Companion and participated in the early Islamic conquests. He narrated approximately sixty-six hadiths from the Prophet ﷺ. Among his most notable narrations is the famous hadith on charity: the Prophet ﷺ urged every Muslim to give charity, even if only a date, and said: 'Protect yourselves from the Fire, even if with half a date.' He is also known for narrating the Prophet's explanation of the Quranic verse about the 'white thread' and the 'black thread' in the context of determining the start of the fasting day — the Prophet ﷺ explained that it means the whiteness of day versus the darkness of night. He lived to approximately 68 AH and settled in Kufa.
bio_short_id: Abu Bakrah adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan tinggal di Basra. Ia dikenal atas pemahamannya yang mendalam tentang hadith-hadith tentang fitnah.

bio_short_en: Adi ibn Hatim al-Ta'i (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet and son of the famous pre-Islamic Arab chieftain Hatim al-Ta'i, renowned for his generosity. Adi initially fled to Syria upon hearing of the Prophet's mission but later returned and embraced Islam, becoming a devoted Companion. He narrated hadith from the Prophet on hunting, prayer, and Islamic conduct. He participated in the early Islamic conquests and his narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Abu Ubaid al-Qasim ibn Sallam al-Azdi al-Harawi was a distinguished Islamic scholar of the third century AH who made fundamental contributions to the sciences of Arabic language, Quranic studies, hadith, and Islamic jurisprudence. Born around 157 AH in Herat (in present-day Afghanistan), he studied under Sufyan ibn Uyayna, Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan, and other leading scholars. He served as a judge in Tarsus for eighteen years under the Abbasid Caliph. Among his most important works is Gharib al-Hadith, a pioneering lexicon explaining the difficult and archaic vocabulary found in hadith, and Fada'il al-Quran on the virtues of Quranic recitation. He also wrote works on Islamic law, Quranic readings (qira'at), and Arabic linguistics. While he himself is not a Companion or Tabi'i, his contribution to the preservation and explanation of hadith knowledge was foundational, and his works remain standard references in the hadith and language sciences. He passed away in Mecca around 224 AH.
bio_short_id: 'Adi b. Hatim adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Ubaid al-Qasim ibn Sallam was a distinguished Islamic scholar of the third century of Islam, known for his encyclopedic knowledge of hadith, Quranic sciences, Arabic language, and Islamic law. He authored several important works and is regarded as a highly reliable narrator and a major authority in Islamic scholarship. His works on Quranic recitation and hadith are still studied by scholars today. He is considered one of the great scholars who contributed to the systematic preservation of Islamic sciences.
bio_en: Sa'id ibn Zayd al-Adawi al-Qurashi (may Allah be pleased with him) was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ and one of the ten Companions given the explicit glad tidings of Paradise by the Prophet ﷺ in a single statement — a distinction shared only with Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, Talha, al-Zubayr, Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf, Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah, and himself. His full name was Sa'id ibn Zayd ibn Amr ibn Nufayl. His father Zayd ibn Amr was one of the Hanifs — pre-Islamic monotheists who rejected idolatry — and came close to Islam before the revelation. Sa'id himself was among the earliest converts. He married Fatima bint al-Khattab, the sister of Umar ibn al-Khattab, and their household is noted in the account of Umar's conversion when he came to confront them and discovered they had already accepted Islam. He participated in all major campaigns and was deeply pious. He narrated approximately fifty hadiths. He passed away in Medina or near Aqiq around 50–51 AH.
bio_short_id: Abu 'Ubaid adalah perawi hadith yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Sa'id ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) was one of the ten Companions given glad tidings of Paradise by the Prophet during his lifetime, the group known as al-Ashara al-Mubashshara. He was the son of Zayd ibn Amr, who had turned away from idolatry before Islam, and the husband of Fatimah bint al-Khattab, the sister of Umar. He embraced Islam early and participated in many campaigns. His narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections. He is among the noblest Companions, and his witnessing of the Prophet's promise of Paradise to him is one of the most honored distinctions in Islamic tradition.
bio_en: Umm Atiyyah al-Ansariyyah, may Allah be pleased with her, was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from Medina. Her full name was Nusayba bint al-Harith al-Ansariyyah, and she was among the most active women companions in the service of the early Muslim community. She participated in seven military expeditions alongside the Prophet ﷺ, not as a combatant but as a nurse and caregiver, tending to the wounded, preparing food, and treating the ill. Her proximity to the Prophet's household gave her deep familiarity with his practices, particularly those relating to women. She narrated hadiths on the washing and preparation of the deceased, the funeral rites for women, the rulings on mourning periods, and restrictions on visiting graves. Her narrations on the washing of the Prophet's daughter Zaynab are among the most detailed accounts of Islamic funeral practice in the hadith literature. Scholars of all four major legal schools cite her narrations extensively in chapters on janaza (funeral rites) and women's religious obligations. Her reports appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the four Sunan collections, and she remains a primary authority in Islamic jurisprudence for matters relating to women's practice.
bio_short_id: Sa'id ibn Zayd adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Umm Atiyyah al-Ansariyyah, may Allah be pleased with her, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad who participated in several military expeditions, tending to the wounded and ill. She was known for her close relationship with the Prophet's household and narrated numerous hadiths concerning women's religious practices, including rulings on washing the deceased, funeral rites, and restrictions on mourning. Her narrations are preserved in the major hadith collections and remain a primary reference for Islamic jurisprudence related to women.
bio_en: Hakim ibn Hizam, may Allah be pleased with him, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and a nephew of the Prophet's first wife Khadijah bint Khuwaylid. He holds the unique distinction of being one of the few people reported to have been born inside the Kaaba itself. Born approximately fifteen years before the Year of the Elephant, he lived to nearly one hundred years of age, having spent roughly half his life before Islam and half within it. He embraced Islam at the conquest of Mecca in 8 AH and thereafter became a sincere and devoted companion. He was renowned throughout Arabia for his extraordinary generosity, reportedly freeing one hundred slaves during the pre-Islamic period and another hundred after accepting Islam. He narrated hadiths on commercial ethics, the rules of trade, and the importance of honesty in transactions — including the well-known hadith affirming that buyer and seller retain the right to cancel a transaction as long as they have not yet parted. His narrations are particularly significant in Islamic commercial jurisprudence and are cited in all four major legal schools. His reports appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and other major collections.
bio_short_id: Ummu Athiyyah al-Anshariyyah adalah Sahabiyah yang meriwayatkan hadith-hadith penting tentang hukum wanita, termasuk tentang memandikan jenazah.

bio_short_en: Hakim ibn Hizam, may Allah be pleased with him, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad and a nephew of Khadijah, the Prophet's first wife. Born inside the Kaaba, he embraced Islam at the conquest of Mecca and lived to nearly one hundred years of age. He was renowned for his generosity, his extensive business dealings conducted with scrupulous honesty, and his narrations on commercial ethics that are widely cited in books of fiqh.
bio_en: Amr ibn Shu'ayb was a prominent Tabi'i scholar from Medina and the grandson of the companion Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As, may Allah be pleased with him. He lived approximately from 50 AH and transmitted hadiths primarily through the chain known as 'Amr from his father Shu'ayb from his grandfather Abdullah ibn Amr' — a well-recognized isnad in the hadith sciences that has been the subject of considerable scholarly discussion. Many major imams including Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn al-Jawzi accepted this chain as reliable, while others expressed reservations about whether it constitutes a continuous chain. His grandfather Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As was one of the first companions to systematically record prophetic traditions in writing, compiling what he called al-Sahifah al-Sadiqah. Amr inherited much of his grandfather's recorded knowledge and transmitted it to later generations. His narrations cover a broad range of legal topics including commercial transactions, family law, criminal law, and acts of worship. The four major schools of jurisprudence frequently cite his narrations as evidence, and his reports appear in the Sunan of Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasai, and Ibn Majah.
bio_short_id: Hakim b. Hizam adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Amr ibn Shu'aib was a prominent Tabi'i scholar from Medina and the grandson of the companion Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As, may Allah be pleased with them. He transmitted many hadiths through the chain known as Amr ibn Shu'aib, from his father, from his grandfather — a well-known and debated isnad among hadith scholars. He was a reliable narrator whose reports on legal matters, particularly in commerce and family law, are frequently cited in the four major schools of jurisprudence.
bio_en: Amir ibn Sharahil al-Sha'bi, known simply as al-Sha'bi, was one of the most brilliant Tabi'i scholars of the early Islamic period. Born around 19 AH in Kufa, he claimed to have met five hundred companions of the Prophet ﷺ, and he narrated directly from major companions including Ali ibn Abi Talib, Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, Aisha, Jabir ibn Abdullah, and many others. He possessed an encyclopedic memory and sharp wit, and was celebrated for his knowledge of Quranic exegesis, hadith, Islamic jurisprudence, history, and pre-Islamic Arab genealogy. He was appointed as a judge and an emissary during the Umayyad period and was reportedly sent by Caliph Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan to the Byzantine emperor. He refused to give legal opinions except on what was definitively established and was known for his intellectual honesty and caution. He died around 103–104 AH. The great imams of the following generation, including Ibrahim al-Nakha'i and Abu Hanifah, drew heavily from his knowledge. His narrations appear throughout the Sahihayn and the major Sunan works, covering prayer, legal transactions, prophetic biography, and many other domains.
bio_short_id: Amr bin Syu'aib meriwayatkan hadith dari ayahnya dari kakeknya (Abdullah bin Amr), yang merupakan sanad yang banyak digunakan dalam kitab-kitab hadith.

bio_short_en: Amir ibn Sharahil al-Sha'bi was one of the greatest Tabi'i scholars of the early Islamic period, known for his encyclopedic knowledge and sharp wit. Born around 19 AH in Kufa, he met many companions of the Prophet and transmitted from them directly, making him a prolific and highly reliable link in many hadith chains. He was a leading jurist of his era, respected by scholars for his broad learning in fiqh, history, and the Quran.
bio_en: Muhammad ibn al-Munkadir was a distinguished Tabi'i scholar and one of the most trusted hadith transmitters in Medina. He was the son of the companion al-Munkadir ibn Abdullah al-Taymi, may Allah be pleased with him. Born in Medina, he narrated from numerous companions including Jabir ibn Abdullah, Aisha, Abu Hurairah, and Anas ibn Malik, may Allah be pleased with them all. He was celebrated not only for the authenticity and breadth of his narrations but also for his exceptional piety, deep weeping in worship, and rigorous asceticism. He was known to spend long nights in prayer and is reported to have said that when the fear of death becomes light in one's heart, one's life has become corrupted. Scholars of hadith praised him in the highest terms: Malik ibn Anas considered him one of the most reliable of Medinan narrators, and Yahya ibn Maʿin declared him thiqah (trustworthy). His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and all four Sunan collections, covering topics of prayer, fasting, the virtues of worship, and prophetic conduct. He died around 130 AH in Medina.
bio_short_id: Ash-Sha'bi adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Muhammad ibn al-Munkadir was a distinguished Tabi'i scholar and one of the most trusted hadith transmitters in Medina. A son of the companion al-Munkadir, he narrated from numerous companions including Jabir ibn Abdullah, Aisha, and Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with them all. He was celebrated for his piety, asceticism, and the authenticity of his narrations, which appear in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Zainab bint Abi Salama, may Allah be pleased with her, was a stepdaughter of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ through his marriage to Umm Salama bint Abi Umayya. Her father was Abu Salama ibn Abd al-Asad, a companion who died from wounds sustained at the Battle of Uhud. After her father's death, the Prophet ﷺ married her mother Umm Salama, and Zainab was raised in the Prophet's household as one of the closest people to him. This proximity gave her direct access to his teachings and the daily practices of his wives. She was a Tabi'iyyah by technical classification but had personal experience of the Prophet's household and narrated hadiths from both the Prophet ﷺ and from her mother Umm Salama. Her narrations cover women's religious practice, household conduct, mourning, and the Prophet's daily habits. She was also a teacher to the next generation of scholars. Her narrations are regarded as highly authentic given her intimate knowledge of the prophetic household, and they appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and other major collections. She was among the most knowledgeable women of the Tabi'in generation on matters pertaining to the Prophet's family.
bio_short_id: Muhammad adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Zainab bint Abi Salama, may Allah be pleased with her, was a stepdaughter of the Prophet Muhammad through his marriage to Umm Salama. Raised in the Prophet's household, she had direct access to his teachings and the practices of his wives. She narrated hadiths on matters of household conduct, mourning, and women's worship, and her narrations are regarded as highly authentic given her proximity to the Prophet's family.
bio_en: Ismail ibn Abi Khalid was a reliable and well-regarded Tabi'i hadith narrator from Kufa who lived approximately from 50 AH and died around 146 AH. He transmitted from a number of senior companions and their close followers, including al-Bara ibn Azib, Abu Juhayfah, Qays ibn Abi Hazim, and others. He was held in very high regard by the leading critics of hadith of his time: Shu'bah ibn al-Hajjaj — one of the strictest and most exacting hadith critics — said of him, 'I have not seen anyone whose memory for what he heard was more reliable than Ismail ibn Abi Khalid.' Yahya ibn Maʿin and Ahmad ibn Hanbal both classified him as thiqah (trustworthy) without reservation. He was particularly known for his transmission of narrations related to Islamic practice, the conduct of the companions, and matters of belief. He narrated from eyewitnesses to key events of the Prophet's era and was considered one of the more precise Kufan narrators. His reports appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and throughout the four Sunan collections.
bio_short_id: Zainab binti Abu Salamah adalah tabiin perempuan yang terkemuka dan anak tiri Nabi ﷺ. Ia banyak meriwayatkan hadith dari ibu kandungnya Ummu Salamah.

bio_short_en: Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid was a reliable Tabi'i hadith narrator from Kufa who transmitted from a number of senior companions and their followers. He was praised by leading hadith critics for his precision and trustworthiness, and his reports appear regularly in the Sahihayn and other major collections. He was particularly known for his transmission of narrations related to Islamic practice and belief from the scholars of Kufa.
bio_en: Abu al-Bakhtari, whose name was Said ibn Fayruz al-Ta'i, was a trustworthy Tabi'i scholar from Kufa who lived approximately until 83 AH. He was a freed slave of the Banu Asad and narrated from major companions of the Prophet ﷺ including Ali ibn Abi Talib, Ibn Abbas, Ibn Umar, Abu Hurairah, and others, may Allah be pleased with them all. He was known for his piety and his reliability as a narrator of hadith. Scholars of hadith criticism — including Yahya ibn Maʿin, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Hibban — classified him as thiqah (trustworthy). His teacher Ibn Abbas said of him that he was among those most devoted to learning. He narrated hadiths on a range of topics including acts of worship, supplications, and matters of Islamic law, and his narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and other major collections. He was killed at the Battle of al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf's siege of Mecca in 83 AH alongside his teacher Said ibn Jubayr, who was martyred for refusing to renounce his principles before the tyrant al-Hajjaj.
bio_short_id: Isma'il adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu al-Bakhtari, whose name was Sa'id ibn Fayruz al-Ta'i, was a trustworthy Tabi'i scholar from Kufa. He narrated from companions including Ali ibn Abi Talib, Ibn Abbas, and Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with them. Known for his learning and piety, his narrations appear in the major hadith collections, and he was respected by scholars of hadith criticism for the reliability of his transmission.
bio_en: This entry refers to hadiths attributed jointly or in parallel to two of the greatest companion scholars: Aisha bint Abi Bakr, may Allah be pleased with her, and Abdullah ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him. Aisha was the Prophet's wife and the most prolific female narrator of hadith, with approximately 2,210 narrations attributed to her. She was an authority on the Prophet's private life, household conduct, prayer, and virtually every domain of Islamic law. Ibn Abbas was the Prophet's cousin, born three years before the Hijra and raised near him, who became the foremost Quranic exegete among the companions — earning the titles Hibr al-Ummah (Scholar of the Nation) and Turjuman al-Quran (Interpreter of the Quran). When a narration is transmitted on the authority of both, it indicates parallel transmission of the same prophetic statement through two independent chains, which strengthens the report's authenticity. The combined scholarly authority of Aisha and Ibn Abbas represents two of the highest levels of narrator credibility in the hadith sciences, and hadiths attributed to both are among the most firmly established in the literature.
bio_short_id: Abu Al-Bakhtari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: This dual attribution refers to a hadith reported jointly on the authority of both Aisha (RA) and Ibn Abbas (RA), two of the most prolific and scholarly Companions. Aisha (RA) narrated over 2,200 hadiths as the Prophet's wife and a leading jurist, while Ibn Abbas (RA), the Prophet's cousin, narrated over 1,600 hadiths and is known as Tarjuman al-Quran. When a hadith is attributed to both narrators, it indicates parallel transmission of the same prophetic statement through two independent chains, which strengthens the authenticity and transmission of the report.
bio_en: Abu Tha'labah al-Khushani, may Allah be pleased with him, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the tribe of Khushain, a subtribe of Quda'ah. His actual name is variously recorded as Jurhum ibn Nashib, Abu Tha'labah being his kunya (teknonym). He pledged allegiance to the Prophet ﷺ and became a devoted companion, transmitting a number of hadiths on religious practice. He participated in the treaty of Hudaybiyyah and other campaigns alongside the Prophet ﷺ. He is particularly known for his narrations on the permissibility of hunting with trained dogs and for hadiths on Islamic dietary law, including the ruling on using vessels owned by non-Muslims. His direct questions to the Prophet ﷺ about hunting practices and their resulting answers represent important legal precedents in classical Islamic jurisprudence. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, the Sunan of Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasai, and Ibn Majah. He settled in Syria after the Prophet's death and continued to transmit prophetic knowledge there.
bio_short_id: Aisha and Ibn 'Abbas adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Tha'laba al-Khushani, may Allah be pleased with him, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the tribe of Khushain. He pledged allegiance to the Prophet and narrated hadiths on various religious rulings including hunting, eating, and general conduct. His narrations are found in several of the six major hadith collections, and he is considered a reliable witness to the Prophet's teachings and practice.
bio_en: Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, may Allah be pleased with him, was a distinguished companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ from the Ansar of Medina. His full name was Khalid ibn Zayd ibn Kulayb al-Khazraji, and he was from the Banu Najjar clan. He had the great honor of hosting the Prophet ﷺ in his home upon the Prophet's arrival in Medina during the Hijra, an honor that came to him when the Prophet's camel chose to kneel at his door. He hosted the Prophet for approximately seven months, providing him with lodging and food until the Prophet's mosque and private quarters were completed. He participated in virtually all of the Prophet's military campaigns and was known for his bravery. After the Prophet's death, he continued to strive in the path of Allah into old age and died during the Muslim siege of Constantinople around 52 AH, becoming one of the most renowned companions to die on foreign soil. The Ottomans later built a magnificent mosque over his grave in Istanbul. He narrated hadiths on prayer, purification, and the rights of neighbors, and his narrations appear in the major hadith collections.
bio_short_id: Abu Tha'laba Al-Khushani adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, may Allah be pleased with him, was a distinguished companion of the Prophet Muhammad from the Ansar of Medina. He had the great honor of hosting the Prophet in his home upon the Prophet's arrival in Medina during the Hijra. He participated in many military campaigns and continued striving in the path of Allah into old age, dying during the siege of Constantinople, where he was buried. His narrations on prayer, worship, and general practice are widely cited.
bio_en: Imran ibn Husayn, may Allah be pleased with him, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who embraced Islam in the seventh year after the Hijra along with his father Husayn ibn Ubayd. He was known for his exceptional piety, profound asceticism, and remarkable spiritual states — it is reported that angels used to greet him and that he heard their salutations until he underwent a medical cauterization procedure, after which the greetings ceased, only to resume later. He participated in several military campaigns and later settled in Basra, where he became one of the city's foremost religious authorities. He narrated hadiths on prayer, worship, legal rulings, and theological matters. He was known for giving legal opinions based on the Quran and Sunnah without reference to personal opinion. He is reported to have narrated 180 hadiths, covering topics from prayer and fasting to eschatology. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the four Sunan collections. He died in Basra around 52 AH after a long illness, which he bore with remarkable patience.
bio_short_id: Abu Aiyub Al-Ansari adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Imran ibn Husayn, may Allah be pleased with him, was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad who embraced Islam in the seventh year after the Hijra. He was known for his asceticism, his notable spiritual states, and the angels' greetings reported in his narrations. He settled in Basra, where he became a leading religious authority, and his hadiths on acts of worship, legal rulings, and morality appear in all the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Shaqiq ibn Salama al-Asadi, also known by his kunya Abu Wa'il, was a prominent Tabi'i scholar from Kufa who lived to approximately 82 AH. Though he did not personally see the Prophet ﷺ, he narrated hadiths from a remarkable array of major companions including Abdullah ibn Masud, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, Aisha, and others. He was particularly close to Abdullah ibn Masud and is counted among his foremost students, transmitting a large body of hadiths and legal opinions on his authority. Hadith scholars of every era have praised him without reservation: Yahya ibn Maʿin, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and al-Nasa'i all considered him thiqah (trustworthy), and he is consistently described as one of the most reliable Kufan narrators. He was known for his strict piety and his reluctance to give fatwas without clear evidence. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and throughout the major Sunan collections, covering prayer, Quranic recitation, prophetic character, and Islamic legal matters.
bio_short_id: Imran bin Husain adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan banyak hadith dan dikenal atas kedudukannya yang tinggi.

bio_short_en: Shaqiq ibn Salama al-Asadi, also known as Abu Wa'il, was a prominent Tabi'i scholar from Kufa who narrated from many companions of the Prophet including Abdullah ibn Masud, Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman, and Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with them. He was held in the highest regard by hadith scholars for his reliability and long chain of transmission. His narrations are found in the Sahihayn and throughout the major hadith literature.
bio_en: Mus'ab ibn Sa'd was a Tabi'i scholar and the son of the illustrious companion Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, may Allah be pleased with him — one of the ten companions given the glad tidings of Paradise by the Prophet ﷺ and the commander who led the Muslim armies to victory at the pivotal Battle of al-Qadisiyyah. Mus'ab was thus raised in a household of the highest prophetic distinction. He narrated hadiths from his father and from other companions, and several of his narrations from his father represent important legal and spiritual guidance. He transmitted hadiths on prayer, the virtues of companions, patience in hardship, and the Prophet's conduct. He was considered a trustworthy narrator by hadith scholars including Yahya ibn Maʿin and Ibn Hibban. His narrations appear in Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the Sunan collections. He also transmitted narrations on the special qualities of his father Sa'd, preserving details of that companion's life and practice that would not otherwise be available. He died in Medina and was regarded as a reliable link in the transmission of his father's narrations to subsequent generations.
bio_short_id: Shaqiq adalah Sahabat Nabi yang meriwayatkan 5 hadith dalam koleksi-koleksi hadith utama.

bio_short_en: Mus'ab ibn Sa'd was the son of the illustrious companion Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, may Allah be pleased with him, one of the ten companions given glad tidings of Paradise. Mus'ab narrated from his father and other companions, transmitting important hadiths on worship, prayer, and the virtues of the companions. He was a trustworthy Tabi'i narrator whose reports appear in the major hadith collections.
bio_en: Warrad was the secretary and freed slave of al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, the companion of the Prophet ﷺ, may Allah be pleased with him. He served as al-Mughirah's personal scribe and transmitted hadiths on his master's behalf, including dictating letters and messages containing prophetic narrations.
